title: Micron’s costly new Boise plant will make memory chips. What is memory, anyway?
summary: Micron Technology Inc. announced a $15 billion plant in Boise, Idaho, to manufacture memory chips, marking a return of memory-making to the city and a significant step in U.S. semiconductor reshoring. The article explains the difference between dynamic random-access memory (DRAM) for speed and flash memory (NAND) for permanent storage, both of which Micron produces and are crucial for modern devices. Micron's CEO highlights the increasing demand for memory in various sectors, including automotive, AI, and large-scale data processing.

title: Micron could become the biggest private employer in the Boise area with new expansion
summary: Micron announced a $15 billion investment to expand its southeast Boise campus, creating 2,000 new jobs in Boise and potentially making it the largest private employer in the area. This expansion includes building a new semiconductor fabrication facility and will also generate over 17,000 American jobs in total. Micron plans to boost STEM education programs, partner with the College of Western Idaho for workforce training, and open a world-class child care facility to support its growth and the community.

title: Risch: Vote against chips bill ‘painful.’ What leaders said as Micron broke ground
summary: Micron Technology Inc. broke ground on its new $15 billion memory manufacturing fab in Boise, a project expected to create 2,000 direct and 15,000 indirect jobs. Local and national leaders, including Boise Mayor Lauren McLean and U.S. Secretary of Energy Jennifer Granholm, lauded the investment. Senator Jim Risch explained that while the Idaho delegation ultimately voted against the CHIPS and Science Act, it was a "painful" decision made after the bill became bloated with unrelated spending, and they were confident the semiconductor support would pass regardless.

title: Micron breaks ground on $15 billion U.S. chip plant, says more to come soon
summary: Micron Technology is breaking ground on a new $15 billion chip plant in Boise, Idaho, and plans to announce another U.S. plant soon. These new factories will significantly increase Micron's U.S. DRAM production to 40% of its global volume, up from 10%. This investment aligns with the U.S. Chips and Science Act to boost domestic semiconductor manufacturing.

title: Exclusive: Micron CEO reveals new details about Boise plant, plans for housing workers
summary: Micron Technology Inc. plans a $15 billion investment to build a new memory manufacturing plant in Boise, creating 2,000 direct and 15,000 indirect jobs. CEO Sanjay Mehrotra and Senior Vice President April Arnzen discussed the plant's details, the highly skilled and well-paying jobs it will offer, and the company's efforts to address the housing shortage for its growing workforce. This expansion represents a return of manufacturing to Boise, supported by the CHIPS and Science Act.

title: NVIDIA Delivers Quantum Leap in Performance, Introduces New Era of Neural Rendering With GeForce RTX 40 Series
summary: NVIDIA unveiled its new GeForce RTX 40 Series GPUs, powered by the Ada Lovelace architecture and new DLSS 3 technology, promising up to 4x performance improvement for gamers and creators. The flagship RTX 4090 and two configurations of the RTX 4080 were introduced, alongside new features like neural rendering and RTX Remix for classic games. These advancements aim to redefine real-time ray tracing, AI-powered graphics, and creative workflows.

title: Cramer's Lightning Round: Keep Your Powder Dry When It Comes to Crown Castle
summary: Jim Cramer offers his quick takes on various stocks in his "Lightning Round." He advises caution with Crown Castle, suggests buying Moderna and Texas Instruments, and recommends waiting for lower prices on Trade Desk and Micron Technology. Cramer also expresses skepticism about Carnival Corp and notes the vulnerability of all semiconductor stocks.

title: Nvidia’s new AI-focused chip produces more realistic images in games
summary: Nvidia Corp. has unveiled its new AI-focused graphics chip, the RTX 4090, which is designed to produce more realistic images in games by combining AI with traditional pixel calculations. The high-end chip, costing US$1,599, features 76 billion transistors and 24GB of memory, and will go on sale October 12, with other versions following in November. This launch aims to give customers a reason to upgrade amidst a slowdown in PC component demand.

title: SanDisk Extreme PRO Portable SSD review: Attractive storage
summary: The SanDisk Extreme PRO Portable SSD is reviewed as an attractive, portable, and capable storage solution, ideal for photographers and videomakers. While it might be slightly slower than advertised, its rugged design, IP55 rating, and appealing golden-orange strip make it stand out from typical hard drives. It's recommended for professionals needing durable external storage on the go, offering good performance despite not reaching its promoted top speeds.

title: Huge Growth of Non-Volatile Memory Market Outlook 2022, Industry Growth, Top Manufactures, Key Segments 2028
summary: This report details the significant growth expected in the Non-Volatile Memory Market between 2022 and 2028. It provides an overview of market dynamics, key competitors, product types, and applications. The study also includes competitive analysis, growth drivers, restraints, and opportunities within the market.

title: Micron’s big new Boise fab will slow US economy, bring higher taxes and worse
summary: Economist Peter Crabb argues that Micron Technology's new $15 billion Boise semiconductor plant, despite being seen as an economic boon, will likely slow the U.S. economy and lead to higher taxes due to federal subsidies and government borrowing. He also suggests that Micron's shareholders will face higher costs and a lower return on investment, as indicated by the company's stock performance and low price-to-earnings ratio compared to peers. Crabb emphasizes the often-overlooked long-term economic consequences of such large-scale, government-subsidized projects.

title: Micron warns of tougher times, plans to cut investments by 30%
summary: Micron Technology announced it would cut its investments by over 30% to around $8 billion for fiscal 2023, signaling tougher times ahead due to falling demand for PCs and smartphones. The company, which was the first major chipmaker to warn of declining demand earlier in the year, expects strong revenue growth in the second half of fiscal 2023 as demand recovers early next year. Despite grim short-term profit and revenue forecasts, analysts noted that production and capital expenditure cuts often signal that memory markets are approaching their lowest point.

title: Micron Technology Reports Results for the Fourth Quarter and Full Year of Fiscal 2022
summary: Micron Technology announced its financial results for the fourth quarter and full fiscal year 2022, reporting record revenue of $30.8 billion for the full year and its sixth consecutive year of positive free cash flow. Despite a weaker near-term supply-demand environment, the company returned $2.9 billion to shareholders and plans to cut wafer fab equipment capex by nearly 50% to navigate the downcycle. Micron remains optimistic about capitalizing on long-term demand for memory and storage due to its technological leadership and strong balance sheet.

title: Japan to give Micron Tech up to $320 million to boost Hiroshima chip output
summary: Japan announced a subsidy of up to 46.5 billion yen ($320 million) for Micron Technology Inc. to boost advanced memory chip production at its Hiroshima plant. This move aligns with growing cooperation between Tokyo and Washington in chip manufacturing and comes as Micron trims capital investments elsewhere due to falling demand. The subsidy is part of Japan's broader strategy to bolster domestic chip production and foster technological collaboration.

title: Japan to give Micron Technology up to $320 mln to boost chip output in Hiroshima
summary: Japan announced it will provide Micron Technology with a subsidy of up to 46.5 billion yen ($320 million) to help build advanced memory chips at its Hiroshima plant. This financial aid highlights the increasing collaboration between the U.S. and Japan in chip manufacturing, amidst technological competition with China. The move follows similar support for Western Digital Corp and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co, as Japan seeks to bolster its domestic chip production capabilities.

title: Commonwealth Equity Services LLC Trims Holdings in Fidelity Low Volatility Factor ETF (NYSEARCA:FDLO)
summary: Commonwealth Equity Services LLC reduced its stake in Fidelity Low Volatility Factor ETF (NYSEARCA:FDLO) by 7.5% in the second quarter, selling 46,571 shares and owning 577,770 shares valued at $25,774,000. Other hedge funds, including Verity Asset Management Inc., Roosevelt Investment Group LLC, AE Wealth Management LLC, Ahrens Investment Partners LLC, and Envestnet Asset Management Inc., significantly adjusted their positions in FDLO during the same period. The article also provides key price performance metrics for FDLO, including its 52-week low and high, and moving averages.

title: TSMC remains world’s largest contract chipmaker
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC) maintained its position as the world's largest contract chipmaker in Q2, holding a 53.4% market share despite a slight dip from the previous quarter. The company recorded $18.15 billion in sales due to demand from high-performance computing, IoT, and automotive electronics. South Korea's Samsung Electronics and Taiwan's United Microelectronics Corp followed with significant market shares.

title: Syracuse University Hosts Micron Technology as Company Announces $100B Historic Investment for Computer Chip Manufacturing Complex in Central New York
summary: Syracuse University hosted the announcement of Micron Technology's $100 billion investment to build a semiconductor fabrication facility in Central New York, the largest private investment in New York state history. This megafab will create nearly 50,000 jobs and increase domestic supply of leading-edge memory, leveraging the bipartisan CHIPS and Science Act. Micron and New York state will also invest $500 million in community and workforce development, focusing on diverse hiring and economic opportunity for underserved communities.

title: 'We're disappointed' | Micron chooses Central New York over Central Texas for $100B investment
summary: Micron Technology announced a $100 billion investment in Central New York, turning down a significant incentive package from Lockhart, Texas. Local officials in Lockhart expressed disappointment over missing out on the manufacturing plant, which would have brought substantial economic development and improvements to the area, including the school district. Despite the setback, Lockhart officials remain optimistic about future economic opportunities and advocate for the renewal of Texas tax incentives to remain competitive.

title: Micron plans to build $100B N.Y. ‘Megafab’ facility, creating 9,000 jobs
summary: Micron Technology Inc. announced plans to invest up to $100 billion in a new "megafab" facility in Clay, N.Y., which will be the largest U.S.-based computer memory fabrication facility. This immense investment aims to create 9,000 direct jobs at Micron and nearly 50,000 associated jobs, producing dynamic random-access memory (DRAM). The project is supported by $5.5 billion in New York state incentives, federal grants and tax credits from the CHIPS and Science Act, and significant local infrastructure support, with site preparation beginning in 2023.

title: How is Cree LED Performing Under New Ownership?
summary: Cree LED, acquired by Smart Global Holdings (SGH) in March 2021, has seen its sales decline for four consecutive quarters, with the most recent quarter dipping below $100 million for the first time under SGH ownership. While SGH aimed for growth and diversification with the acquisition, Cree LED's financial performance represents 22% of SGH's total revenues. The article suggests it might be too early to definitively judge Cree LED's performance due to long design-in sales cycles, but indicates that an upward trend in sales is necessary by mid-fiscal year 2023 for SGH to realize a favorable return on investment.

title: Micron Technology to build chip fab in upstate New York
summary: Micron Technology Inc. announced plans to build a semiconductor plant in upstate New York, representing a long-term investment of up to US$100 billion and potentially creating 50,000 jobs. The decision was influenced by significant federal, state, and local incentives, including US$5.5 billion in state tax credits and US$280 billion from the US CHIPS and Science Act aimed at bolstering the semiconductor industry and US competitiveness. This facility is expected to be the most advanced memory chip manufacturing plant in the US, with Micron planning an initial US$20 billion investment by the end of the decade.

title: Micron’s ‘mega fab’ planned for New York won’t be bigger than Boise’s plant. Yet
summary: Micron Technology announced plans to invest $100 billion over two decades for a new "mega fab" in Clay, New York, which is projected to eventually be larger than its Boise plant. Despite the New York expansion, Boise will remain the center for Micron's technology development efforts. Both the New York and Boise facilities aim to boost U.S.-based DRAM manufacturing, with Micron anticipating long-term demand for memory products despite current market challenges.

title: Micron would change Syracuse area for decades to come. Are we up to the challenge?
summary: Micron Technology's plan to build a $100 billion semiconductor fabrication campus near Syracuse promises thousands of high-paying tech jobs and significant regional growth. However, a major challenge lies in developing a skilled workforce to fill these specialized positions, requiring a concerted effort from educational institutions. The expansion also necessitates planning for infrastructure, housing, and social services to support the anticipated population influx and economic changes in the area.

title: Strive U.S. Semiconductor ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NYSEARCA:SHOC)
summary: The Strive U.S. Semiconductor ETF (SHOC) invests in the 30 largest U.S. semiconductor companies and was launched on October 6, 2022. The ETF has current assets under management of $90.85 million and a net expense ratio of 0.40%. Its top holdings include NVIDIA, Broadcom, and Micron Technology.

title: Can Any Emerging Memory Technology Topple DRAM and NAND Flash?
summary: This article explores the challenges emerging memory technologies face in unseating DRAM and NAND Flash, prompted by Intel's exit from the Optane business. It delves into the history and current state of ferroelectric RAM (FeRAM), resistive RAM (RRAM), phase-change memory (PCM), and magnetic RAM (MRAM), discussing their breakthroughs and limitations. The author questions whether any of these contenders can achieve the "Holy Grail" of storage-class memory.

title: US to allow Samsung, SK Hynix to import to China for 1 year
summary: The US has granted Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix a one-year waiver to import semiconductor equipment into their Chinese factories, temporarily easing concerns over the impact of new US export curbs. This decision aims to mitigate potential disruptions to the US semiconductor industry and global supply chains, but South Korean chipmakers remain in a precarious position between US regulations and their significant market presence in China. The long-term implications, including technology competitiveness and potential license requirements after the grace period, are still a concern for the companies involved.

title: KLA to stop chip tool supply to customers in China: Report
summary: KLA Corp. will cease supplying certain chip tools and services to customers in China, including SK Hynix Inc., to comply with new U.S. regulations. These regulations require companies to obtain a license from the U.S. Department of Commerce to provide advanced manufacturing equipment to Chinese chipmakers, impacting companies like SK Hynix and Samsung Electronics which operate factories in China. KLA's decision affects sales and service for advanced fabs producing NAND, DRAM, and logic chips, with China representing a significant portion of KLA's revenue.

title: TSMC cuts capex on tool delays, demand woes; cautious on outlook
summary: TSMC, the world's largest contract chipmaker, has cut its 2022 capital expenditure by at least 10% and issued a cautious outlook for demand, predicting a chip downturn next year due to rising inflation and other macroeconomic factors. Despite an 80% surge in third-quarter profit, the company expects the semiconductor industry to decline in 2023, though TSMC itself anticipates a growth year. The company cited tool delivery challenges and capacity optimization as reasons for the capex reduction and stated that new U.S. export controls on advanced chip manufacturing for China would have a limited impact on its operations.

title: Back to the Future: CNY is poised to return to a familiar role as a hub of leading-edge American manufacturing
summary: Central New York (CNY) is set to reclaim its historical role as a hub of high-tech American manufacturing, spurred by Micron Technology's massive $100 billion investment in a new Megafab facility. This transformational development echoes past successes, such as Carrier Corporation's move to Syracuse in 1937 and General Electric's establishment of Electronics Park in 1944, both significantly boosting local employment and innovation through public-private partnerships. The article suggests that if history is any indication, this new investment could lead to unprecedented prosperity for the region, returning it to a prominent position in advanced manufacturing.

title: U.S. Speaker Pelosi's husband sold Nvidia, Micron options at a loss
summary: U.S. House Speaker Nancy Pelosi's husband, Paul Pelosi, sold call options in chipmakers Micron Technology and Nvidia, incurring losses close to $1 million. The transactions were disclosed in a transparency filing, which also showed he exercised call options for Alphabet shares and allowed Walt Disney call options to expire at a loss. This comes amid scrutiny over stock trading by lawmakers and their families, leading to legislative efforts to restrict such activities.

title: SEMI: Global 300mm Chip Fab Capacity Forecast to Reach New High in 2025
summary: Global 300mm chip fab capacity is projected to expand at a nearly 10% CAGR from 2022 to 2025, reaching 9.2 million wafers per month, driven by strong demand for automotive semiconductors and new government funding. China is expected to significantly increase its global share of 300mm front-end fab capacity, nearing Korea and surpassing Taiwan. Regional investments and incentives, including the U.S. CHIPS Act and European CHIPS Act, are also contributing to this growth, with power-related and analog capacities showing the strongest expansion rates.

title: Micron’s jobs: Tech giant would need engineers -- and an army of HVAC, assembly and machine workers
summary: Micron Technology Inc.'s new fabrication plant in Clay, NY, is projected to create thousands of jobs over the next two decades. While many positions will require high-level scientific and engineering training, the article emphasizes that a significant portion of the 9,000 jobs will also be filled by skilled tradespeople like HVAC, assembly, and machine workers. This highlights the diverse range of employment opportunities beyond traditional high-tech roles.

title: ASML shrugs off slowdown, U.S. China sanctions, reports strong Q3
summary: ASML Holding NV reported better-than-expected third-quarter sales and profit, along with record new bookings, despite the current economic slowdown and U.S. sanctions on China. The company's CEO, Peter Wennink, noted strong long-term demand from customers and a limited impact from U.S. regulations on China because ASML is a European company with few U.S. parts in its non-EUV machines. ASML's backlog has reached 38 billion euros, and it is expanding production capacity to meet demand.

title: Micron moves into vacant former Maxtor campus in Longmont
summary: Micron Technology Inc. has leased a significant portion of the formerly vacant Maxtor Corp. campus in Longmont, Colorado. The computer data storage company will occupy 207,000 square feet of the 475,000-square-foot facility, with the owner Broe Real Estate Group touting the repositioning of the asset for life sciences, advanced manufacturing, and flex office tenants.

title: Town hall in Clay to discuss Micron project: jobs, housing, infrastructure
summary: The first of a series of community discussions about Micron Technology's $100 billion computer chip plant in Clay, NY, will be held on Monday. The town hall aims to engage residents on the project's impact on jobs, housing, and infrastructure in Central New York, which is projected to create 50,000 jobs over 20 years. County Executive Ryan McMahon emphasized the importance of community input to ensure the positive impact of Micron's investment is felt throughout the region.

title: Three years, hundreds of meetings, billions of incentives offered and no deal: How Lockhart lost Micron and what's next
summary: Lockhart, a small, rural area near Austin, Texas, spent three years, held hundreds of meetings, and offered billions in incentives in an attempt to lure a multibillion-dollar Micron chip factory. Despite these extensive efforts, Micron ultimately chose to build its factory in New York. The article explores how Lockhart nearly secured this major project and what lessons can be learned for future large-scale developments.

title: How will Onondaga County supply Micron with 20 million gallons of water every day?
summary: Onondaga County faces the challenge of supplying Micron Technology's planned chip plant in Clay with 20 million gallons of water daily. This demand is equivalent to that of 150,000 homes. The supply plan involves drawing half the water from Lake Ontario and recycling the other half through a county wastewater treatment plant, an approach with limited specific details thus far.

title: The impact of US sanctions on the Chinese semiconductor landscape
summary: New US export regulations, specifically restricting American residents' involvement in Chinese semiconductor fabs and limiting equipment supply to advanced fabs in China, are significantly impacting the Chinese semiconductor industry. These rules have caused an exodus of American talent, including high-level management, and are making it much harder for companies like SMIC, YMTC, and CXMT to acquire essential manufacturing equipment and support. As a result, China's efforts to develop advanced silicon manufacturing capabilities are facing considerable challenges, potentially leading to a focus on advanced semiconductor packaging like chiplet technology as a workaround.

title: Chipmaker UMC cuts capex in readiness for prolonged downturn
summary: Taiwanese chipmaker United Microelectronics Corp (UMC) has announced a significant cut in its planned 2022 capital expenditure by almost a fifth, citing weakening consumer demand and a prolonged market downturn. The company expects headwinds in the fourth quarter and sees no immediate signs of recovery, particularly in smartphone and PC demand. Despite the cuts, UMC affirmed that expansion projects in Singapore and Tainan are proceeding to meet long-term supply commitments.

title: President Biden visits Syracuse to highlight Micron’s megafab plans
summary: President Joe Biden visited Syracuse, New York, on October 27, 2022, to highlight Micron Technology Inc.'s plans to build a $100 billion semiconductor plant in Clay. This marked his first presidential visit to Syracuse, celebrating federal efforts to boost domestic semiconductor research and manufacturing. The event at Onondaga Community College emphasized the substantial investment and job creation expected from the Micron megafab plant in Central New York.

title: President Biden leaves Syracuse after Micron victory lap, speech on economic gains
summary: President Joe Biden visited Syracuse to celebrate the federal government's efforts to boost semiconductor manufacturing, highlighted by Micron Technologies' plan for a $100 billion megafab plant in Central New York. During his speech at Onondaga Community College, Biden emphasized economic gains, including achievements in solar energy, low unemployment, and Medicare prescription caps, while also touching on manufacturing growth and student loan relief. The visit, just prior to the mid-term elections, underscored the impact of the CHIPS and Science Act, which provides $280 billion to support domestic semiconductor production, anticipated to create thousands of direct and indirect jobs in the region.

title: ASE announces capital spending cuts
summary: ASE Technology Holding Co, the world’s largest chip testing and packaging service provider, reported record-high net profits last quarter but announced a 10% cut to its capital spending for the year, down to US$1.8 billion from US$2 billion, due to slowing demand and customer inventory digestion. The company expects next year's revenue to be flat due to an industry downturn and anticipates a seasonal revenue decline of 5-10% next quarter. Despite geopolitical tensions and US semiconductor curbs on China, ASE sees no material financial impact from the latter and is expanding EMS capacity outside China to meet customer requests.

title: Micron would get $284M property tax break under proposed deal with Onondaga County, records show
summary: Micron Technology Inc. is poised to receive a nearly $284 million property tax break over 49 years from Onondaga County for its planned $100 billion computer chip plant in Clay, New York. This deal, part of a larger $9 billion incentive package, is unprecedented in its length and is presented as necessary to secure the megafab project over other competing states. The project is expected to generate significant economic benefits for the state, including an average increase of $565.5 million in local government revenues and 45,418 jobs from 2025 to 2055.

title: Semiconductors: Three-way Contest In 2022, Plus Predictions For 2023 And Beyond
summary: The semiconductor industry saw a three-way contest in 2022, with TSMC topping the third quarter, while Intel is expected to finish a distant third for the full year. Although a chip downturn is predicted for 2023, growth is expected to return in 2024, with the industry potentially reaching $1 trillion in sales by 2032. The author emphasizes the importance of India approving silicon fab proposals to capitalize on the upcoming upcycle in the middle of the decade.

title: Micron Ships World's Most Advanced DRAM Technology With 1-Beta Node
summary: Micron Technology has announced it is shipping qualification samples of its 1β (1-beta) DRAM technology to select smartphone manufacturers and chipset partners, achieving mass production readiness for the world's most advanced DRAM technology node. This LPDDR5X mobile memory delivers top speeds of 8.5 gigabits per second, with significant gains in power efficiency (15% improvement) and bit density (over 35% improvement) at 16Gb per die capacity. The 1β node is poised to support highly responsive applications from mobile to data centers and intelligent vehicles, stemming from Micron's pioneering R&D and nanomanufacturing capabilities.

title: Syracuse University Student Chosen as First Micron Intern Hire From Syracuse Veteran Community
summary: Syracuse University student Savion Pollard, a U.S. Navy veteran, has been selected as Micron Technology's first intern hire from the Syracuse veteran community. This selection is part of Micron's initiative to build a $100 billion semiconductor fabrication facility in Clay, New York, and a broader $500 million investment in education and workforce development focusing on veterans and underrepresented communities. Pollard, pursuing an electrical engineering degree, will work as an equipment engineer intern, contributing to Micron's goal of hiring over 1,500 veterans in the region.

title: Glass-based semiconductor fab breaks ground
summary: Absolics Inc., a subsidiary of SK Group, has started construction on a $600 million glass-based semiconductor manufacturing facility in Covington, Georgia. This new fab will produce glass substrates that allow for more compact multi-chip packaging and reduce power consumption by up to 50%. The project aligns with the U.S. CHIPS and Science Act, aiming to strengthen the domestic semiconductor supply chain and create over 400 jobs, with Absolics claiming to be the first globally to mass-produce this material.

title: SanDisk Professional PRO-G40 portable SSD review: Good, but expensive
summary: The SanDisk Professional PRO-G40 portable SSD offers impressive speeds, a rugged build, and dual Thunderbolt 3 and USB 3.2 Gen 2 support, making it ideal for creative professionals. However, its high price, starting at $299 for 1TB and $449 for 2TB, makes it a significant investment. While the drive performs exceptionally well under sustained load and is highly durable with IP68 water/dust resistance and drop protection, its cost is a barrier for general users who might find cheaper alternatives sufficient for their daily needs.

title: HP says it will bring jobs back to Corvallis from overseas if it gets CHIPS Act money
summary: HP Inc. announced plans to bring manufacturing jobs back to Corvallis, Oregon, if it secures funding from the CHIPS Act. This move would reverse years of downsizing in Oregon operations and consolidate development, prototyping, and manufacturing. The company sees the $52 billion federal allocation as an opportunity to reinvigorate its significant research and development site in Corvallis, which specializes in MEMS and Microfluidics.

title: HPE Expands Supercomputing Portfolio with New HPE Cray EX and XD Systems
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) has expanded its supercomputing portfolio with new HPE Cray EX and HPE Cray XD systems, designed to make high-performance computing more accessible to enterprises. These new systems leverage HPE's exascale innovation to offer enhanced performance and AI-at-scale capabilities in smaller, more energy-efficient form factors and at lower price points. This expansion aims to help various industries, including energy, financial services, health, life sciences, and manufacturing, accelerate R&D, improve time-to-market, and strengthen competitiveness through advanced modeling, simulation, and AI.

title: Micron Ships Data Center-Class DDR5, Ready For New AMD EPYC CPUs
summary: Micron Technology announced it is shipping its new data center-class DDR5 SDRAM memory in volume, coinciding with the release of AMD's new Zen 4 EPYC processors. This transition from DDR4 to DDR5 is described as a "once-in-a-decade change," crucial for handling the increasing data volumes and performance demands of modern data centers. The new DDR5 memory offers increased bandwidth, more channels, and improved power efficiency compared to its predecessor.

title: Veterans Day 2022: Why Micron is looking to Syracuse University to help hire vets at Clay plant
summary: Micron Technology Inc. plans to hire 1,500 veterans for its new $100 billion semiconductor plant in Clay, New York, representing 17% of the total workforce. The company is partnering with Syracuse University’s D’Aniello Institute for Veterans and Military Families to train veterans for jobs in the advanced manufacturing facility, recognizing their discipline and technical skills. This collaboration aims to leverage the institute's existing certification programs and develop new ones tailored to the semiconductor industry's specific needs.

title: Japan and 8 big firms pool funds for advanced chip production
summary: The Japanese government and eight major Japanese firms, including Toyota and Sony, are investing in Rapidus, a new company aimed at domestically producing next-generation semiconductors. The government will provide 70 billion yen in subsidies to Rapidus, which seeks to mass-produce advanced chips for quantum computing, autonomous driving, and AI. This initiative, along with the establishment of the Leading-edge Semiconductor Technology Center (LSTC) in collaboration with the U.S., is part of Japan's broader strategy to enhance its national economic security and strengthen its competitiveness in the global semiconductor sector.

title: Does the U.S. Need to Reduce Its Dependence on Taiwan for Semiconductors?
summary: The U.S. is heavily reliant on Taiwan for semiconductors, with Taiwan accounting for 65% of the global supply and TSMC alone producing 55%. While the U.S. is taking steps like the CHIPS Act to boost domestic production and reduce this dependence, experts believe Taiwan will remain a dominant force. The U.S. also seeks Taiwan's cooperation for export controls against China, highlighting a complex relationship where diversification is planned but difficult to achieve quickly.

title: The real winners of the chip wars are in East Asia
summary: The US is expanding restrictions on China's access to semiconductor technology, leading to a surge of investment in semiconductor production facilities across East Asia as supply chains shift away from China. Countries like Malaysia and Japan are set to benefit significantly from this redirection of investment, while South Korea and Singapore navigate a more complex path, balancing trade relations with both the US and China. Vietnam is also emerging as a major winner, attracting substantial foreign investment in its semiconductor industry.

title: Micron Unleashes Next-Generation Smartphone Experiences
summary: Micron Technology announced that its LPDDR5X mobile memory has gained significant market traction, being validated for Qualcomm Technologies' latest Snapdragon 8 Gen 2 mobile platform for flagship smartphones. This advanced memory is integrated into Snapdragon 8 Gen 2 reference designs and is now in mass production, enabling the world's first smartphones with LPDDR5X at peak speeds of 8.533 Gbps. This development supports enhanced experiences in 5G, AI, AR/VR, and high-resolution camera use cases.

title: Labor secretary encourages Micron to hire underrepresented groups, urges housing plan
summary: U.S. Labor Secretary Marty Walsh encouraged Micron Technology to recruit women, people of color, veterans, and rural residents to fill 2,000 new jobs at its planned $15 billion Boise plant, addressing a worker shortage and skills gap. Walsh also advised Boise Mayor Lauren McLean on the need for a robust housing plan, backed by federal and private investment, to support the influx of workers. Micron is launching an apprenticeship program to develop a talent pipeline from underrepresented communities.

title: Micron Technology to cut back on memory chip production
summary: Micron Technology, a memory chip maker with a manufacturing plant in Manassas, announced it is reducing its production levels. This decision comes as a direct result of a significant slump in demand for memory chips.

title: Nvidia beats revenue estimates, reassures investors
summary: Nvidia reported better-than-expected revenue for the fiscal third quarter, driven by strong demand for its AI and data-center chips, despite a slowdown in the PC market and challenges in China. The company's data center unit saw a 31% sales increase, offsetting a 51% drop in its gaming business. Although the fourth-quarter outlook was slightly below some estimates, it was sufficient to alleviate investor concerns, with CEO Jensen Huang acknowledging ongoing macroeconomic difficulties and COVID-19 restrictions in China.

title: Applied Materials to weather slowdown, forecast shows
summary: Applied Materials Inc. provided a better-than-expected sales forecast for the current quarter, indicating it can navigate an industry slowdown despite challenges like declining chip orders and US-China trade tensions. The company expects to lose up to $2.5 billion in fiscal 2023 revenue due to trade restrictions but is optimistic about outperforming the broader industry by resolving supply chain issues and leveraging continued customer spending on new technology. Its Q4 earnings and sales exceeded analyst expectations.

title: Union official: We’ll need thousands more members to build Micron megafab in Clay
summary: A Syracuse-based plumbers and steamfitters union anticipates needing thousands of new members to help construct Micron Technology Inc.'s $100 billion semiconductor plant in Clay. The union plans to ramp up recruiting, including targeting veterans, and expects an influx of experienced tradespeople and around 1,500 transient workers to meet the massive labor demand for the multi-year project.

title: After yearslong pause, 208-apartment complex near Micron site is moving ahead
summary: A 208-unit apartment complex, Tocco Villaggio III, which has been on hold for eight years, is now proceeding near the future Micron Technology Inc. chip factory in Clay, New York. Homebuilders Joseph E. Alberici and Joseph G. Alberici plan to construct the $57 million complex, consisting of eight buildings with one- and two-bedroom apartments, with an aim to complete it within two years. The developers are seeking $3 million in tax abatements to accelerate construction, citing rising costs and the anticipated demand for housing from the Micron plant and a new Amazon distribution center.

title: 400+ upscale rental houses. 100s of apartments. Micron’s big annexation. Coming near you
summary: The Treasure Valley in Idaho is experiencing a surge in development, with various projects underway across Boise, Ada County, Caldwell, Garden City, Nampa, and Meridian. Key highlights include Micron's request for Boise to annex 358 acres for a $15 billion chip-manufacturing plant, new upscale rental home and apartment complexes, and a proposed riverfront development in Garden City. Local opposition and city official recommendations are shaping the outcomes of several of these projects.

title: Micron Technology to invest RM4.4bil on 2nd factory in Penang
summary: Micron Technology announced a new investment of US$1 billion (RM4.4 billion) to build a second factory in Penang, adding to its existing US$1 billion investment since 2019. This expansion will bring their total factory space to 1.5 million sq ft and is expected to begin production next year. CEO Sanjay Mehrotra highlights the company's strong position in the semiconductor market despite current industry challenges, emphasizing the critical role of its Penang operations in its global network.

title: Micron Technology to invest RM4.4bil on 2nd factory in Penang
summary: Micron Technology announced plans to invest another US$1 billion (RM4.4 billion) in its operations in Penang, Malaysia, to build a second factory, bringing its total factory space to 1.5 million sq ft. This new investment is in addition to the US$1 billion already invested since 2019 and is expected to begin production next year, strengthening Micron's position in the semiconductor market despite a current industry down-cycle. The company also emphasizes workforce development and work-life balance initiatives, including a public-privately funded childcare center, in its Penang operations.

title: Taiwan Semiconductor Plans to Build Second Fab at Phoenix Site
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) has begun construction on a second semiconductor chip plant in Phoenix, investing a total of $40 billion across both facilities. The first fab is expected to be operational in 2024 with 4-nanometer technology, while the second will feature 3-nanometer technology by 2026. President Biden highlighted these projects as a significant step in bringing manufacturing back to the U.S. and strengthening the supply chain, supported by initiatives like the Chips and Science Act.

title: KLA Launches Revolutionary X-Ray Metrology System
summary: KLA Corporation (NASDAQ: KLAC) announced the launch of its new Axion® T2000 X-ray metrology system designed for advanced memory chip manufacturers. This revolutionary system extends KLA's metrology portfolio by addressing vertical scaling challenges in memory chip manufacturing, particularly for 3D NAND and DRAM chips. The Axion T2000 utilizes patented transmissive X-ray technology to provide high-resolution measurements of critical dimensions and 3D shapes, aiding in process control and improving yield in high-volume production.

title: TSMC triples Arizona chip plant investment, Biden hails project
summary: Taiwanese chipmaker TSMC announced it would more than triple its investment in its new Arizona plant to $40 billion, marking one of the largest foreign investments in U.S. history. President Joe Biden praised the project as a move to bring American manufacturing back and strengthen the U.S. supply chain for semiconductors. The expanded investment includes building a second facility for advanced 3-nanometer chips by 2026, aiming to create 13,000 high-tech jobs and address semiconductor shortages.

title: Micron Delivers the World's Most Advanced Client SSD Featuring 232-Layer NAND Technology
summary: Micron announced the shipment of its 2550 NVMe SSD to global PC OEM customers, marking it as the world's first client SSD to use NAND with over 200 layers (232-layer NAND). This new SSD offers significant performance improvements and power savings, extending battery life for laptops and desktops. The Micron 2550 SSD is available in various form factors and capacities, providing flexibility for system designers.

title: Dimensional US Large Cap Value ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NYSEARCA:DFLV)
summary: The Dimensional US Large Cap Value ETF (DFLV) is an actively managed exchange-traded fund launched on December 6, 2022, focusing on a diversified selection of U.S. large-cap companies with value characteristics. The ETF has assets under management of $5.29 billion, an expense ratio of 0.22%, and its stock has increased by 3.8% this year. Its top holdings include major companies like Exxon Mobil, JPMorgan Chase & Co., and Johnson & Johnson.

title: Toshiba Develops SiC MOSFET with Embedded Schottky Barrier Diode that Delivers Low On-Resistance and High Reliability
summary: Toshiba Electronic Devices & Storage Corporation and Toshiba Corporation have developed a new SiC MOSFET featuring an embedded Schottky barrier diode (SBD) arranged in a check pattern. This innovation significantly reduces on-resistance by approximately 20% compared to current SiC MOSFETs, without compromising reliability. The enhanced design addresses the trade-off between low on-resistance and high reliability, making SiC MOSFETs more suitable for applications like inverters in motor drive systems, which are crucial for electric vehicles and industrial equipment.

title: Emerging memories move to replace embedded NOR flash
summary: Emerging memories like ReRAM, MRAM, and FRAM are poised to replace embedded NOR flash, driven by its limitations below 28-nm process technology and the need for low-power, high-reliability solutions in demanding applications. Major foundries like Samsung, TSMC, and GlobalFoundries are shipping production parts with ReRAM or MRAM, while companies like Infineon, Weebit Nano, and Everspin Technologies are advancing their respective technologies for automotive, industrial, aerospace, and data center applications, addressing challenges in density, scalability, and integration.

title: AMD Radeon RX 7900 XT Review - Circuit Board Analysis
summary: This article provides a detailed circuit board analysis of the AMD Radeon RX 7900 XT, focusing on its power delivery systems for the GPU and memory. It identifies specific components like the Monolithic Power Systems controllers and DrMOS, and details the Hynix GDDR6 memory chips. The review also highlights AMD's Navi 31 graphics processor, which utilizes a chiplet architecture with a Graphics Compute Die (GCD) and Memory Cache Dies (MCDs).

title: Micron Technology Stock Has Further to Fall, Analyst Says.
summary: An analyst predicts that Micron Technology's stock will continue to decline due to worsening chip demand and elevated supply, advising investors to wait for a better entry point. The article highlights the challenging market conditions for the semiconductor company.

title: System in Package Market Size, Industry Share, Forecast to 2034
summary: The System in Package (SiP) market is projected to grow significantly due to increasing demand for compact, internet-based electronics, IoT devices, 5G-connected devices, and the miniaturization of electronic components. Key drivers include the adoption of SiP technology in smart wearables, smartphones, gaming processors, and graphic cards. The Asia Pacific region is expected to lead this growth, primarily fueled by the consumer electronics industry.

title: Mark J. Heil named vice president of finance and administration and chief financial officer at Ohio University
summary: Ohio University President Hugh Sherman has announced that Mark J. Heil has been named the university’s new vice president for finance and administration and chief financial officer. Heil previously served as interim vice president since August 2022 and brings over 30 years of experience, including roles at Boise State University and Micron Technology, Inc. He is recognized for his leadership in building effective teams and implementing financial frameworks to achieve institutional goals.

title: Micron Technology Inc. stock falls Wednesday, underperforms market
summary: Micron Technology Inc. (MU) stock declined 1.18% to $54.59 on Wednesday, underperforming the broader market. This marked the second consecutive day of losses for the company, with its shares trading significantly below their 52-week high reached in January. Trading volume for the day was also below its 50-day average.

title: Qualcomm releases IoT-optimized LTE-modem
summary: Qualcomm Technologies, Inc. has announced its new Qualcomm QCX216 LTE IoT Modem, designed to enhance computing capabilities, connectivity, and location-based technologies for IoT solutions. This modem offers optimized speeds without significantly impacting battery life, making it suitable for applications such as smart utility meters, trackers, e-mobility, and home automation. It is a cost-optimized Cat.1bis modem that supports global operation on existing LTE cellular infrastructure and enables the development of ultra-low-power IoT devices.

title: Tech: Micron looks past semiconductor downturn for opportunities, ramps up investment in Penang
summary: Micron Technology Inc. is navigating a semiconductor downturn characterized by inventory adjustments and weakened consumer demand but sees significant long-term growth opportunities driven by data-intensive technologies like AI and 5G. Despite current challenges, Micron is ramping up its investment in Penang, Malaysia, with an additional US$1 billion planned for a second factory, bringing its total investment in the Batu Kawan site to US$2 billion. The company emphasizes Malaysia's crucial role as a Centre of Excellence for its assembly and test operations and its commitment to continued expansion in the region.

title: U.S. Adds Top Chinese Chip Maker to Export Blacklist. It’s a Setback for China Relations.
summary: The U.S. government announced its intention to add Yangtze Memory Technologies, a leading Chinese memory-chip manufacturer, to an export blacklist. This move is part of an ongoing effort to restrict China's access to advanced technology. The decision is expected to be a setback for U.S.-China relations.

title: TrendForce: YMTC NAND could exit the market
summary: TrendForce has drastically revised its 2023 forecast for YMTC's NAND bit shipments from 60 percent growth to a 7 percent decline, suggesting the Chinese semiconductor company might exit the NAND market entirely. This downgrade is attributed to YMTC's confirmed Entity List status, which restricts its access to critical equipment, technical support, and development capabilities for advanced 3D NAND. Consequently, TrendForce also lowered its total market bit supply growth rate for 2023, and anticipates YMTC will be at a technology and cost disadvantage by 2024.

title: Micron Technology announces expansion and partnership with College of Western Idaho
summary: Micron Technology Inc. has announced a partnership with the College of Western Idaho (CWI) to support its new $15 billion manufacturing facility in Boise, which is expected to create 17,000 new American jobs. This collaboration will provide CWI students with education and training in mechatronics engineering technology, preparing them for careers at Micron through an "earn and learn" apprenticeship model. CWI plans to expand its relevant programs and hire more faculty to meet the workforce needs generated by Micron's expansion.

title: Micron sales could dive more than 50%, and more belt-tightening is expected before outlook improves
summary: Micron Technology expects its revenue declines to potentially exceed 50% before customer inventories are cleared and sales pick up in the latter half of 2023. The memory-chip maker is implementing austerity measures and forecast an adjusted loss for the fiscal second quarter that was wider than analyst expectations, with revenue significantly lower than the previous year. The CEO anticipates customer inventories to be in a healthier position by mid-year.

title: Micron (MU) to Cut 10% of Workforce as Demand for Computer Chips Slumps
summary: Micron Technology Inc. announced plans to cut 10% of its workforce and implement other cost-cutting measures to combat a significant industry downturn. The largest US memory chip maker anticipates difficulty returning to profitability in 2023 due to a slump in demand, projecting steep sales declines and larger losses than analysts expected for the current quarter.

title: Micron To Reduce Workforce By Almost 5,000 Due to Plunging Demand
summary: Micron Technology Inc. announced it will cut 10% of its workforce, impacting nearly 4,800 positions, and suspend 2023 bonuses due to a significant downturn in demand for semiconductors and soaring inventories. The company reported a $195 million net loss in the first quarter of its 2023 fiscal year and expects rebalancing of customer inventory levels by mid-2023. This move follows similar workforce reductions by other tech giants like Meta and Snap amid a slowing global economy and inflation-related concerns.

title: Micron to cut 10% of workforce, suspend bonuses in 2023
summary: Micron Technology announced plans to reduce its workforce by 10% and suspend bonuses in 2023 due to a significant supply-demand mismatch and challenging profitability expectations. Nearly 5,000 employees will be affected by the job cuts, which will result in a $30 million charge in the current fiscal quarter. Despite difficult conditions, the CEO reported first-quarter revenue and EPS within guidance and anticipates improved revenue in the second half of the fiscal year.

title: Applied Materials breaks ground on $600 million facility in Singapore that will add 1,000 jobs
summary: Applied Materials is investing $600 million to build a new manufacturing and research facility in Singapore, expected to create 1,000 new jobs. This expansion, part of their "Singapore 2030" plan, will significantly boost their production capacity outside the U.S. and enhance R&D capabilities. This investment underscores Singapore's continued importance as a hub for the semiconductor industry, despite recent global market slowdowns.

title: Micron Technology Sees Chip Recovery by the End of 2023
summary: Micron Technology Inc. stock has fallen significantly in 2022 due to weak demand for DRAM chips and an oversupply in the market. Despite a challenging fiscal Q1 2023 with missed earnings and lowered guidance, Micron expects improving customer inventories to lead to increased revenue in the fiscal second half of 2023. Analysts have revised their ratings and price targets, with some maintaining overweight or buy ratings despite the current downturn.

title: U.S. Pours Money Into Chips, but Even Soaring Spending Has Limits
summary: The U.S. is experiencing a significant surge in chip manufacturing investments, with companies pledging nearly $200 billion since early 2020. This boom, driven by a tech cold war with China, aims to solidify U.S. technological leadership and prevent China from dominating advanced chip production. Major companies like Intel, Micron Technology, and TSMC are investing billions in new factories and expansions across 16 states.

title: Why ASML, Applied Materials, and Micron Stock Could Be Good Plays Now
summary: Despite a poor performance in the semiconductor sector last year, New Street Research identifies potential bargains in chip stocks. The article suggests ASML, Applied Materials, and Micron stock could be good investment plays following significant declines. It focuses on the opportunity for investors to find value in the current market.

title: 10 Largest Companies with Headquarters in Idaho
summary: This article lists the top 10 largest companies headquartered in Idaho, highlighting their origins, operations, and economic impact. Key companies include Albertsons, Micron Technology, J.R. Simplot, and Boise Cascade, showcasing the state's diverse business landscape from retail and technology to agriculture and manufacturing. The article provides brief descriptions of each company, many with significant employee counts and global reach.

title: TiVoとAmlogicがスマートテレビ用チップセットにTiVo OSを搭載した製品を発表
summary: Xperiの子会社であるTiVoとファブレス半導体企業Amlogicは、米国およびヨーロッパ市場向けに、Amlogic T962D4およびT950D4チップセットにTiVo® OSを事前搭載することを発表しました。この提携により、テレビメーカーは、コスト効率の高いターンキーソリューションを通じて、独自プラットフォームの構築が難しいスマートテレビ市場において、TiVo搭載スマートテレビを迅速に市場に投入できるようになります。TiVo OSは、テレビOEMに操作性の向上、主要コンテンツへのアクセス、収益性の高いパートナーシップモデルを提供し、独立したメディアプラットフォームとしてスマートテレビ業界を支援します。

title: Kioxia and Western Digital Revive Merger Negotiations
summary: Kioxia and Western Digital have reportedly revived merger negotiations, aiming to create a publicly traded company. This potential merger, previously halted in 2021, could reshape the global NAND flash market into a duopoly with Samsung Electronics. The move is driven by the need for economies of scale amidst supply chain disruptions and increased investment costs.

title: Great Northern Mall buyer sues over stalled sale. Could Micron drive up the price?
summary: The sale of the Great Northern Mall in Clay, N.Y., is stalled, leading developer Guy Hart Jr. to sue mall owner Michael Kohan. Hart alleges Kohan failed to meet conditions of the purchase agreement, while Kohan claims Hart lacks financing and is stalling. Kohan believes the mall's value has significantly increased due to Micron Technology's planned large chip fabrication plant nearby, potentially attracting a higher offer.

title: Buffett-Backed Taiwan Semiconductor Braces for Chip-Sector Downturn
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC) reported record profits for its fourth quarter, exceeding analyst expectations, but also issued a warning about a potential downturn in demand for 2023. This news comes as the company, backed by Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway, indicates higher costs for building facilities in the U.S. compared to Taiwan. TSMC is preparing for a challenging period in the chip sector despite its strong recent financial performance.

title: How Both Taiwan and the US Can Benefit from TSMC's Investment
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) is increasing its investment in the U.S. to $40 billion, building a second factory in Phoenix, Arizona. This move, supported by the CHIPS and Science Act, aims to strengthen the U.S. domestic semiconductor industry and diversify global supply chains. Experts suggest that while complete self-sufficiency for the U.S. might be unrealistic, this investment helps address critical dependencies, enhances national security, and fosters innovation in chip manufacturing.

title: Could Micron help Clay realize decades-old dream of a waterfront village?
summary: The town of Clay, New York, hopes that Micron Technology's new computer chip plant will finally bring to fruition its two-decades-old dream of developing a vibrant waterfront village at Three Rivers Point. This development is envisioned to provide a town square for the most populous town in Onondaga County, attracting residents and visitors with shopping, dining, recreation, and housing for the incoming Micron workforce. The project, located at the confluence of the Oneida, Oswego, and Seneca rivers, could transform Clay into a central hub.

title: ASML beats earnings forecasts, sees 2023 growth amid China worries
summary: ASML Holding NV exceeded fourth-quarter earnings forecasts and projects over 25% sales growth in 2023, despite potential new U.S. export restrictions affecting its sales to China. The company's order backlog reached a record 40 billion euros, indicating strong demand from major semiconductor manufacturers. CEO Peter Wennink noted that economic conditions are expected to improve towards the end of the year, with China's economy recovering, maintaining high demand for ASML's equipment.

title: TechInsights Teardown: Roku Streambar Pro
summary: This article provides a detailed teardown analysis of the Roku Streambar Pro, a streaming device designed for an enhanced streaming experience. It highlights the device's key components, including its quad-core SoC, memory, and various integrated circuits across its main, remote control, and PSU boards. The article also breaks down the manufacturing cost of the Roku Streambar Pro.

title: Samsung Electronics Facing Tough Challenges in Memory Industry
summary: Samsung Electronics is facing increasing competition and challenges to its long-held technological leadership in the global DRAM and NAND flash memory markets. Competitors like Micron Technology, China's YMTC, and SK Hynix are rapidly advancing their NAND flash technology, with some even surpassing Samsung in layer count. While Samsung still leads in market share, industry experts warn that its technological dominance is being eroded by these fast-moving latecomers.

title: ‘Everybody was wrong’: The $160B memory-chip sector is suffering one of its worst routs ever despite vows to escape the boom-and-bust cycle
summary: The $160 billion memory-chip sector is currently experiencing one of its most severe and unprecedented downturns, despite earlier industry assurances of more predictable earnings. This crisis, marked by chip gluts, plunging prices, and order cuts, is severely impacting major players like SK Hynix Inc. and Micron Technology Inc., destabilizing suppliers, and affecting Asian economies. The industry is responding with production cuts and investment reductions, with consolidation expected, particularly in the NAND market, as companies navigate a complex mix of economic challenges.

title: Top Chinese memory chip maker YMTC to cut workers after US sanctions, sources say
summary: Yangtze Memory Technologies Corp (YMTC), a top Chinese memory chip maker, is reportedly laying off up to 10% of its workforce following its addition to a US trade blacklist. The affected employees, identified as "underperformers," include several hundred individuals, and some are being asked to repay subsidies for apartments bought through a company housing policy if they worked for less than five years. This move has garnered significant attention and sympathy on Chinese social media.

title: AMD revenue beats targets, Wall St relieved after Intel's grim outlook
summary: AMD announced revenue that surpassed Wall Street expectations, with its shares rising about 1.5% in after-hours trading, offering relief to investors following a grim outlook from rival Intel. Despite a challenging PC market leading to a 51% drop in client segment revenue for AMD, its Data Center segment saw a 42% increase. CEO Lisa Su expressed confidence in continued market share gains and a stronger second half of the year, contrasting with Intel's struggles in inventory correction and slumping PC business.

title: Volatile times for non-volatile memory as NAND sales decline
summary: NAND flash memory vendors like Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron are reporting significant drops in earnings due to a "demand implosion" for flash storage. This downturn is attributed to factors such as a post-pandemic shift in consumer spending, current macroeconomic uncertainties, and an oversupply of inventory, leading to aggressive price erosion and production cuts across the industry. Although the market is currently struggling, vendors are hopeful for a recovery in the second half of 2023 as investments and production are reduced to balance supply and demand.

title: Micron Getting Ready to Reduce Headcount at Idaho Fab
summary: Micron announced plans to reduce its global workforce by about 10%, starting with its Boise, Idaho fab. The company expects to complete these job cuts by the end of the current month and is also implementing cost-saving measures like cutting executive salaries, suspending employee bonuses, halting share buybacks, and reducing production due to lower demand. These actions come despite Micron's multi-billion dollar investment in a new fab in the same Idaho location.

title: GM locks up chip supply in deal with GlobalFoundries
summary: General Motors Co (GM) has signed a deal with GlobalFoundries Inc to secure semiconductor production capacity, aiming to alleviate the chip shortage that has impacted auto production for several years. GlobalFoundries will dedicate capacity at its New York plant for chips used in GM vehicles, with GM planning for its chip design suppliers to utilize this production for essential components. This move is intended to ensure a resilient supply of critical technology as GM anticipates its semiconductor requirements more than doubling in the coming years.

title: Revenue at three science parks set to exceed NT$4tn
summary: Taiwan's three major science parks are projected to see their revenue surpass NT$4 trillion (US$132.3 billion) this year, driven by robust demand for high-end semiconductors, despite a global technology industry downturn. The National Science and Technology Council noted that 2022 revenue already hit NT$4 trillion, reflecting a 10 percent year-on-year increase. The Cabinet has approved seven new expansion projects and allocated 693 hectares of additional land across these parks to support further growth and create 30,000 jobs.

title: Texas Instruments will invest $11B in Utah expansion
summary: Texas Instruments announced an $11 billion investment to expand its semiconductor manufacturing operations in Lehi, Utah, adding a second fabrication plant and 800 new jobs with average wages of $110,000. Utah Governor Spencer Cox hailed it as the state's largest economic investment, supported by state tax incentives and a $9 million contribution to the Alpine School District from Texas Instruments. Despite drought concerns, state officials are confident in Utah's water resources, noting the new facility will incorporate water-saving and reuse technologies.

title: Utah strikes deal with Texas Instruments for $11 billion expansion. Here’s what that means for tax breaks, jobs
summary: Utah has secured an $11 billion expansion deal with Texas Instruments for its microchip fabrication plant in Lehi, marking the largest private economic investment in the state's history. The expansion will add 800 high-paying jobs with an average salary of $110,000 and is projected to generate over $2.4 billion in wages and $111 million in state tax revenue over 20 years. Texas Instruments will receive up to $34.6 million in tax breaks through a post-performance refundable tax credit for choosing Utah among other options, thanks to its talented workforce and the incentives from the CHIPS and Science Act.

title: Taoyuan offers help to Micron, Federal workers after downsizing
summary: The Taoyuan City government has announced a special assistance program for employees of Micron Technology, Inc. and Federal Corp. Both companies, a U.S. DRAM chip supplier and a Taiwan tire brand respectively, have recently undergone downsizing. The initiative aims to support the affected workers in the region.

title: Micron Technology Inc. stock falls Friday, underperforms market
summary: Micron Technology Inc. (MU) shares fell 1.73% on Friday, closing at $59.01, underperforming the broader market. This marked the third consecutive day of losses for the company, which closed significantly below its 52-week high. Trading volume was also lower than its average.

title: PoE Switch Integrates Advanced Network, Security Features for Outdoor Applications
summary: Microchip Technology has released the PDS-204GCO switch, an outdoor Power over Ethernet (PoE) switch designed for smart city and building applications. This switch enhances network reliability and cybersecurity for mission-critical uses, offering advanced features like high ingress protection, cybersecurity for data and system management, and redundancy through fiber links and Ethernet Ring Protection Switching. It supports long-distance connectivity and simplifies installation for distributed IoT devices.

title: This university is addressing the shortage of U.S. semiconductor engineers
summary: Purdue University has launched an interdisciplinary Semiconductor Degrees Program to address the shortage of U.S. semiconductor engineers, partnering with major chip companies. This initiative includes undergraduate and graduate training, associate degrees, and an advisory board of industry executives to shape the curriculum and recruit talent. The program also involves plans for a MediaTek chip design center and a SkyWater Technology manufacturing facility on Purdue's campus, reinforcing the university's commitment to supporting the domestic chip industry.

title: Samsung’s DRAM market share rises amid industrywide revenue drop
summary: Despite an industrywide revenue decline, Samsung Electronics Co. saw its DRAM market share increase to 45.1% in the fourth quarter of last year, even as its DRAM sales revenue dropped by 25.1%. The company recorded the smallest revenue fall among the top three DRAM players, while competitors SK Hynix Inc. and Micron Technology Inc. experienced steeper declines in both revenue and market share. This shift indicates Samsung's aggressive pricing strategy to boost shipments amidst weakening demand, prompting major chipmakers to plan production cuts for 2023.

title: Rockwell Automation acquires Knowledge Lens
summary: Rockwell Automation has acquired Bengaluru-based enterprise insights provider Knowledge Lens for an undisclosed sum. This acquisition aims to boost Rockwell's capabilities in data utilization, autonomous manufacturing, and continuous optimization for manufacturers worldwide. Knowledge Lens will join Rockwell's digital services business, Kalypso, to accelerate transformational outcomes for its global clientele, aligning with Rockwell's strategy to expand capabilities and explore early-stage investments in India.

title: DC-DC converter Global Market Report 2023
summary: The global DC-DC converter market grew from $10.24 billion in 2022 to $11.68 billion in 2023 at a CAGR of 14.1%, with projections to reach $18.43 billion by 2027. This growth is driven by increasing power consumption and technological advancements, such as Rohm Semiconductor's new buck DC/DC converter IC for ADAS. North America was the largest region in 2022, and key market players include Texas Instruments, Murata, and Infineon Technologies.

title: ASUS Intel 700, 600 Series Motherboards Support 48 GB of DDR5-7000 modules
summary: ASUS has released BIOS updates for its Intel 700 and 600 series motherboards, adding support for up to 192 GB of DDR5 memory. This includes support for new Corsair Vengeance DDR5 48 GB (2 x 24 GB) 7000 MT/s modules, allowing for up to 48 GB of DDR5 RAM at 7000 MT/s. The update caters to enthusiasts and professionals who require high-capacity, high-speed memory for demanding workloads like gaming, video editing, and 3D modeling.

title: Super-investor Mohnish Pabrai’s value investing principles
summary: Mohnish Pabrai, founder and CEO of Pabrai Investment Funds, is a value investor inspired by Warren Buffett. He has amassed a significant net worth by focusing on undervalued stocks for the long term, despite only holding a few select companies. The article outlines five of Pabrai's key investment strategies, including minimizing downside risk, avoiding shorting, approaching retail investments cautiously, buying at "maximum pessimism," and understanding a company's "DNA structure."

title: Korea to raise young chip engineers for Samsung, SK Hynix
summary: South Korea plans to establish specialized "meister" high schools in Yongin, Incheon, and Kangwon Province to train young semiconductor engineers, aiming to address a significant shortage of skilled workers for companies like Samsung and SK Hynix. These chipmakers will partner with the schools, providing expertise and guaranteeing jobs for graduates. The initiative seeks to strengthen Korea's chip industry against global competition from countries like Taiwan.

title: Hightec Systems continues to grow as essential partner for semiconductor and FPD manufacturers
summary: Hightec Systems Corporation, led by CEO Moriaki Abe, has thrived despite Japan's "lost 30 years" by developing a unique three-pronged business model that includes buying/selling used manufacturing equipment, offering engineering services, and importing advanced microelectromechanical systems technology. The company emphasizes long-term customer relationships and is now looking to expand its global reach into the U.S. and European markets, building on its existing business in China, South Korea, and Taiwan. Abe's persistent efforts in securing new clients and fostering customer trust have been crucial to the company's growth from three to 100 employees and $200 million in annual sales.

title: Qualcomm opens Hsinchu center
summary: Qualcomm Inc. has opened a new manufacturing engineering and testing center in Hsinchu, Taiwan, signifying an expansion of its presence and investment in the region. The company expects its purchases in Taiwan to increase to NT$300 billion next year and is strengthening its ties with Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC). This new center is Qualcomm's largest and most advanced engineering testing center outside its US headquarters.

title: Taiwan PCB makers moving production to Southeast Asia
summary: Taiwanese Printed Circuit Board (PCB) manufacturers are relocating production from China to Southeast Asian countries like Thailand, Malaysia, and Vietnam. This move is driven by factors such as investment incentives and lower manufacturing costs in these regions, despite challenges including wastewater management and anti-Chinese sentiment in some areas. Companies like Unimicron, Kinsus Interconnect Technology, and Tripod Technology are establishing or expanding facilities, capitalizing on the growing semiconductor supply chain in Malaysia and Vietnam.

title: Micron Technology Inc. stock falls Friday, underperforms market
summary: Micron Technology Inc. (MU) shares fell 0.29% on Friday, closing at $61.16, despite a positive overall market performance with the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average both rising. The closing price was $25.08 below its 52-week high. Trading volume for Micron exceeded its 50-day average.

title: Memory chipmaker Micron expects revenue drop, expects AI to boost sales in 2025
summary: Micron Technology expects a significant revenue drop for its third quarter, in line with Wall Street predictions, but anticipates a strong recovery in 2025 driven by artificial intelligence. The company plans to reduce investments and cut headcount while emphasizing its strong position to capitalize on the growing demand for memory chips in AI servers. Despite near-term challenges, Micron is proceeding with new factory constructions and sees improving customer inventories and supply-demand balance.

title: Micron Could Report Largest Ever Loss in Q2
summary: Micron Technology Inc. (MU) is projected to report its largest-ever loss in the second fiscal quarter of 2023, with an estimated net loss of nearly $1.2 billion and EPS of -$1.06, a significant drop from the prior year. This financial struggle is attributed to weak consumer demand, high inflation, inventory issues, and industry-wide supply constraints. Analysts predict negative gross margins for the first time in 15 years, with an expected inventory write-down in the range of a billion dollars.

title: Intel’s stock set for best month in 22 years as execs lay out plans to take on AMD’s rise in servers
summary: Intel's stock experienced its best month since 2001, with shares jumping 7.6% on Wednesday after an investor presentation detailed the company's strategy to regain market share in data-center chips. The company aims to reverse its manufacturing decline and compete more effectively against AMD. This surge pushed Intel's March gain to 26.4%.

title: US-China chip war: Japan plans to restrict some equipment exports
summary: Japan's government plans to restrict exports of 23 types of semiconductor manufacturing equipment, following similar actions by the US and the Netherlands, amidst the ongoing US-China chip dispute. While Japan stated the measures are for international peace and stability and not specifically targeting China, they will impact major Japanese technology companies like Tokyo Electron and Nikon. The US had previously urged its allies to adopt such restrictions to curb China's access to advanced chip technology.

title: News | Micron Deal Fortifies Bustling District's Position as True Tech Hub
summary: Micron Technology's lease for over 93,000 square feet at Portman Holdings' 712 West Peachtree project in midtown Atlanta has been recognized with a 2023 CoStar Impact Award for best lease. This deal firmly establishes midtown Atlanta as a prominent tech hub, with Micron's new design center expected to create 500 jobs and involve a $15 million investment. The 20-story office complex, completed in 2021, is becoming a key location for major technology companies.

title: Micron: 'We stand by the security of our products' in response to announcement of review by China
summary: China's Cyber Security Review Office announced a review of products sold in China by Micron Technology, a Boise-based semiconductor company. Micron, which derives 11% of its annual sales from China, stated it is cooperating with the review and stands by the security of its products. This announcement comes amidst increasing U.S.-China tensions and a challenging period for Micron, which recently reported a significant net loss and plans for cost-cutting measures.

title: China hits Micron with review of chips, citing security risks
summary: China has launched a cybersecurity review of memory chips produced by U.S. firm Micron Technology, escalating the semiconductor dispute between the two countries. Beijing stated the review aims to ensure supply chain integrity and national security, following worsening tensions over Taiwan and alleged spy balloons. Micron's shares fell by 4.4% on the news, as mainland China accounts for approximately 11% of its sales.

title: Beijing’s Micron Probe Spurs $12 Billion Rally in China Chip Stocks
summary: Chinese chip stocks experienced a significant rally, collectively adding over $14 billion in market value, following Beijing's probe into Micron Technology Inc. This surge reflects optimism that domestic firms will benefit from China's increased focus on self-reliance in the semiconductor industry. Companies like Ingenic Semiconductor and Advanced Micro-Fabrication Equipment saw substantial gains.

title: 7 AI Stocks to Buy for 100% Returns
summary: This article identifies seven AI stocks that could potentially offer 100% returns for investors. It discusses companies like Micron Technology, CrowdStrike, Duos Technologies, Ambarella, Alibaba, JD.com, and BigBear.ai, highlighting their AI applications and financial health. The broader AI market is projected to grow significantly, making these speculative investments potentially rewarding.

title: ION Stock Price | Proshares S&P Global Core Battery Metals ETF
summary: This article provides detailed information about the Proshares S&P Global Core Battery Metals ETF (ION), including its current stock price, trading exchange (NYSE), and various key statistics such as expense ratio, dividend yield, and historical performance. It also includes an overview of the ETF's holdings and company profile, indicating its focus on companies involved in the mining of lithium, nickel, and cobalt.

title: Tata Electronics Appoints Dr. Randhir Thakur as Chief Executive Officer & Managing Director
summary: Tata Electronics Private Limited (TEPL) has appointed Dr. Randhir Thakur, a prominent leader in the semiconductor industry, as its Chief Executive Officer & Managing Director. Dr. Thakur brings over 40 years of experience from global manufacturing, R&D, and P&L management, including his recent role as President of Intel Foundry Services. N. Chandrasekaran, Chairman of Tata Sons, expressed confidence that Dr. Thakur's leadership will significantly advance Tata Electronics' role in India's semiconductor and precision manufacturing sectors.

title: TSMC seeks $15B in U.S. support despite ‘unacceptable’ conditions
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) is seeking $15 billion in U.S. government aid for its Arizona chip plants but finds some of the conditions attached to the CHIPS Act funding "unacceptable." The primary concern involves rules requiring profit-sharing if returns exceed projections, which TSMC chairman Mark Liu fears could complicate profit calculations and make the Arizona investment less attractive. Other conditions, like providing affordable childcare and restrictions on expanding manufacturing in "countries of concern" such as China, also pose challenges for chipmakers.

title: Semiconductor industry electricity consumption to more than double by 2030: study
summary: A Greenpeace East Asia report projects that global semiconductor manufacturing will consume 237 terawatt hours of electricity by 2030, more than doubling current levels and equating to Australia's 2021 electricity consumption. This energy-intensive industry, critical to the tech supply chain, is also set to emit 86 million tonnes of CO2e by 2030, exceeding Portugal's 2021 total emissions. The report criticizes major manufacturers like Samsung and TSMC for their reliance on fossil fuels and lack of ambitious 100% renewable energy commitments by 2030, urging them to accelerate renewable energy development in East Asia.

title: Lam Research has closed its U.S. facilities for the week amid the chip downturn
summary: Lam Research has temporarily closed its U.S. facilities for an additional week this year due to a downturn in the semiconductor industry and decreased demand. This marks a third week of closure, beyond the typical two, and follows previous announcements of job cuts by the company.
