title: SK Hynix sees H2 memory chip rebound; outlook helps shares brush off record loss
summary: SK Hynix reported a record operating loss of $2.54 billion in the first quarter but expects market conditions for memory chips to improve in the second half of 2023 due to production cuts across the industry. The company also forecast that artificial intelligence applications would drive a significant increase in memory chip revenue over the next five years. This positive outlook helped its shares rise despite the substantial loss.

title: Top U.S Memory Chipmaker Micron Likely To Secure Approval To Set Up A $1-billion ATMP-focussed Semiconductor Unit In India
summary: U.S. memory chipmaker Micron Technology is reportedly close to securing approval for a $1 billion investment to establish an assembly, testing, marking, and packaging (ATMP) facility in India. This move is part of India's broader ambition to achieve self-sufficiency in semiconductor manufacturing under the India Semiconductor Mission, which offers fiscal support for such facilities. Micron, a major player in DRAM and NAND flash memory, plans to invest $150 billion globally over the next decade in manufacturing and R&D, despite recent market headwinds.

title: Chinese memory chip suppliers said to benefit from Beijing’s probe into Micron
summary: China's cybersecurity review of US memory chip giant Micron Technology is creating a significant opportunity for domestic suppliers, particularly Yangtze Memory Technologies Co (YMTC), to fill a potential market gap. This development comes amid heightened US-China tensions and could see Chinese companies benefit from a possible ban on Micron products, especially as South Korean rivals Samsung and SK Hynix also face uncertainties. Local analysts and merchants believe Chinese firms are well-positioned to address the demand in the memory chip sector.

title: Micron to invest $3.7 billion in Japan for new DRAM chips
summary: Micron Technology Inc plans to invest up to 500 billion yen ($3.70 billion) in extreme ultraviolet (EUV) technology in Japan over the next few years, with government support. This investment aims to produce 1-gamma chips using the latest EUV chipmaking machines, marking Micron as the first semiconductor company to bring EUV technology to Japan for production. The company expects to ramp EUV into production on the 1-gamma node in Taiwan and Japan from 2025 onwards, following previous mass production of 1-beta DRAM chips in Hiroshima.

title: End-Users Have No Idea How Cool the UFS 4.0 Standard Is and That’s Okay
summary: The article discusses the Universal Flash Storage (UFS) 4.0 standard, highlighting its significant improvements in data transfer speeds, power efficiency, and security compared to previous generations and the eMMC standard. It explains how UFS 4.0 enables advanced applications like AR/VR, automotive systems, and smartphones, despite end-users not necessarily understanding the underlying technology. The authors emphasize the importance of selecting the right partners for designing and verifying UFS 4.0 solutions due to the inherent complexities.

title: Applied Materials Launches Multibillion-Dollar R&D Platform in Silicon Valley to Accelerate Semiconductor Innovation
summary: Applied Materials, Inc. announced a multibillion-dollar investment to build the Equipment and Process Innovation and Commercialization (EPIC) Center in Silicon Valley. This facility will be the world’s largest and most advanced for collaborative semiconductor process technology and manufacturing equipment R&D, aiming to accelerate innovation and reduce the time to commercialization for new technologies. The center will provide dedicated space for chipmakers and university researchers, fostering collaboration and addressing critical industry challenges like talent shortages and R&D costs.

title: Invesco PHLX Semiconductor ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:SOXQ)
summary: The Invesco PHLX Semiconductor ETF (SOXQ) tracks a market-cap-weighted index of 30 U.S.-listed semiconductor companies, launched in June 2021. As of March 2026, SOXQ was trading at $64.09, showing a 14.9% increase year-to-date, with key holdings including NVIDIA, Broadcom, and Advanced Micro Devices. The ETF manages over $1 billion in assets, driven partly by significant investments in AI chip stocks.

title: SOXQ Top Holdings List & Exposure (Invesco PHLX Semiconductor ETF)
summary: This article provides a detailed list of the top holdings and exposure for the Invesco PHLX Semiconductor ETF (SOXQ). It breaks down the fund's investments by company, geographic region, currency, sector, and industry. The top holdings include NVIDIA, Broadcom, and Advanced Micro Devices, with a significant allocation to the United States and the technology sector, specifically semiconductors.

title: Applied Materials' Big Bet: A ‘Unique’ $4B Chip R&D Center
summary: Applied Materials is investing up to $4 billion to build the "EPIC Center," a 180,000-square-foot semiconductor R&D facility in Silicon Valley. This unique center will bring together chipmakers, academics, and ecosystem partners to accelerate manufacturing innovation, reduce costs, and shorten product development timelines, especially crucial in the complex 'angstrom era' of chipmaking. The investment's final size is contingent on subsidies from the U.S. government’s CHIPS and Science Act.

title: Direxion Daily GOOGL Bear 1X Shares ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:GGLS)
summary: This article provides details about the Direxion Daily GOOGL Bear 1X Shares (GGLS) ETF, which offers inverse exposure to Alphabet Inc. Class A stock. It includes current pricing, key statistics such as market capitalization and expense ratios, news headlines, and details on its holdings and major shareholders. The ETF was launched on September 7, 2022, and is managed by Direxion.

title: Stock of the week - Micron Technology (25.05.2023)
summary: Micron Technology faced a significant development this week as China banned some of its products due to security concerns, impacting a portion of its revenue, primarily from the public sector. Despite this, the company plans a substantial investment in Japan, which could help offset the losses from China. Micron is also navigating a market glut and inventory write-downs, expecting stabilization in fiscal-Q3 and a return to profitability by mid-2024, with its stock showing bullish technical patterns.

title: ISMC's India chip plan stalls after Tower-Intel deal in setback to Modi
summary: India's ambitious drive to establish itself as a significant semiconductor manufacturing hub is facing considerable hurdles. A proposed $3 billion chip facility by the ISMC consortium has stalled following Intel's acquisition of Tower Semiconductor, a key technology partner. Additionally, a $19.5 billion joint venture between Vedanta and Foxconn is slow to progress, as talks to secure European chipmaker STMicroelectronics as a partner have reached an impasse.

title: Analog Devices reaffirms its position in Singapore’s semiconductor market with a new facility
summary: Analog Devices has opened a new 38,951 sq. ft. facility in Singapore's Kallang Industrial Park, bolstering its presence in the region's semiconductor market. This facility will serve as a central hub for R&D, test engineering, sales, and manufacturing, reflecting the company's global expansion and commitment to leveraging Singapore's talent pool and advanced manufacturing. The investment aligns with Singapore's efforts to grow its electronics sector and attract more Western chipmakers.

title: Here’s how Applied Materials manages supply chain and semiconductor research
summary: Applied Materials plans to invest US$4 billion over seven years into a collaborative R&D facility in Silicon Valley, the Equipment and Process Innovation and Commercialization (EPIC) Center, to accelerate semiconductor technology development. The company emphasizes the critical role of R&D and collaboration in the semiconductor industry and discusses global supply chain changes and its investment strategy in the US and Singapore. Applied Materials also outlines its Singapore 2030 plan, focusing on manufacturing capacity expansion, deepening R&D, strengthening partnerships, and talent development, with Singapore serving as its sole Center of Excellence for advanced packaging.

title: Samsung Electronics makes 17-fold gains from investment in ASML
summary: Samsung Electronics has seen its initial investment in ASML Holding N.V. grow 17-fold, reaching $4.56 billion, despite reducing its stake to 1.6%. The article highlights how major chipmakers like Samsung, Intel, and TSMC invested in ASML over a decade ago when the company needed funding for R&D. The gains underscore the critical importance of ASML's EUV scanners in the semiconductor industry, with Samsung actively securing these machines to compete with market leader TSMC.

title: Micron Makes 178 MW Wind Power Deal with Terra-Gen
summary: Micron Technology Inc. has signed a 15-year virtual power purchase agreement with Terra-Gen LLC for approximately 178 MW of wind electricity capacity and associated renewable energy certificates annually. This deal supports Micron's U.S. target to reach 100% renewable energy by the end of 2025 and is a step towards achieving net zero greenhouse gas emissions by 2050. The agreement will help Micron diversify its energy portfolio and manage long-term energy costs.

title: Power Transistor Market Size, Growth | Forecast Report [2034]
summary: The global power transistor market was valued at USD 15.00 billion in 2025 and is projected to grow to USD 40.56 billion by 2034, exhibiting a CAGR of 12.30% during the forecast period. Asia Pacific dominated the market in 2025, driven by the presence of modern semiconductor manufacturing units and R&D centers. Key factors driving market growth include increasing use of transistors in connected devices and growing environmental concerns in the electronics industry, while high costs and durability issues act as restraining factors.

title: Invesco Semiconductors ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (PSI) $PSI
summary: MarketBeat has assigned the Invesco Semiconductors ETF (PSI) an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy" with an average price target of $95.38. This rating is derived from analyzing 505 analyst ratings over the past year covering 25 companies within the ETF, which represent 89.4% of its portfolio. The report provides a detailed breakdown of consensus ratings and price targets for the ETF's top 25 holdings, alongside their individual share prices, market rank, and potential upside.

title: Lam Research Introduces World's First Bevel Deposition Solution to Increase Yield in Chip Production
summary: Lam Research has unveiled Coronus DX, the semiconductor industry's inaugural bevel deposition solution designed to enhance chip production yield. This innovation applies a protective film to wafer edges, preventing defects that can occur during advanced manufacturing processes for next-generation logic, 3D NAND, and advanced packaging. The Coronus DX aims to address manufacturing complexities, reduce wafer damage, and increase yield by 0.2% to 0.5% per step, potentially adding millions of dollars in value for high-volume manufacturers.

title: Applied Materials to invest $400 million in India for new engineering center
summary: U.S. semiconductor toolmaker Applied Materials (AMAT.O) announced a $400 million investment over four years to establish a new engineering center in India. This move follows Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi's meeting with Applied Materials CEO Gary Dickerson and is part of a broader wave of investments in India's chip industry. The new facility, planned for Bengaluru, aims to support over $2 billion in planned investments and create 500 new engineering jobs.

title: Micron confirms up to $825 million investment in India chip facility
summary: Micron Technology announced an investment of up to $825 million to build its first chip assembly and test facility in Gujarat, India. The total project investment is expected to reach $2.75 billion, with significant contributions from the Indian central government and the state of Gujarat. Construction will begin in 2023, with the first phase operational by late 2024, creating up to 5,000 direct jobs.

title: Micron, Applied Materials make big investments in India
summary: Chipmakers Micron Technology and Applied Materials are making significant investments in India, bolstering the nation's ambition to become a silicon superpower. Micron plans to build an assembly and test facility valued at $825 million, while Applied Materials will establish a $400 million engineering center. These announcements coincide with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi's state visit to Washington, highlighting growing US-India collaboration in technology and defense, including jet engine manufacturing and spaceflight partnerships.

title: Nike Inc. (NKE Stock) – Earnings on June 29, Implications?
summary: Nike Inc. (NKE) is set to release its earnings report on June 29, with an expected revenue of $12.583 billion, facing an overall anticipated decline in Q2 S&P 500 earnings. Despite a recent drop in share price, analysts project a moderate buy rating with a 20.6% upside, contingent on positive earnings and market conditions to overcome current resistances. The article highlights financial analysis, revenue and EPS expectations, and technical indicators for NKE stock.

title: Applied to set up engineering center in India
summary: Applied Materials plans to invest US$400 million over four years to establish a collaborative engineering center in Bangalore, India. This center will focus on developing semiconductor manufacturing equipment technologies, creating 500 new advanced engineering jobs and supporting an additional 2,500 jobs in the manufacturing ecosystem. The initiative aims to enhance India's role in the global chip industry and foster semiconductor talent.

title: Micron signs preliminary agreement for India chip facility
summary: U.S. memory chip firm Micron Technology signed a memorandum of understanding with the Indian government to build its first semiconductor plant in the country. Micron will invest up to $825 million, with the total investment reaching $2.75 billion with support from both the Indian central government and the state of Gujarat. The facility will be constructed in Sanand, near Ahmedabad.

title: Chipmaker Micron beats on demand from booming AI, easing glut
summary: Micron Technology exceeded third-quarter revenue estimates driven by high demand for its memory chips in the rapidly expanding artificial intelligence sector and an easing supply glut in traditional markets. The company's shares rose over 2% after reporting stronger-than-expected earnings and anticipating improved pricing trends. Despite ongoing U.S.-China tech tensions impacting its presence in the Chinese market, Micron expressed confidence in the memory industry's recovery.

title: China puts the choke on raw materials used in semiconductors
summary: China is imposing export restrictions on gallium and germanium, two elements crucial for semiconductors and other electronics, effective August 1. This move is widely seen as retaliation for Western restrictions on chip technology sales to China. While these controls might not cause an immediate global shortage, they highlight the rising geopolitical tensions over critical supply chains, particularly in the semiconductor industry.

title: HPE signs pact to produce $1 bn worth of servers in India
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) has signed an initial pact with VVDN Technologies to produce high-end servers worth $1 billion in India over the next 4-5 years. This collaboration falls under the Indian government's Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for IT hardware and aligns with the "Make in India" initiative. Production is slated to begin in November, with HPE aiming to better serve Indian customers and capitalize on the country's growing digital economy.

title: ASE, Amkor, UMC and Samsung Getting a Slice of the CoWoS Market from AI Chips, Challenging TSMC
summary: The surging demand for AI chips is overwhelming TSMC's CoWoS packaging capacity, leading chipmakers like Nvidia to diversify their supply chains. This shift creates opportunities for other foundries and OSATs such as UMC, ASE (including SPIL), and Samsung, which are developing their own advanced packaging solutions to meet the growing market needs. Samsung, with its turnkey service and significant R&D investments in 2.5D packaging, is actively pursuing a larger share of the AI chip market.

title: FTC Solar Appoints Tony Alvarez as Board Observer
summary: FTC Solar, a global provider of solar tracker systems, announced the appointment of Tony Alvarez as a Board Observer, effective July 5, 2023. Alvarez brings over 35 years of experience in the solar and semiconductor industries, having held senior roles at companies like Infineon, Solaria, and Cypress Semiconductor. His expertise is expected to be invaluable as FTC Solar positions itself for continued growth.

title: Samsung takes on Micron with new flash auto memory UFS 3.1
summary: Samsung Electronics has begun mass production of its new Universal Flash Storage (UFS) 3.1 automotive chip, designed for in-vehicle infotainment systems with significantly reduced power consumption. The company aims to surpass Micron Technology as the market leader by 2025 by supplying these chips to global automakers and parts manufacturers. This move strengthens Samsung's presence in the rapidly growing automotive semiconductor market, driven by increasing demand for advanced chips in electric and self-driving vehicles.

title: Xtrackers Semiconductor Select Equity ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:CHPS)
summary: The Xtrackers Semiconductor Select Equity ETF (CHPS) invests in global semiconductor companies screened for ESG criteria. Launched on July 13, 2023, the ETF has seen its shares increase by 23.3% this year and holds $43.51 million in assets under management. Its top holdings include SK hynix Inc., Applied Materials, and Micron Technology.

title: Micron to host public open house on semiconductor project in Clay
summary: Micron Technology Inc. will host a public open house in August to provide details about its planned $100 billion semiconductor fabrication facility in Clay, New York. The event will include remarks from Micron and local officials, an overview of the project, and opportunities for the public to ask questions of Micron staff. This comes after the Clay Town Board approved rezoning for the 1,200-acre White Pine site for industrial use.

title: Micron applies for an estimated $300 million in local tax breaks for Clay megafab
summary: Micron Technology Inc. has filed an application for an estimated $300 million in property tax breaks for its planned $100 billion semiconductor fabrication facility in Clay, N.Y. State officials anticipate the deal will include nearly $85 million in payments in lieu of taxes over 49 years, saving Micron approximately $284 million. This application marks a significant step for what Micron projects to be the largest semiconductor facility in U.S. history, creating up to 50,000 jobs.

title: Micron seeks estimated $284M in tax breaks for Clay chip fab
summary: Micron Technology Inc. has formally applied to the Onondaga County Industrial Development Agency for an estimated $284 million in property tax breaks over 49 years for its $100 billion semiconductor plant in Clay. The application moves the company closer to finalizing a Payment in Lieu of Taxes (PILOT) plan. This step is crucial for the project to proceed with construction, as the Onondaga County IDA has been named the lead agency for the environmental reviews.

title: After 9 quiet months, Micron project in Clay vaults forward in one week
summary: After nine months of behind-the-scenes work, the Micron Technology project in Clay, New York, made significant public strides in one week. The town board rezoned the project site, Micron formally applied for local tax breaks and agreed to a $25 million development fee, and the county development agency voted to initiate an environmental impact study. These steps mark a crucial advancement for the $100 billion semiconductor plant, which has a projected construction start in late 2024 and production as early as 2026, contingent on federal CHIPS Act funding.

title: Nvidia is ‘dominating’ and could unlock $300 billion in AI revenue by 2027, analyst says
summary: Mizuho analyst Vijay Rakesh is increasingly optimistic about Nvidia's prospects, projecting the company could tap into $300 billion in AI-related revenue by 2027. Rakesh believes Nvidia will maintain a significant share in the AI server market despite potential future competition. This outlook comes as Nvidia's stock has already seen a 200% increase this year.

title: Micron Delivers Industry's Fastest, Highest-Capacity HBM to Advance Generative AI Innovation
summary: Micron Technology announced it has begun sampling the industry's first 8-high 24 GB HBM3 Gen2 memory, boasting bandwidth greater than 1.2 TB/s and pin speed over 9.2 Gb/s, a significant improvement over existing HBM3 solutions. This new memory offers a 2.5 times performance per watt improvement, setting new benchmarks for AI data center metrics and reducing training times for large language models like GPT-4. Micron's 12-high stack with 36 GB capacity is expected to sample in early 2024, further enhancing capacity and efficiency for generative AI and HPC applications.

title: U.S. chipmaker AMD to invest $400 million in India by 2028
summary: U.S. chipmaker Advanced Micro Devices (AMD.O) announced a significant investment of $400 million in India over the next five years, including the establishment of its largest design center in Bengaluru, creating 3,000 new engineering jobs. This move aligns with India's efforts to become a chipmaking hub, attracting other major players like Applied Materials and Micron with its incentive programs. AMD's expansion underscores the country's growing importance in the global semiconductor industry.

title: AMD to establish $400M chip design center in India
summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) announced plans to invest $400 million in a new chip research and engineering center in Bangalore, India, which will be its largest design center globally with 3,000 engineers. This expansion will bring AMD's total headcount in India to over 9,000 employees. The move follows similar investments by other major chipmakers like Micron Inc. and Applied Materials Inc. in India.

title: China is anticipating its first 28nm lithography machine by the end of 2023
summary: China's Shanghai Micro Electronics Equipment (SMEE) is expected to deliver its first domestically produced 28nm lithography machine by the end of 2023. This development aims to reduce China's reliance on foreign semiconductor technology, especially in light of export restrictions imposed by the US, Japan, and the Netherlands. While a significant step towards self-sufficiency, challenges remain regarding bulk production and the reliance of complex chipmaking components on a global supply chain.

title: Micron Launches Memory Expansion Module Portfolio to Accelerate CXL 2.0 Adoption
summary: Micron Technology announced the sample availability of its CZ120 memory expansion modules, designed to accelerate CXL 2.0 adoption. These modules, available in 128 GB and 256 GB capacities, enhance server systems by providing incremental memory capacity and bandwidth, particularly beneficial for AI, SaaS, and HPC workloads. Micron is collaborating with Intel and AMD to validate the CZ120 modules on their CXL-compatible platforms.

title: US investors flag retaliation risks after Biden's China tech curbs
summary: US investors are concerned about potential retaliation from China after President Biden's new executive order restricting certain US technology investments in China. While the initial market reaction was subdued, portfolio managers fear China might strike back, as it has in the past, potentially by restricting crucial exports or targeting more US companies. Some investors are already reducing their exposure to China due to ongoing US-China tensions.

title: Li Lu Dumps Micron Technology, Adds To East West Bancorp
summary: Himalaya Capital Management founder Li Lu significantly adjusted his second-quarter portfolio, selling his entire stake in Micron Technology Inc. and increasing his holdings in East West Bancorp Inc. These moves reflect Li Lu's adherence to value investment principles, focusing on companies with high economic moats, growth potential, and trustworthy management. The article details the reasons behind these changes, including Micron's overvaluation and East West Bancorp's strong performance potential.

title: Mohnish Pabrai’s 5 Biggest Investments in 10 Years
summary: This article examines one of Mohnish Pabrai's significant past investments, Micron Technology, Inc. (NASDAQ: MU), highlighting its performance and the reasons behind his recent sell-off. Despite a 26.7% stock decline since late 2021 due to slowing demand for memory chips, the article also touches on the company's promising future in the AI memory market. It includes commentary from LRT Capital Management regarding market reactions to Micron's guidance.

title: Crucial 32GB DDR5-5600 Pro Desktop Memory - Reviews - all-pages
summary: This article reviews the Crucial 32GB DDR5-5600 Pro Desktop Memory kit, highlighting its features like a low-profile heat spreader, Intel XMP 3.0 and AMD EXPO support, and improved power efficiency. While it performs well and is optimized for the latest Intel and AMD processors, its conservative timings mean it's not the fastest on the market, though some overclocking is possible at the risk of voiding the warranty. The memory is positioned as a suitable choice for gamers and content creators seeking stability and performance for modern CPUs.

title: Samsung weighs retooling Galaxy S series with Exynos chipsets
summary: Samsung Electronics is considering reintroducing its Exynos chipsets into some Galaxy S models next year to reduce its reliance on Qualcomm's Snapdragon chips, which have led to rising application processor (AP) purchasing costs. The company spent \$4.3 billion on APs in the first half of this year, and aims to regain bargaining power lost by exclusively using Qualcomm chips in its latest premium phones. Samsung is reportedly looking at a dual-chip strategy for the Galaxy S24 series, with Exynos chips potentially used in some variants.

title: Japan's export controls on lithography and thin film deposition more likely to be enforced, says DIGITIMES Research
summary: DIGITIMES Research analyst Eric Chen predicts that Japan's export controls, which became effective July 23, will particularly impact China's advanced semiconductor manufacturing in areas of lithography and thin film deposition equipment. These restrictions cover 23 types of semiconductor equipment and specifically target technologies involving metal interconnect deposition, atomic layer deposition for sub-40nm processes, and hard mask deposition. The controls also extend to immersion DUV lithography and certain EUV-related process and inspection equipment, as well as SiGe etching equipment due to its use in aerospace and military applications.

title: SOXQ ETF: Huawei's Latest Phone And Implication For Semis Stocks
summary: Huawei has launched the Mate 60, a new 5G smartphone, signaling its recovery from past sanctions and strengthening China's semiconductor industry. This development poses competitive risks for holdings within the Invesco PHLX Semiconductor ETF (SOXQ). Given these implications for the semiconductor market, the author maintains a "Hold" position on SOXQ.

title: Micron invests in Taichung fab
summary: Micron Technology Inc. is expanding its most advanced DRAM chip production in Taiwan, with the Taichung fab set to mass produce 1-gamma node chips using EUV technology by 2025. Donghui Lu, head of Micron Taiwan, emphasized Taiwan's well-established semiconductor ecosystem as a key reason for continued investment, despite geopolitical concerns and calls for supply chain localization. More than 65 percent of Micron's DRAM products are already manufactured in Taiwan, with capacity expected to grow in both Taiwan and Japan.

title: Micron Technology Inc. stock rises Thursday, outperforms market
summary: Micron Technology Inc. (MU) saw its stock increase by 1.33% on Thursday, closing at $71.79. This rise occurred during a generally positive trading day, with the S&P 500 Index (SPX) gaining 0.84% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) rising 0.96%. This marked the third consecutive day of gains for Micron's stock.

title: Judge Albright Orders Plaintiff to Produce Documents Related to Defendants’ Marking Defense
summary: Judge Albright issued an order compelling Viasat Inc. to produce documents identifying its products incorporating flash memory, the dates of sale, and the specific flash memory used, in response to a discovery dispute initiated by Western Digital. Western Digital argued that Viasat's products should have been marked under 35 U.S.C. § 287(a), citing prior case law where components were incorporated into final products. Despite Viasat's arguments regarding burden and differing interpretations of "patented article," Judge Albright granted Western Digital's production request.

title: Micron begins construction of US$15 billion DRAM fab in Boise ...
summary: Micron Technology Inc. has started construction of a new memory wafer fab at its Boise, Idaho headquarters, an investment totaling US$15.3 billion over the decade. This facility marks the first memory fab built in the US in 20 years and is supported by anticipated grants from the CHIPS and Science Act. Micron also plans a larger fab campus in New York and has committed US$75 million to community and workforce development in Idaho.

title: REX FANG & Innovation Equity Premium Income ETF (FEPI) Price, Holdings, & News
summary: The REX FANG & Innovation Equity Premium Income ETF (FEPI) is an actively managed exchange-traded fund launched on October 9, 2023. It aims to mirror the returns of the Solactive FANG Innovation Index while generating monthly income by investing in index securities and writing call options. The fund currently has $584.04 million in assets under management and a dividend yield of 27.49%.

title: Beryllium Oxide (Beo) Powder Market Size, Share, Report [2033]
summary: The global Beryllium Oxide (BeO) Powder market was valued at USD 0.69 billion in 2024 and is projected to reach USD 0.86 billion by 2033, exhibiting a CAGR of 2.55%. Key market drivers include demand from aerospace, defense, and electronics industries due to BeO's excellent thermal management properties, while restraining factors include health risks associated with beryllium exposure and stringent environmental regulations. North America is expected to dominate the market owing to its advanced electronics and aerospace sectors.

title: Micron Commemorates 45 Years of Innovation with the Inauguration of its State-of-the-Art Assembly & Test Facility in Malaysia
summary: Micron Technology has opened a new state-of-the-art assembly and test facility in Batu Kawan, Penang, marking its 45th anniversary. This expansion, part of a $2 billion investment, increases Micron Malaysia's production capabilities for NAND, PCDRAM, and SSD modules to meet demand for technologies like AI and electric vehicles. The facility emphasizes sustainability, featuring green building certifications and operating on 100% renewable electricity, aligning with Malaysia's New Industrial Master Plan 2030 (NIMP 2030).

title: MU Stock Price: Will Micron Technology Stock Hit New Highs?
summary: Micron Technology (MU) stock has bounced 8% from recent lows, defending the $65 support level and sustaining above both 50-day and 200-day EMAs. The positive reaction was driven by strong quarterly earnings reported on September 28, 2023, where the company beat EPS and revenue estimates. While the stock shows a bullish trend and is expected to hit a new 52-week high by November end, it is currently near a supply zone around $70-$72, requiring careful monitoring for a confirmed breakout.

title: Micron Low-Power Memory Solution Boosts Mixed and Virtual Reality Experiences on Snapdragon XR2 Gen 2
summary: Micron Technology announced that its LPDDR5X DRAM and UFS 3.1 embedded solutions have been qualified for Qualcomm's Snapdragon XR2 Gen 2 Platform. These solutions offer significant speed, performance, and low-power consumption in small form factors, essential for untethered mixed reality (MR) and virtual reality (VR) devices. This advancement aims to enhance immersive experiences, with the Snapdragon XR2 Gen 2 processor already commercially debuted in Meta Quest 3, promoting thinner and more comfortable headsets with extended battery life.

title: Micron Announces Sampling of 9.6 Gbps LPDDR5X Memory
summary: Micron Technology announced it is shipping production samples of its 1β (1-beta) mobile-optimized LPDDR5X memory, operating at a record 9.6 Gbps. This memory is designed for use with Qualcomm's Snapdragon 8 Gen 3 mobile platform to enable generative AI at the edge. It offers enhanced performance and power efficiency crucial for on-device AI applications, reducing reliance on cloud-based solutions.

title: [News] Western Digital’s Impressive Results Raise Hopes for Memory Market Revival
summary: Western Digital reported Q1 2024 revenues of $2.75 billion, a 3% sequential increase, leading to optimism for a memory market recovery. The company's CEO attributed the improved margins to differentiated products and anticipates sustained growth in consumer, client, and cloud markets. TrendForce data reveals Western Digital holds a 14.7% NAND Flash market share, and the storage industry is poised for an upturn with projected price increases for DRAM and NAND Flash in Q4 2023.

title: TTM Technologies to invest $130m in manufacturing facility, sparking tech boom in CNY
summary: TTM Technologies plans to invest up to $130 million in a new high-tech manufacturing facility in DeWitt, New York, adjacent to their current plant. This investment aims to create 400 new jobs and establish Central New York as a hub for advanced printed circuit board manufacturing crucial to the U.S. semiconductor industry. The project is seen as a significant step towards a tech boom in the region, drawing approval from local leaders and politicians.

title: Micron Announces 128GB DRAM Low-Latency, High-Capacity RDIMMs
summary: Micron Technology has launched new 128 GB DDR5 RDIMM memory modules featuring 32Gb monolithic die technology, offering best-in-class performance up to 8000 MT/s. These high-capacity, low-latency modules are designed for data center and cloud environments, particularly for AI, in-memory databases, and high-performance computing, providing significant improvements in bit density, energy efficiency, latency, and AI training performance over competitive products. AMD and Intel have expressed interest in these new RDIMMs for their latest server platforms due to the enhanced capacity and bandwidth benefits.

title: XAIX ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns XETR:XAIX
summary: This article provides an analysis of the Xtrackers Artificial Intelligence & Big Data UCITS ETF (XAIX), outlining its investment objectives, holdings, and performance. It details the fund's asset allocation, top holdings like Samsung Electronics and Micron Technology, and key operational metrics such as AUM, expense ratio, and the index it tracks. The article also confirms that XAIX does not pay dividends and discusses its recent price and NAV performance.

title: Who Benefits from Rewiring Supply Chains?
summary: This article examines which industries and companies are likely to benefit from the ongoing deglobalization trend and the rewiring of global supply chains. It highlights that certain industries are more susceptible to these shifts, with some companies facing negative repercussions while others gain from strategies like nearshoring, friendshoring, or reshoring. Using MSCI Economic Exposure data, the authors identify potential beneficiaries, particularly in emerging markets excluding China, illustrating an indexed approach that considers both thematic and structural opportunities.

title: TSMC mulls next-gen chip fab in Japan
summary: TSMC is reportedly considering building a third advanced 3-nanometer chip manufacturing plant in Japan, specifically in Kumamoto, indicating Japan's potential to become a global chipmaking hub. This move builds on existing plans for two other facilities in Japan, and is supported by significant subsidies from the Japanese government aiming to bolster its domestic semiconductor ecosystem. The decision is driven by Japan's expertise in chip materials and machinery, and is expected to bring substantial economic benefits to the Kyushu region.

title: US CHIPS Act threatens to hollow out Asian semiconductor industry
summary: The US CHIPS Act, designed to boost domestic semiconductor manufacturing, raises concerns in Asia that it could hollow out the region's tech industries. While the US aims to reduce its reliance on foreign fabrication, especially from China and Taiwan, the act's subsidies and tax credits attract major semiconductor companies to the US, potentially affecting Asian exporters. This leads to fears of a global semiconductor glut and protectionism as other countries also implement their own subsidy programs.

title: Apple announces expanded partnership with Amkor for advanced silicon packaging in the U.S.
summary: Apple announced an expanded partnership with Amkor, designating Apple as the first and largest customer for Amkor's new manufacturing and packaging facility in Peoria, Arizona. This facility will package Apple silicon produced at the nearby TSMC fab, strengthening Apple's commitment to American manufacturing. Amkor plans to invest approximately $2 billion in the project, creating over 2,000 jobs, as part of Apple's broader commitment to invest $430 billion in the U.S. economy.

title: ASML boss Wennink to retire in April; veteran Fouquet to step up
summary: ASML Chief Executive Peter Wennink will retire in April 2024 after overseeing a decade of significant growth, during which the company became Europe's largest technology firm. He will be succeeded by company veteran Christophe Fouquet, who has extensive experience with ASML's critical extreme ultraviolet (EUV) product lines. Additionally, Chief Technology Officer Martin van den Brink will also retire, and Jim Koonmen will join the management board as Chief Customer Officer.

title: QTEC ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns NASDAQ:QTEC
summary: This article provides a detailed analysis of the First Trust NASDAQ-100-Technology Sector Index Fund (QTEC), an ETF that offers equally-weighted exposure to large-cap tech stocks listed on Nasdaq. It covers key statistics like Assets Under Management, expense ratio, and dividend history, along with its investment strategy, holdings, and recent performance. The fund is noted for its unique equal-weighting approach which differs from market-cap weighted tech funds.

title: Axcelis Announces Shipments of Purion XE High Energy and Purion H High Current Implanters to DRAM Memory Chipmakers
summary: Axcelis Technologies, Inc. announced the shipment of its Purion XE high energy and Purion H high current implanters to DRAM memory chipmakers in Korea and China. These shipments support capacity expansions, including a follow-on shipment to an existing customer and a new customer penetration in China. The Purion XE and Purion H systems are recognized for their advanced technology, offering high reliability, wide energy range, and superior contamination control, contributing to increased yield and lower cost of ownership for chip manufacturers.

title: Governor Hochul Makes Transformative Investments in Central New York to Prepare Students for Jobs in Growing Technology Manufacturing Industry
summary: Governor Kathy Hochul announced significant investments in Central New York, including breaking ground on the $74 million Syracuse STEAM High School, to prepare students for jobs in the growing technology manufacturing industry. This initiative, supported by New York State, Micron Technology, and Amazon, aims to create a tech-talent pipeline and establish a global chip-making hub in New York, building on Micron's $100 billion investment in the region. The state and Micron will also invest $4 million in a New York Advanced Technology Framework to help school districts develop high-tech manufacturing curriculums.

title: [News] After Memory Price Hike, Shortages Emerge in Some Products?
summary: Following a prolonged downturn, the memory market is showing optimism with price hikes by manufacturers like Western Digital, Samsung, and Micron, leading to shortages in certain products. Western Digital anticipates a cumulative price increase of over 55% for flash memory components, while Samsung considers a 20% sequential price increase for Q1 and Q2 next year. Industry players like Phison and ADATA report significant recovery and expect potential supply shortages for DRAM and NAND Flash in the coming year, although long-term demand growth and inventory management remain key concerns.

title: ASML, Samsung to spend US$760m to build S Korea plant
summary: ASML and Samsung Electronics will jointly invest US$760 million to construct a plant in South Korea dedicated to developing advanced semiconductor processing technology, utilizing ASML's next-generation extreme ultraviolet equipment. This initiative expands ASML's presence in South Korea and aims to strengthen the country's technology sector, with South Korean President Yoon Suk-yeol actively promoting stronger tech ties and domestic innovation. The collaboration also includes plans for a "Korea-Netherlands Advanced Semiconductor Academy" to address labor shortages and enhance educational opportunities in the field.

title: Micron Technology Inc. stock rises Tuesday, outperforms market
summary: Micron Technology Inc. (MU) saw its stock rise by 0.75% on Tuesday, closing at $82.17. This increase contributed to a positive day for the broader market as well, with the S&P 500 Index (SPX) gaining 0.59% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) rising 0.68%. This marks the second consecutive day of gains for Micron Technology.

title: Micron Technology Inc. stock rises Thursday, outperforms market
summary: Micron Technology Inc. (MU) saw its stock rise by 8.63% to $85.48 on Thursday, outperforming the broader market. The company also reached a new 52-week high, surpassing its previous peak from December 15th, as the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average also posted gains.

title: The U.S.-China Chip War: Who Dares to Win?
summary: The U.S. and China are engaged in a semiconductor industry rivalry, with the U.S. aiming to boost domestic chip production through the CHIPS and Science Act while imposing sanctions on China to hinder its self-reliance in the sector. However, the CHIPS Act faces challenges like high production costs and specific conditions that may deter leading companies. Other nations like the EU and Japan are also implementing strategies to increase their semiconductor manufacturing capabilities amid global supply chain concerns.

title: Netherlands blocks some ASML exports to China
summary: The Dutch government has partially revoked an export license for certain ASML chipmaking equipment to China, impacting a small number of Chinese customers. This decision follows US export restrictions and affects advanced lithography systems. Despite China being a major market for ASML, the company does not expect a material impact on its financial outlook.

title: Micron awaits big decision on how much feds will kick in for $100 billion chip plant
summary: Micron is awaiting a crucial decision from the federal government regarding the size of the grant it will receive for its planned $100 billion computer chip plant in the Syracuse area, which could total $3 billion or more. The CHIPS and Science Act provides $52 billion in incentives to boost the U.S. semiconductor industry, and Micron's CEO has stated that federal financial incentives are essential for their expansion in the U.S. The company has applied for grants for projects in both New York and Idaho, with the New York facility aspiring to be the largest semiconductor manufacturing complex in U.S. history.

title: Micron Technology Inc. stock rises Friday, outperforms market
summary: Micron Technology Inc. (MU) shares rose 0.89% on Friday, closing at $83.45. This performance outpaced the broader market, which also saw gains with the S&P 500 Index rising 0.18% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average increasing 0.07%. This marks the second consecutive day of gains for Micron's stock.

title: Samsung, SK hynix to build $471B chip hub in South Korea
summary: Samsung Electronics Co. Ltd. and SK hynix Inc. are planning to invest approximately $471 billion (622 trillion won) to construct a massive chip hub in South Korea, with Samsung contributing 80% of the capital. This ambitious project, spanning Pyeongtaek to Yongin and continuing until 2047, will include 13 new fabs and three research facilities, aiming to produce 7.7 million silicon wafers monthly by 2030 and push advancements in chip manufacturing, such as gate-all-around architectures. The investment highlights the global race among chipmakers to expand manufacturing capacity and innovate new semiconductor technologies.

title: NBT chief says strong bench gave him confidence to retire now
summary: John Watt, CEO of NBT Bancorp, announced his retirement effective in May, citing the strength of the company's internal leadership bench as a key factor. Watt, who oversaw the doubling of NBT's assets since 2016, emphasized his pride in the succession plan that will see CFO Scott Kingsley take over as CEO, with other internal promotions for key roles. He also highlighted his focus on ensuring a smooth transition and supporting NBT's strategic initiatives, including leveraging economic opportunities from Micron Technology's investment in the Syracuse region.

title: Lam Research delivers strong results, forecast as electronics demand recovers
summary: Lam Research has exceeded analyst expectations for third-quarter revenue and profit forecasts, driven by a rebound in demand for electronics products and an increase in chipmakers' orders, partly due to the boom in artificial intelligence. The company's shares rose after the announcement, and its results, alongside those of ASML and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co., suggest a recovery in the computer chip market despite a drop in revenue from China due to US export restrictions.

title: Intel collaborates with UMC to develop new 12nm process node
summary: Intel Foundry Services (IFS) is partnering with United Microelectronics Corporation (UMC) to develop a new 12nm semiconductor process platform. This new node, aimed at high-growth markets like communication infrastructure and networking, will be manufactured at Intel's Arizona fabs, with production anticipated to start in 2027. The collaboration is expected to leverage UMC's capabilities and market access to bolster Intel's foundry business and global semiconductor supply chain presence.

title: Kioxia and WD SSD merger on-off talks are back on again
summary: Merger talks between Kioxia and Western Digital for their NAND/SSD businesses have reportedly resumed, despite previous failures due to complications with Kioxia's ownership structure and SK hynix's competitive concerns. A combined Kioxia-WD entity would rival Samsung in market share, suggesting potential adjustments to accommodate SK hynix's interests, possibly through a stake in the merged business. Western Digital is also in the process of separating its disk drive and NAND/SSD units, further adding to the complexity of the proposed merger.

title: [News] Multiple Fabs in Japan to Commence Production Post-2024 Boosting Industry
summary: Japan is set for a significant revitalization of its semiconductor industry with multiple new fabs, including TSMC's Kumamoto fab, commencing large-scale production post-2024. This development will enhance Japan's semiconductor manufacturing capabilities, transitioning to more advanced process technologies like JASM’s 12-nanometer from Renesas’s 40-nanometer. The Japanese government is heavily subsidizing these projects, attracting major players like Kioxia, Western Digital, Renesas, Toshiba, ROHM, Micron Technology, and Rapidus, despite concerns about talent shortages.

title: Micron Technology Inc. stock falls Wednesday, still outperforms market
summary: Micron Technology Inc. (MU) shares fell 0.48% to $85.75 on Wednesday, marking its second consecutive day of losses. Despite the drop, the stock still outperformed the broader market, as the S&P 500 Index fell 1.61% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average dropped 0.82% in what was a poor trading session overall.

title: National Advanced Packaging Manufacturing Program
summary: The CHIPS National Advanced Packaging Manufacturing Program (NAPMP) has awarded $300 million to Absolics Inc., Applied Materials Inc., and Arizona State University for advanced substrates and material research. This funding aims to boost the U.S. leadership in advanced packaging, enabling domestic manufacturing and addressing technological challenges like designing tightly integrated chips, power delivery, heat dissipation, testing, and reliability. The program seeks to create a robust domestic advanced packaging ecosystem by accelerating innovation transfer, developing digital tools, and fostering partnerships to build the workforce.

title: Micron Technology Inc. stock falls Tuesday, underperforms market
summary: Micron Technology Inc. (MU) stock declined by 2.74% on Tuesday, closing at $84.60, despite a generally positive day for the broader market. This drop ended a two-day winning streak for the company's shares. Meanwhile, the S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average both saw increases.

title: Manufacturer positions itself for semiconductor success - Buffalo Business First
summary: Materion Corp. is strategically investing in its Buffalo plant to capitalize on the growing semiconductor industry. With approximately 200 local employees, the company produces specialty materials for major semiconductor manufacturers like Micron and Intel. This positioning allows Materion to benefit from the increasing momentum in the region's semiconductor sector.

title: 21 Top Semiconductor Companies in India
summary: India is rapidly expanding its semiconductor industry, with a focus on both design and manufacturing, supported by the $10.2 billion India Semiconductor Mission. The article highlights 21 key companies, including major global players and local innovators, that are crucial to this growth, with the sector projected to reach $64 billion by 2026. These companies contribute to various aspects of semiconductor technology, from design and fabrication to memory solutions and AI processors.

title: Prem Watsa Bolsters Stake in Orla Mining Ltd
summary: Prem Watsa's Fairfax Financial Holdings significantly increased its stake in Orla Mining Ltd, adding over 17 million shares at $3.53 each. This transaction boosts Orla Mining's representation in Fairfax's portfolio to 11.28% and reflects Watsa's continued confidence in the gold and silver exploration company. The move aligns with Watsa's conservative value investment philosophy and his strategy of long-term shareholder value.

title: ASML's next chip challenge: rollout of its new $350 mln 'High NA EUV' machine
summary: ASML is ramping up production of its new $350 million "High NA EUV" machine, a critical device for advanced chipmaking, with plans to ship "a number" this year. The company anticipates an inflection point for customer adoption around 2026-2027, despite some analysts suggesting economic viability might be further in the future. Intel has already received a pilot device, while other major chipmakers like TSMC and Samsung intend to use the technology.

title: GraniteShares 2x Long NVDA Daily ETF (NVDL) Short Interest & Short Float | Updated Mar 2026
summary: This article details the short interest and short float for GraniteShares 2x Long NVDA Daily ETF (NVDL) as of December 15, 2025. NVDL had 14.34 million shares sold short, representing 29.90% of the public float, marking a 32.88% increase from the prior report. title: US considering more than $10 billion in subsidies for Intel, Bloomberg reports
summary: The Biden administration is reportedly in talks to provide Intel Corp with over $10 billion in subsidies, including loans and direct grants, under the CHIPS Act. This funding aims to boost semiconductor manufacturing in the U.S., supporting Intel's plans for chip factories in Arizona, New Mexico, and Ohio, and potentially impacting other chipmakers like TSMC, Micron, and Samsung also seeking funds. The move comes as Intel faces delays in its Ohio plant due to market slowdowns and the slow rollout of federal dollars.

title: Kioxia offers to make chips for SK Hynix to help revive merger talks with Western Digital: Report
summary: Kioxia has reportedly offered to produce non-volatile memory for SK Hynix at its Japanese fabs to address SK Hynix's concerns regarding a merger with Western Digital. This strategic move aims to secure SK Hynix's approval for the merger, which would create a dominant player in the global 3D NAND market. The combined Kioxia and Western Digital entity would control a significant portion of the 3D NAND market, potentially even if some capacity is dedicated to SK Hynix.

title: GlobalFoundries, DOC finalize $1.5B funding award
summary: GlobalFoundries has finalized a $1.5 billion funding award from the U.S. Department of Commerce under the CHIPS and Science Act. This federal funding, alongside over $550 million from New York State, will support GlobalFoundries' $13 billion investment to expand its semiconductor manufacturing facilities in Malta, New York, and Essex Junction, Vermont. The expansion aims to boost domestic chip production for various industries, create jobs, and enhance U.S. competitiveness in semiconductor manufacturing.

title: Micron Technology Inc. stock rises Friday, outperforms market
summary: Micron Technology Inc. (MU) saw its stock rise by 0.10% to $86.00 on Friday, outperforming the broader market. This marked the company's fourth consecutive day of gains, despite the S&P 500 Index and the Dow Jones Industrial Average also posting modest increases.

title: Micron begins production of a 'better' HBM3E memory
summary: Micron Technology has started volume production of HBM3E DRAM for AI datacenters, claiming its offering consumes 30 percent less power than competitors. Its 24Gbyte 8-high HBM3E components will be featured in Nvidia's H200 tensor core GPUs shipping in Q2 2024, potentially positioning Micron ahead of rival SK Hynix in HBM3E production. The company is also preparing a 12-high 36Gbyte HBM3E component for introduction in March 2024, with its stock opening almost 5 percent higher on the news.

title: Analog Devices Strengthens Capacity and Resiliency Through Expanded Partnership with TSMC
summary: Analog Devices (ADI) has expanded its partnership with TSMC, securing long-term wafer capacity through TSMC's Japanese manufacturing subsidiary, JASM. This strategic arrangement strengthens ADI's chip supply for critical applications using 40nm and finer process technology nodes, reinforcing its resilient hybrid manufacturing network. The collaboration aims to ensure more robust supplies and faster responses to customer needs and market changes.

title: No 'intel' inside: US court says Chinese company didn't spy and steal trade secrets from Micron
summary: A US court has ruled in favor of a Chinese chipmaker accused by the US Department of Justice of stealing trade secrets from American memory chip manufacturer Micron Technology. The court dismissed all charges, citing insufficient evidence to support the espionage claims. This ruling could influence the Biden administration's approach to prosecuting such cases and may affect US-China tensions related to technology.

title: 2 Reasons to Buy Into Lam's 185% Rally, 1 Reason to Run Away
summary: This article examines two reasons investors might consider buying Lam Research (LRCX) stock after its significant rally, primarily driven by its position in the semiconductor equipment industry and its robust cash flow and shareholder returns. However, it also highlights one major risk: the cyclical and often volatile nature of the semiconductor industry, which could lead to sharp downturns. Investors are encouraged to weigh these factors carefully before investing.

title: Lam Research Honored by Ethisphere as One of the 2024 World's Most Ethical Companies®
summary: Lam Research has been recognized by Ethisphere as one of the 2024 World's Most Ethical Companies for the second consecutive year, highlighting its commitment to business integrity. The company is the only wafer fabrication equipment provider on this year's global list. This distinction reflects Lam Research's robust ethics, compliance, and governance practices, which underpin its values-led culture and focus on semiconductor breakthroughs.

title: TSMC to Win More Than $5 Billion in Grants for US Chip Plant
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) is expected to receive over $5 billion in federal grants for its chipmaking project in Arizona, a significant move for President Biden's bid to revitalize American semiconductor manufacturing. This award, part of the 2022 Chips and Science Act, is not yet finalized but marks a major step in distributing funds to leading chipmakers like Intel, Micron Technology, and Samsung. TSMC is investing $40 billion in two Arizona fabrication facilities, though the project has faced delays due to labor shortages and technological considerations.

title: Micron Technology appoints Swan to board
summary: Micron Technology has appointed Robert "Bob" Swan, former CEO and CFO of Intel Corporation, to its board of directors. Swan brings extensive experience from the semiconductor, technology, and industrial sectors, having also held executive roles at eBay, Electronic Data Systems Corp., TRW, Inc., Webvan Group, Inc., and General Electric. His expertise in driving growth and operational excellence is expected to be invaluable to Micron's strategic direction and expansion in the memory and storage industry.

title: Semiconductor Intellectual Property (IP) Companies -Arm Limited (UK) and Synopsys, Inc. (US) are the Key Players
summary: The semiconductor intellectual property (IP) market is projected to grow from USD 7.5 billion in 2024 to USD 11.2 billion by 2029, driven by increasing demand for electronics in healthcare and telecommunications. Key players like Arm Limited and Synopsys, Inc. are strengthening their market position through acquisitions and partnerships, focusing on new processor IP and advanced microcontroller units. The market faces challenges such as concerns related to Moore's Law and increasing IP thefts, but opportunities arise from the rising demand for semiconductor components in automotive and data center verticals.

title: Micron Technology Inc. stock rises Tuesday, still underperforms market
summary: Micron Technology Inc. stock saw a modest increase of 0.23% on Tuesday, closing at $94.00. Despite this rise, the company's performance still lagged behind the broader market, as both the S&P 500 Index and the Dow Jones Industrial Average recorded more significant gains. This marks the third consecutive day of gains for Micron Technology.

title: Intel awarded up to $19.5 billion in grant, loan for US chip production
summary: The Biden administration has awarded Intel Corp. a federal grant of up to $8.5 billion and a loan of up to $11 billion to expand semiconductor manufacturing in Arizona, New Mexico, Ohio, and Oregon. This is the largest award from the 2022 CHIPS and Science Act, aiming to reduce U.S. dependence on foreign chip suppliers. The funding is expected to stimulate over $100 billion in private investment from Intel and create 10,000 manufacturing jobs and 20,000 construction jobs.

title: Micron Technology Q2 earnings causes 20% share price increase
summary: Micron Technology experienced a 20% surge in its stock price following stronger-than-expected Q2 fiscal 2024 earnings, driven by robust artificial intelligence memory sales. The company reported an adjusted profit of 42 cents per share against an anticipated loss, with revenues significantly exceeding forecasts at $5.82 billion. This positive performance and an optimistic outlook for the fiscal third quarter have generated strong buying interest in Micron stock.

title: US memory chip maker Micron soars after AI growth boosts revenue forecast
summary: Micron Technology provided a surprisingly strong revenue forecast for the current quarter, driven by increasing demand for artificial intelligence (AI) hardware. The company anticipates revenue between US$6.4 billion to US$6.8 billion, surpassing analyst estimates. This positive outlook signals a rebound for the memory chip industry, with projections for record sales levels in 2025.

title: Inside Micron's most advanced memory fab - we visited the colossal control room and newest A3 fab in Taiwan
summary: This article details a visit to Micron's highly advanced DRAM manufacturing facilities in Taiwan, specifically highlighting the scale of operations at Fab 16, the cutting-edge A3 facility, and the Remote Operations Center. It touches upon Micron's sustainability efforts, diverse memory product portfolio, and the company's optimistic outlook amid the AI boom, contrasting it with previous industry downturns. The article also provides a profile of Dr. Donghui Lu, Head of Micron Taiwan, emphasizing his passion for PC DIY and background in the tech industry.

title: Micron has own take on novel selector-only memory
summary: Micron has developed its own version of a novel memory type called Single-chalcogenide X-Point Memory (SXM), which is based on Ovonic Threshold Selector (OTS) devices. This technology, similar to selector-only memory from SK Hynix and Samsung, combines memory cell and selector functions, potentially offering improved density and latency with non-volatility. Micron reported on a 256Gbit 4-deck SXM component with 15 million cycle endurance and 7-year retention at 40 degrees C.

title: Netherlands devises €1B plan to keep ASML in the country
summary: The Dutch government is reportedly finalizing a €1 billion plan to invest in the Eindhoven region, where chip-machine maker ASML is based, in an effort to prevent the company from relocating. This initiative, dubbed "Operation Beethoven," aims to address ASML's concerns about the business climate by targeting infrastructure, housing, and an expansion of the Eindhoven University of Technology to foster a skilled workforce. ASML is Europe's most valuable tech company and a crucial player in the global semiconductor supply chain, making its retention a top priority for the Netherlands.

title: Micron Tech cements bond with China
summary: Micron Technology is strengthening its commitment to the Chinese market by expanding its Xi'an plant and increasing investment. Company executives emphasize China's critical role in their global business and its significance for innovation and customer-oriented products. This move comes despite previous cybersecurity reviews by Chinese authorities, signaling Micron's dedication to its Chinese customers and the local semiconductor industry.

title: Micron Technology Inc. stock falls Thursday, underperforms market
summary: Micron Technology Inc. (MU) stock declined by 1.05% on Thursday, closing at $117.89, despite an overall positive trading day for the broader market. This drop ended a nine-day winning streak for the company's shares. The S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average both saw slight increases.

title: PSI ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns AMEX:PSI
summary: This article provides a detailed analysis of the Invesco Semiconductors ETF (PSI), covering its investment strategy, asset allocation, performance metrics, and dividend information. PSI is a passively-managed fund that invests in 30 semiconductor companies, weighting them based on quantitative methodology. Key details include its $1.30 billion AUM, 0.08% dividend yield, and its recent price performance.

title: Chip maker SkyWater cancels plans for Indiana plant—at least for now
summary: SkyWater Technology has canceled its plans for a $1.8 billion semiconductor R&D and fabrication facility in West Lafayette, Indiana, which was expected to create 750 jobs. The decision comes as the project was dependent on funding from the CHIPS and Science Act, which SkyWater did not receive. Despite this, Purdue University states SkyWater remains a valued partner for research opportunities, and the Indiana Economic Development Corp. committed incentives, though none were claimed.

title: TSMC's $6.6bn Arizona deal the latest in the CHIPS Act rollout
summary: The Biden administration has announced a preliminary agreement for up to $6.6 billion in grants and $5 billion in loans for Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) under the CHIPS program. This investment, combined with TSMC's planned expansion in Arizona, totals $65 billion and aims to restart the semiconductor industry in the U.S. The Commerce Department expects the U.S. to produce about 20% of the world’s most advanced chips by 2030, a goal supported by the CHIPS funding and noted for its potential political implications in swing states.

title: Samsung wins $6.4 billion CHIPS grant for Texas project. Is Micron next?
summary: Samsung Electronics has secured a $6.4 billion federal CHIPS Act grant to expand its chip manufacturing and research facilities in Texas, committing nearly $45 billion in investment. This agreement makes Samsung the sixth company to receive CHIPS funding, with attention now turning to Micron Technology and Texas Instruments, who are the last major applicants yet to finalize deals. Micron is planning a massive $100 billion chip plant complex in upstate New York and is a key candidate for the remaining CHIPS Act funds, particularly for advanced memory chip production.

title: Avnet bullish on China's growth
summary: Avnet, a US-based semiconductor distributor, is optimistic about China's long-term growth, citing opportunities in the digital economy and low-carbon transformation. The company plans to continue investing in the Chinese market, focusing on new energy facilities and supporting domestic chip manufacturers' global expansion. This confidence is bolstered by China's emphasis on technological innovation and the resilience of its semiconductor industry despite challenges.

title: Corsair MP600 Elite review: "Managed to scrape past the read speeds of the FireCuda 530"
summary: The Corsair MP600 Elite is a new Gen 4 NVMe SSD offering competitive performance for both PC and PS5 users. It boasts impressive read and write speeds, surpassing some established rivals like the Seagate FireCuda 530 in certain aspects, and features an aesthetically pleasing and functional heatsink design. Priced competitively, it presents a strong alternative in a market experiencing rising SSD costs and limited stock for popular models.

title: Micron wins $6.1 billion CHIPS grant for Central NY and Idaho projects, Schumer says
summary: Micron Technology is set to receive a $6.1 billion grant from the federal government through the CHIPS and Science Act to support the construction of a massive computer chip plant complex in Syracuse, New York, and an expansion at its Idaho headquarters. This grant is a crucial milestone for Micron's planned $100 billion investment in Central New York, which is expected to create thousands of jobs and dramatically boost U.S. semiconductor manufacturing. The deal is still preliminary and non-binding, pending further review and final negotiations.

title: Micron Poised to Get Over $6 Billion in Chips Grants in Announcement Next Week
summary: Micron Technology Inc. is expected to receive $6.1 billion in grants from the Commerce Department under the CHIPS Act. This funding will support domestic factory projects as part of efforts to boost US semiconductor production. The announcement is anticipated next week, and Micron, like Intel and TSMC, will also accept loans as part of its award package.

title: US to Provide $6.1 Billion for Micron Technology Chip Plants
summary: The Biden administration will provide $6.1 billion in government support for Micron Technology to produce advanced memory computer chips in New York and Idaho. This funding, from the 2022 CHIPS and Science Act, aims to revive the manufacturing sector, create 50,000 jobs, and bolster domestic semiconductor production. Micron plans to invest a total of $100 billion in upstate New York over two decades and $15 billion in Boise, Idaho.

title: Micron set to get $6.1 bln in chip grants from US
summary: Micron Technology is expected to receive $6.1 billion in grants from the U.S. Commerce Department to support its domestic chip factory projects in New York and Idaho. This funding, part of the CHIPS & Science law, aims to boost U.S. chip production and reduce reliance on foreign manufacturing. The award follows similar grants to Intel, TSMC, and Samsung, reinforcing the U.S. strategy to revitalize its semiconductor industry.

title: Huge CHIPS Grants Awarded to TSMC, Samsung, and Micron
summary: The U.S. government has announced significant CHIPS Act grants to Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC), Samsung, and Micron, following an earlier grant to Intel. These grants, totaling billions of dollars, are intended to support the expansion and construction of semiconductor manufacturing facilities within the U.S. The Commerce Department plans to disburse all $39 billion in manufacturing incentives under the CHIPS and Science Act by the end of the year.

title: Semiconductor Market size is set to grow by USD 156.39 billion from 2023-2027, Analog Devices Inc., Broadcom Inc. and Infineon Technologies AG, and more to emerge as Some of the Key Vendors, Technavio
summary: The global semiconductor market is projected to grow by USD 156.39 billion from 2023 to 2027, with a CAGR of 5.08%, driven by increasing technology integration, renewable energy, and IoT. Key vendors such as Analog Devices Inc., Broadcom Inc., and Infineon Technologies AG are capitalizing on this growth through strategic alliances and partnerships. APAC is expected to hold the largest market share, experiencing significant incremental growth during the forecast period.

title: Industrial park for Micron-related supply chain companies gets a new name
summary: Onondaga County officials are renaming the industrial park intended for Micron Technology Inc. supply chain companies from "White Pine South" to "White Pine Science & Technology Park." This change aims to clarify the specialized tenants they seek, especially after marketing efforts in Taiwan. The county has purchased 105 acres for this purpose and is receiving $325,000 from New York for development.

title: Micron Becomes First Brand to Produce 200+ Layer QLC NAND SSDs for Client and Data Centers
summary: Micron Technology announced that its 232-layer QLC NAND is now in mass production and shipping in select Crucial SSDs, as well as being sampled to OEM PC manufacturers in the Micron 2500 NVMe SSD. This makes Micron the first brand to produce 200+ layer QLC NAND SSDs for both client and data center applications, offering significant improvements in bit-density, I/O speeds, and read/programming performance. The Micron 2500 client SSD is highlighted for its ability to enhance the PC user experience and accelerate QLC adoption with its superior performance and endurance.

title: Statement by Acting Secretary Su on announcement of construction of new semiconductor fabrication sites in New York, Idaho
summary: Acting Secretary Julie Su issued a statement announcing Micron Technology Inc.'s plans to build three new semiconductor fabrication facilities in New York and Idaho, expected to create over 70,000 jobs. The announcement highlights the Biden-Harris administration's "made in America" economic strategy through the CHIPS and Science Act, emphasizing Micron's commitment to pro-worker practices, including Project Labor Agreements, respecting workers' right to organize, providing childcare, and expanding Registered Apprenticeship programs. Micron is also joining the Department of Labor’s Advanced Manufacturing Sector Table to ensure a diverse and job-ready manufacturing workforce.

title: State unveils new initiative: Mohawk Valley Empowers
summary: Governor Kathy Hochul has announced "Mohawk Valley Empowers," a three-year, $10 million initiative aimed at strengthening Central New York's workforce, particularly in semiconductor manufacturing. The program partners with nine colleges, BOCES schools, and 22 non-profit organizations to create accessible pathways to jobs and develop a robust talent pipeline. SUNY Poly received a $4 million grant for its Semiconductor Processing to Packaging Research, Education and Training Center, which seeks to increase graduates in advanced manufacturing disciplines and foster collaboration with industry leaders.

title: Jaguar Health, Inc. (JAGX) Surges 30.31% Amid High Volume: Technical and Fundamental Analysis
summary: Jaguar Health, Inc. (JAGX) saw its stock surge by 30.31% to $0.29 with high trading volume, indicating strong investor interest but also overbought conditions with an RSI of 85. Despite bullish technicals showing the price above moving averages, fundamental analysis reveals significant losses with negative EPS and PE ratio. The article concludes with a "Hold" recommendation due to high volatility, speculative nature, and fundamental weaknesses, advising investors to monitor for improvement in financials, especially after the upcoming earnings announcement.

title: Super Micro suffers drop following outlook, AI-related stocks follow suit: update
summary: Super Micro Computer (SMCI) experienced a 15% drop in mid-afternoon trading, dragging down other AI and computer hardware-related stocks. Nvidia (NVDA), Broadcom (AVGO), and Micro Technology (MU) also saw declines. This slump followed an updated outlook for Super Micro, impacting the broader AI sector.

title: Micron’s stock is among the S&P 500’s best this year. Why it just got upgraded.
summary: Baird analyst Tristan Gerra upgraded Micron Technology Inc. (MU) stock to a top pick in the semiconductor sector, despite its 34% year-to-date surge. Gerra acknowledged the upgrade was "a bit late" but noted a recent 10% pullback from its April record high, indicating meaningful upside opportunities. Micron's performance makes it one of the S&P 500's best stocks this year.

title: ST rolls a new FD-SOI microcontroller with phase-change memory (PCM) for automotive applications
summary: STMicroelectronics has announced a new generation of automotive microcontrollers featuring embedded phase-change memory (ePCM), fabricated by Samsung using a jointly developed 18nm FD-SOI process. These microcontrollers target the automotive industry with its stringent temperature requirements, addressing a historical challenge for PCM technology. ST plans to sample these devices to automotive customers in the second half of 2024, with production slated for the latter half of 2025.

title: Robert Dennard, Inventor of Computer DRAM at IBM, Dies at 91
summary: Robert Dennard, the IBM engineer credited with inventing dynamic random access memory (DRAM) in the late 1960s, has passed away at the age of 91. His groundbreaking invention was crucial in the development of personal computers, mobile phones, tablets, and video game players. Dennard's death was announced on April 23, according to a notice in the Journal News.

title: Nvidia, Micron and Super Micro among companies that shine in Wall Street’s ‘beat-and-raise’ game
summary: This article highlights companies like Nvidia, Micron, and Super Micro that demonstrate a "beat-and-raise" pattern, leading to rising stock prices. The analysis focuses on companies whose calendar 2025 earnings-per-share estimates have seen significant increases, indicating strong future profit expectations. These upward revisions in earnings estimates are crucial for supporting sustained stock price growth.

title: Lam Research tapping India for semiconductor wafer fabrication equipment components, says senior executive
summary: Lam Research, a US-headquartered company specializing in semiconductor manufacturing equipment, is expanding its global supply chain to India for components like precision metal, plastic machined parts, and PCBAs. This move aligns with India's efforts to build a comprehensive semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem, supported by government schemes and investments. While Lam Research is sourcing components from India, it is not currently planning to assemble semiconductor wafer equipment there.

title: Microchip companies need federal grant money. They’re rolling out child care to get it.
summary: Companies seeking federal grants from the CHIPS and Science Act are implementing child care plans to meet grant requirements, aiming to draw more women into the semiconductor and construction industries. While large corporations like Micron and Intel are offering various child care solutions, experts warn that without careful implementation, these initiatives could worsen the existing child care crisis by not adequately increasing supply. States like Oregon and New York are developing broader strategies, such as creating dedicated funds or investing in community-based care, to ensure the new demand for child care is met sustainably.

title: Groupon stock is rising: Activists who called the move say it’s just the beginning
summary: Groupon (GRPN) stock has recently seen a significant 60% increase, driven by activist investors like Pale Fire Capital and Windward who believe the company is poised for a major turnaround. These investors, having taken control of the board and installed a new CEO, point to improved fundamentals, including the first year-over-year revenue growth in eight years, significant cost cutting leading to positive EBITDA, and a strong cash position. They anticipate further growth from new initiatives, asset sales, and potential share buybacks, projecting a market cap of approximately $2 billion and a stock price of around $55.

title: Prioritizing partnerships pays off for Purdue
summary: Purdue University is experiencing significant success through strategic collaborations, particularly in the semiconductor industry. These partnerships, including a nearly $4 billion investment from SK hynix and agreements with Dassault Systèmes and Lam Research, are leading to major advancements in AI and semiconductor technology, workforce development, and global research. The university's efforts aim to bolster the U.S. semiconductor ecosystem and secure its position as a leader in critical technology sectors.

title: NSF partners with Micron and GlobalFoundries to invest in semiconductor workforce development at minority-serving institutions
