summary: The U.S. National Science Foundation (NSF) announced a new partnership with Micron Technology, Inc., and GlobalFoundries (GF) to significantly invest in semiconductor workforce development. This collaboration aims to increase access to equitable education and cultivate a diverse, semiconductor-ready STEM workforce at minority-serving institutions (MSIs), including HBCUs, Hispanic-serving institutions, and Asian American and Native American Pacific Islander-serving Institutions. The initiative seeks to diversify the semiconductor industry and build robust talent pathways through education and career reentry programs, addressing critical needs in the growing U.S. semiconductor sector.

title: GlobalFoundries Partners with Micron and U.S. National Science Foundation to Drive Semiconductor Workforce Development at Minority Serving Institutions
summary: GlobalFoundries announced a partnership with Micron Technology and the U.S. National Science Foundation to invest in semiconductor workforce development at Minority Serving Institutions. This initiative aims to diversify the semiconductor workforce, build talent pathways through education and career reentry, and align with federal and state legislation like the CHIPS and Science Act. The collaboration seeks to address the growing need for a robust and diverse talent pipeline in the U.S. semiconductor industry.

title: Nvidia Earnings Surge Pulls Up Nasdaq, Chip ETFs, and Others in AI Ecosystem
summary: Nvidia's shares significantly rose after reporting better-than-expected earnings, positively impacting the Nasdaq 100, several semiconductor ETFs, and other companies within its AI ecosystem. The chipmaker's increased market capitalization led to jumps in funds heavily invested in Nvidia and boosted related companies like Dell Technologies, Micron Technology, and Marvell Technology. This surge reflects Nvidia's continued dominance and influence in the AI and semiconductor industries.

title: Micron hit with $445 million US verdict in Netlist patent trial
summary: A U.S. jury has ordered chipmaker Micron Technology to pay Netlist $445 million for infringing on Netlist's patents related to memory-module technology for high-performance computing. The jury found that Micron willfully infringed the patents, which could lead to a tripling of damages. This verdict follows a previous win for Netlist against Samsung in a related patent dispute.

title: More Than Half Of HDDs Could Use HAMR By 2027
summary: Toshiba announced it will ship HAMR HDDs over 30TB in 2025, following Seagate's anticipated volume production in late 2024. The article predicts that by 2026-2027, all three major HDD manufacturers will be producing HAMR drives in volume, potentially seeing over half of all shipped HDDs utilize HAMR technology to maintain a lower cost per TB compared to SSDs for nearline storage. Western Digital's strategy for HAMR adoption is also discussed, suggesting they might follow rapidly due to shared component suppliers.

title: STMicroelectronics receives $2.2B to build the world’s first silicon carbide factory
summary: STMicroelectronics has secured a €2 billion ($2.2 billion) grant from the Italian government to establish a €5 billion ($5.4 billion) chip production facility in Sicily. This factory is slated to become the world's first fully integrated silicon carbide (SiC) semiconductor factory, designed to produce microchips primarily for electric vehicles. The investment is part of the EU's broader strategy to bolster domestic chip manufacturing and reduce reliance on single suppliers, with STMicroelectronics expecting the facility to begin production by 2026 and reach full capacity with 15,000 SiC wafers per week in seven years.

title: TSMC-backed VIS joint venture confirms construction of S$10.5 billion chip plant in S'pore
summary: Vanguard International Semiconductor (VIS) and NXP Semiconductors NV are establishing a S$10.5 billion chip plant in Singapore, a joint venture that will begin construction in late 2024 and production in 2027. While not producing cutting-edge chips, the facility will address high demand for automotive and industrial chips, contributing to supply chain diversification and creating 1,500 jobs. Singapore was chosen due to its skilled workforce and previous successful collaborations between the companies.

title: Nand Flash Market size is set to grow by USD 18.8 billion from 2024-2028, Rising demand for nand flash in automotive industry boost the market, Technavio
summary: The global NAND flash market is projected to grow by USD 18.8 billion from 2024-2028, driven by rising demand in the automotive industry and the proliferation of cloud computing services. Technavio reports a CAGR of 5.02% during this period, despite challenges like demand-supply fluctuations. Key players include Samsung, Micron, and Kioxia, with APAC expected to contribute 60% of the market growth.

title: See the staggering amounts of steel and concrete going into Micron’s new Boise plant
summary: Micron is halfway through constructing its $15 billion memory-chip manufacturing plant in Boise, with completion expected in late 2025 and production starting in 2026. The project involves massive amounts of steel and concrete, with over 8,000 tons of steel already delivered and the site excavated using 2 million pounds of explosives. Once operational, the 600,000-square-foot facility will create 2,000 direct jobs and 15,000 indirect jobs, significantly boosting local employment and bringing DRAM manufacturing back to Boise, supported by $6.1 billion in federal subsidies.

title: ‘Record demonstration’: Dark horse in quantum race passes new milestone
summary: Diraq, an Australian quantum startup, has achieved a significant milestone with 99.9% control accuracy for a quantum bit, a precision level needed for error-corrected quantum computing. The company is partnering with GlobalFoundries Inc and IMEC to manufacture its compact, silicon-based quantum computer chip. Diraq aims to produce affordable and compact quantum computers, positioning its technology as a competitive alternative to larger, more energy-intensive systems developed by rivals like PsiQuantum.

title: Nova nears record $7b market cap
summary: Nova Ltd. is approaching a $7 billion market cap, with its share price reaching an all-time high of $238, a 73.2% increase this year. The company, which produces metrology devices for semiconductor manufacturing, is benefiting from the strong demand in the chip industry driven by AI applications. Bank of America has recommended Nova, forecasting significant growth due to its exposure to AI and advanced packaging.

title: Micron’s stock gets a target boost ahead of earnings: ‘Don’t overthink it’
summary: Ahead of Micron Technology Inc.'s upcoming earnings report, Cantor Fitzgerald analyst C.J. Muse has raised his price target for MU stock, predicting a 27% upside despite concerns over an earthquake impact and the absence of a positive pre-announcement. Muse advises investors to "Don't overthink it," suggesting confidence in the company's performance, likely benefiting from AI and pricing improvements. Micron shares have already seen a significant 66% surge this year.

title: SK Hynix patents-owned Mimir IP files complaint against Micron
summary: Mimir IP, a patent management company that acquired 1,500 chip-related patents from SK Hynix, has filed a complaint against Micron Technology Inc. for patent infringement. The lawsuit, also targeting Tesla, Dell, HP, and Lenovo, was filed in the US District Court for the Eastern District of Texas and the US International Trade Commission. This case highlights a growing trend of patent infringement disputes involving non-practicing entities (NPEs) in the competitive chip industry, with potential damages reaching $480 million.

title: Micron Stock Soars With the AI Boom—Is It a Buy or a Sell?
summary: Micron Technology's stock has surged due to a recovering semiconductor memory market and the AI boom, but Morningstar analysts warn it's overvalued given the cyclical nature of its business. While AI provides a growth vector, sustained high growth and moderated cyclicality are not seen as reasonable expectations. The stock is rated 1-star with a fair value estimate of $80 due to high cyclicality and lack of an economic moat.

title: To address talent shortage, chipmakers skilling engineering students
summary: Semiconductor companies in India, including Intel India, AMD, Applied Materials, and Tessolve, are actively addressing the industry's talent shortage by partnering with engineering students and academic institutions. They are establishing labs, driving industry-academia collaborations for research in core design and AI training, and sponsoring student research. These initiatives aim to develop fresh talent, build a skilled workforce, and improve diversity within the rapidly growing Indian semiconductor sector, which is projected to more than double by 2026.

title: Silvaco Reports First Quarter 2024 Financial Results
summary: Silvaco Group, Inc. reported strong first-quarter 2024 financial results with revenue up 27% sequentially and 11% year-over-year to $15.9 million. The company, which recently completed its IPO, highlighted robust demand for its TCAD and EDA products, excellent gross margins of 88%, and provided a positive outlook for Q2 and full-year 2024. Key business highlights included the introduction of an AI-based platform for digital twin modeling and an expanded partnership with Micron Technology.

title: Micron Has to Resolve a Bat Problem Before Building Syracuse Chip Fab
summary: Micron Technology Inc. faces delays in starting construction on its multibillion-dollar chipmaking factory in Syracuse, New York. One significant hurdle is the presence of endangered bats on the proposed site, requiring resolution before building can commence. The article highlights this ecological issue as a key factor slowing down the project.

title: An update on Micron's Boise expansion
summary: Micron's $15 billion computer chip fabrication plant in Southeast Boise is undergoing significant construction with 11 cranes and hundreds of workers. BoiseDev.com recently flew a drone over the site to provide an update on the expansion. Don Day, founder and editor of BoiseDev.com, joined Idaho Matters to discuss the progress of this massive project which includes on-site concrete production and rock crushing.

title: Tata Electronics Inks MoU With Synopsys For Its Fab Plant In Gujarat’s Dholera
summary: Tata Electronics has signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with US-based Synopsys to accelerate wafer production at its semiconductor fabrication plant in Dholera, Gujarat. This collaboration will help develop a foundry design platform for chip development and establish factory automation and other essential processes. The partnership aligns with Tata Group's significant investment of INR 91,000 Cr in the Dholera fab plant, aiming to produce 50,000 wafers per month for various sectors.

title: Biden awards up to $75 million in CHIPS Act grant to Entegris
summary: The Biden administration has awarded up to $75 million in CHIPS Act funding to Entegris to support the development of a new manufacturing facility in Colorado Springs, Colorado. This grant is part of the U.S. effort to boost domestic semiconductor production and bolster suppliers. The facility will produce essential components for chip manufacturing, including liquid filter membranes and specialized containers, primarily for major chipmakers.

title: Micron Technology, Inc. Reports Results for the Third Quarter of Fiscal 2024
summary: Micron Technology announced strong financial results for its third quarter of fiscal 2024, driven by robust AI demand and High Bandwidth Memory (HBM) sales. The company reported significant increases in revenue, net income, and operating cash flow compared to the prior quarter and the same period last year. Micron anticipates continued growth, projecting a substantial revenue record in fiscal 2025 due to expanding AI-driven opportunities.

title: Micron tumbles as AI revenue surge falls short of lofty expectations
summary: Micron's shares fell 5% after its quarterly revenue forecast, though a significant increase, failed to meet the lofty expectations set by the AI boom and its recent stock surge. Despite the market's initial disappointment, some analysts view the pullback as a buying opportunity, citing improving market conditions and Micron's strong position in the high-bandwidth memory (HBM) market. The reaction highlights the high expectations placed on companies in the AI ecosystem.

title: South Korea sets sights on fostering EDA tech to win HBM chip war
summary: South Korea is aiming to develop its Electronic Design Automation (EDA) technology to maintain its lead in the high-bandwidth memory (HBM) chip market, which is crucial for AI chip manufacturing. While US companies currently dominate the EDA market, Chinese firms are increasing their presence in Korea, prompting the South Korean government to acknowledge the importance of nurturing a domestic EDA ecosystem. The country faces challenges in talent retention, as engineers leave due to a lack of a robust local EDA industry.

title: 3 Data Storage Stocks Poised to Profit From the AI Boom
summary: The article highlights three data storage stocks—Micron (MU), Western Digital (WDC), and Seagate Technologies (STX)—that are well-positioned to benefit from the ongoing artificial intelligence (AI) boom. These companies provide essential components like memory chips and storage solutions for AI data centers. Each company is innovating in its respective field, with Micron focusing on high-performing memory, Western Digital preparing for a value-unlocking spin-off, and Seagate advancing storage capacity with new technology.

title: Semiconductor Market Size Set to Grow by $112.8 billion from 2024-2028
summary: The global semiconductor market is projected to grow by $112.8 billion from 2024-2028, driven primarily by the increasing adoption of IoT devices and other advanced technologies. Key market players are investing in sustainability initiatives to meet growing demand, although the industry faces challenges related to supply-demand gaps and the high cost of R&D. The market is expected to continue its growth trajectory, spurred by demand from AI, ML, IoT, and the automotive sector.

title: Applied Materials reveals chip wiring innovations for energy-efficient computing
summary: Applied Materials has introduced new chip wiring innovations designed to enhance energy efficiency in computing, particularly for 2-nanometer node manufacturing and beyond. These advancements utilize new materials like Ruthenium Cobalt liners and enhanced low-k dielectrics, which reduce electrical resistance by up to 25% and capacitance by up to 3%, ultimately supporting the development of trillion-transistor chips for AI and graphics processing. The company's solutions aim to improve performance-per-watt, strengthen chips for 3D stacking, and address challenges in scaling Moore's Law, with adoption already seen by leading logic and memory chipmakers including Samsung and TSMC.

title: Sewers for Micron and expected population growth in Onondaga County could cost $1 billion
summary: Onondaga County plans to invest up to $1 billion in an expanded wastewater treatment system to support Micron Technology's chipmaking complex in Clay and the anticipated population growth. This significant investment will be split between an industrial wastewater system for Micron, which the company will ultimately fund, and an expanded municipal system for community development. The project includes transforming the Oak Orchard treatment plant and laying new sewer lines, aiming to accommodate both industrial and residential needs.

title: U.S. Navy Funds Mercury to Advance Chip-Scale Technologies
summary: The U.S. Navy has awarded Mercury Systems, Inc. a $13.2 million contract to advance chip-scale technologies for radar and electronic warfare systems. This initiative, part of the Office of Naval Research’s ORCA program, aims to reduce development timelines by increasing the modularity of components at the chip level. Mercury will develop a next-generation RF System-in-Package, integrating commercial chips from major semiconductor providers to create smaller and lighter solutions.

title: Optimism abounds as semiconductor industry takes aim at AI-fueled trillion-dollar market
summary: The semiconductor industry is poised for significant growth, aiming for a trillion-dollar market, driven by the widespread adoption of artificial intelligence. This expansion is further fueled by substantial U.S. government investments through the CHIPS Act, which is boosting domestic chip production and attracting new talent. Industry leaders also emphasize the importance of addressing sustainability and exploring advanced computing areas like quantum computing amidst this AI-driven boom.

title: Lai thanks Micron CEO for his trust in Taiwan
summary: President William Lai of Taiwan thanked Micron Technology CEO Sanjay Mehrotra for the company's continued investment and trust in Taiwan, particularly for its advancements in DRAM process technology and contributions to the semiconductor industry. This public meeting, considered rare for a Taiwanese president with a foreign tech executive, highlighted Taiwan's commitment to developing its semiconductor industry and becoming an "AI island." Micron is a key provider of high-bandwidth memory chips crucial for advanced AI systems.

title: BTCZ Stock Price | T-Rex 2X Inverse Bitcoin Daily Target ETF
summary: This article provides details on the T-Rex 2X Inverse Bitcoin Daily Target ETF (BTCZ), including its current stock price of 5.28, key statistics, and performance metrics. It lists the ETF's trading on the NYSE and outlines its investment strategy, which involves leveraging derivatives to implement a short strategy in Bitcoin. Additionally, it offers historical data, dividend information, and technical analysis perspectives for potential investors.

title: [News] China Contributes Over 40% of U.S. Chip Equipment Makers’ Revenue despite Export Controls
summary: Despite tightening U.S. export controls on the semiconductor sector, American chip equipment manufacturers like Applied Materials and KLA Corporation are becoming increasingly reliant on the Chinese market, with China accounting for over 40% of their sales. This trend, highlighted by reports from Nikkei and SEMI, indicates a growing dependence on China for non-advanced chip manufacturing equipment, even as the U.S. aims to curb Beijing's technological progress. China, in response, is significantly investing in its domestic semiconductor industry to counter these sanctions.

title: New York to launch application process for $500 million green chips community fund
summary: New York State is launching an online portal on August 15th for organizations to apply for grants from a $500 million Green CHIPS Community Investment Fund. This fund aims to benefit communities surrounding Micron Technology Inc.'s planned semiconductor megafab in Clay, with grants supporting workforce development, education, community development, and housing. The guidelines for the grants were approved by Empire State Development, with contributions from ESD, Micron, and private philanthropic sources.

title: Check out OneStream's stock price (OS) in real time
summary: OneStream Inc. (OS) stock closed at $17.74, down 1.77%, with its 52-week range between $16.51 and $31.61. The company, which provides an AI-enabled Digital Finance Cloud platform, has a market cap of $4.325 billion. Recent news includes analyst calls and a CFO transition plan.

title: DDR5 vs DDR4 DRAM - All the Advantages & Design Challenges
summary: This article details the advantages of DDR5 DRAM over DDR4, outlining seven significant specification advances such as increased bandwidth (up to 8.4 GT/s), lower operating voltage, and a new power architecture. It also discusses the design challenges introduced by DDR5's higher speeds and lower voltages, focusing on signal integrity, power delivery, and thermal management. The article concludes by highlighting how DDR5 memory interface chipsets, like those from Rambus, help address these challenges to harness the full potential of DDR5.

title: Rambus Expands Industry-Leading Memory Interface Chip Offering to High-Performance PCs with DDR5 Client Clock Driver
summary: Rambus has launched its DDR5 Client Clock Driver (CKD) for next-generation high-performance desktops and notebooks, expanding its memory interface chip offerings to the client market. This new CKD enables client DIMMs (CSODIMMs and CUDIMMs) to achieve data rates up to 7200 MT/s, delivering enhanced performance for gaming, content creation, and AI in PCs. Rambus leverages its 30 years of memory systems expertise to provide superior signal and power integrity for these advanced DDR5 solutions.

title: Marvell Introduces Breakthrough Structera CXL Product Line to Address Server Memory Bandwidth and Capacity Challenges in Cloud Data Centers
summary: Marvell Technology, Inc. has launched its new Structera product line of Compute Express Link (CXL) devices to tackle memory performance and scaling issues in cloud data centers. The line includes Structera A CXL near-memory accelerators, optimized for high-bandwidth applications like deep learning with integrated Arm Neoverse processors, and Structera X CXL memory-expansion controllers, designed to add terabytes of memory for high-capacity applications and enable recycling of DDR4 DIMMs. These products are the first to support four memory channels, integrate inline compression, and are manufactured using a 5nm process, aiming to improve efficiency, reduce e-waste, and lower operational costs for cloud operators.

title: Lam Research Introduces Lam Cryo 3.0 Cryogenic Etch Technology to Accelerate Scaling of 3D NAND
summary: Lam Research introduced Lam Cryo 3.0, its third-generation cryogenic dielectric etch technology, designed to accelerate the scaling of 3D NAND flash memory. This technology uses ultra-cold temperatures, high-power confined plasma, and surface chemistry innovations to achieve industry-leading precision and profile control for manufacturing future leading-edge 3D NAND, including enabling 1,000-layer devices. Lam Cryo 3.0 offers significant improvements in etch rate, sustainability, and compatibility with existing equipment while supporting the increasing demand for high-capacity memory driven by AI.

title: Xtrackers Artificial Intelligence and Big Data ETF Holdings
summary: The article details the holdings of the Xtrackers Artificial Intelligence and Big Data ETF (XAIX), which was established on August 2, 2024. The ETF focuses on theme equity with an emphasis on artificial intelligence and big data, holding 92 positions and managing 111.51 million in assets under management. Key holdings include tech giants like Samsung Electronics, Micron Technology, Alibaba, and Meta Platforms.

title: Lam provides a path toward 1,000-layer 3D NAND flash
summary: Lam Research Corp. has introduced its third-generation cryogenic dielectric etch technology, Cryo 3.0, designed to enable the manufacturing of 1,000-layer 3D NAND flash memory. This technology uses cold temperatures, high power confined plasma, and advanced surface chemistry to achieve precise etching for high aspect ratio memory channels. Cryo 3.0 offers significant improvements in etch rate, energy consumption, emissions, and wafer-to-wafer repeatability, addressing key challenges in next-generation NAND production for the AI era.

title: Xtrackers Artificial Intelligence and Big Data ETF
summary: This article provides a comprehensive profile of the Xtrackers Artificial Intelligence and Big Data ETF (XAIX), including its stock quote, holdings, expenses, performance, and ESG scores. It details the ETF's investment strategy, top holdings (such as Samsung, Micron, NVIDIA, and Alphabet), and various analytical data points for financial professionals. The fund tracks the Nasdaq Global AI and Big Data Index and offers exposure to the technology sector, particularly in robotics and AI.

title: Western Digital flash spinoff valuation could mirror Solidigm’s
summary: Western Digital is spinning off its NAND and SSD business, which Wedbush analyst Matt Bryson suggests could be valued similarly to Solidigm. Bryson's analysis highlights that the market currently undervalues Western Digital's NAND business, with its HDD operations comprising nearly the entire company valuation. He estimates the combined value of the separated SanDisk and WD entities could be at least $30 billion, potentially reaching over $40 billion if NAND and HDDs enter a prolonged up cycle.

title: Semiconductor Manufacturing Equipment Market on the Rise: Expected Demand of USD 182.30 Billion by 2030
summary: The Semiconductor Manufacturing Equipment Market is projected to nearly double from USD 91.20 billion in 2023 to USD 182.30 billion by 2030, exhibiting a CAGR of 10.4%. This growth is driven by increasing demand for high-performance semiconductors, advancements in technologies like 5G and AI, and the adoption of sophisticated manufacturing processes such as EUV lithography and 3D ICs. The Asia Pacific region is identified as a key driver of this market due to significant investments and expanding production capabilities.

title: SEMI: Global Chip Manufacturing Industry Strengthens in 2Q 2024
summary: The global semiconductor manufacturing industry showed significant improvement in Q2 2024, driven by strong demand for AI chips and high bandwidth memory (HBM). This led to substantial growth in IC sales, stabilization of capital expenditure, and an increase in installed wafer fab capacity. While electronics sales saw a slight dip in the first half of the year, they are projected to rebound strongly, with IC sales expected to surpass record levels due to AI demand.

title: Top 10 OSAT (Outsourced Semiconductor Assembly and Test) Companies in 2024
summary: This article identifies and details the top 10 Outsourced Semiconductor Assembly and Test (OSAT) companies globally in 2024, highlighting their market share, headquarters, key services, and recent developments. It emphasizes their crucial role in the semiconductor supply chain, providing essential services like assembly, packaging, and testing of semiconductor devices. The leading companies, including ASE Technology Holding Co., Ltd. and Amkor Technology, Inc., are driving innovation in advanced packaging and testing to meet the evolving demands of the electronics industry.

title: US to award HP $50 mln for semiconductor tech project
summary: The U.S. Commerce Department plans to award $50 million to HP to expand and modernize an Oregon facility, boosting semiconductor technologies crucial for life sciences and AI applications. This funding is part of a larger $39 billion subsidy program for U.S. semiconductor manufacturing approved by Congress in 2022. The project specifically leverages HP's expertise in microfluidics and microelectromechanical systems to produce silicon devices vital for drug discovery and other life sciences research.

title: Micron eyeing two AUO-owned plants, reports say
summary: Micron Technology Inc. is reportedly looking to acquire two plants from flat-panel maker AUO Corp. in Tainan for NT$10 billion to NT$20 billion. These facilities would expand Micron's advanced chip packaging, testing, and high-bandwidth memory production capabilities. This news follows TSMC's recent acquisition of a plant and equipment from Innolux Corp., another flat-panel maker, to boost its CoWoS packaging capacity for AI chips.

title: CXL memory drives the future of data centers with low-latency solutions
summary: CXL memory is emerging as a critical solution for modern data centers, offering high speed and low latency to boost data throughput and scalability for applications like generative AI and autonomous driving. Industry leaders from Micron Technology, Supermicro, Intel, and MemVerge discussed the transformative impact of CXL on data center architectures, highlighting its ability to leverage existing PCIe interfaces for lower latency and its potential to repurpose older memory technologies like DDR4. Supermicro is playing a key role in CXL adoption by integrating it into its latest platforms, supporting up to eight CXL memory modules to expand memory capacity efficiently.

title: [News] 300mm Wafer Fab Construction Picks up Steam Worldwide
summary: The global 12-inch wafer production is accelerating due to rising demand for high-performance computing, HBM, CoWoS packaging, and high-performance storage in the AI market, prompting major companies like TSMC, Intel, and UMC to expand their fabrication facilities worldwide. Several companies, including VIS, TSMC, UMC, Toshiba, Powerchip, Texas Instruments, Intel, GlobalFoundries, and various Chinese firms, are investing heavily in new 12-inch wafer fab construction, with many projects expected to begin production between 2024 and 2029, significantly boosting global semiconductor manufacturing capacity.

title: Rambus unveils industry-first HBM4 controller IP, ready to super-speed next-gen AI workloads
summary: Rambus has introduced the industry's first HBM4 controller IP, designed to accelerate next-generation AI workloads. This new IP supports JEDEC specifications of 6.4Gbps and operations up to 10Gbps, offering a throughput of 2.56TB/sec to each memory device. The solution aims to address memory bandwidth and capacity bottlenecks crucial for advanced AI training and inference.

title: NXP Semiconductors to invest over $1 bn in India as it boosts R&D efforts
summary: NXP Semiconductors announced plans to invest over $1 billion in India, doubling its R&D efforts in the country, as global chipmakers increase their presence in the nation. This move aligns with India's economic strategy to become an electronics powerhouse, supported by a $10 billion incentive package. The investment underscores India's growing importance in the global semiconductor ecosystem as it aims to reduce reliance on other manufacturing hubs.

title: A death cross looms for Micron’s stock, but this analyst says slower growth is a ‘temporary blip’
summary: Micron Technology Inc. is facing a looming "death cross" technical signal, which typically indicates a sustained bearish trend. Despite this, a Raymond James analyst views the recent slowdown in volume growth for Micron's stock as a temporary issue. The analyst has, however, cut the stock price target and trimmed earnings estimates.

title: ETRI succeeds in the development of a p-type semiconductor material to lead semiconductor innovation
summary: The Electronics and Telecommunications Research Institute (ETRI) has successfully developed new p-type semiconductor materials and thin-film transistors, overcoming limitations of existing p-type semiconductors like low mobility. This innovation, using a chalcogenide-based Se-Te alloy, is expected to enhance next-generation displays with higher refresh rates and lower power consumption, and advance 3D integration methods in semiconductors. The research has been published in "ACS Applied Materials & Interfaces" and is poised to impact OLED TVs, XR devices, and other semiconductor applications.

title: Micron’s stock drops as first ‘death cross’ in 2 years appears
summary: Micron Technology Inc.'s stock dropped due to a "death cross" chart pattern, signaling potential accelerating losses, and a downbeat outlook from Morgan Stanley. Morgan Stanley analysts believe earnings-growth expectations for the memory-chip market will peak and reverse, potentially leading to a significant contraction in sector valuation. The last death cross for Micron occurred in April 2022, after which the stock fell another 30% before bottoming out five months later.

title: A Look at Micron Stock Before Earnings
summary: Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) is scheduled to report fiscal fourth-quarter financial results on September 25, with options trading indicating a potential 13% move. Despite a recent double downgrade and a cut in its price target by Wells Fargo, the stock has shown pullbacks since June. Short-term players have been call-biased, and MU has seen a modest 2.1% year-to-date gain after prior struggles.

title: Analog Devices, Tata Group in talks to make semiconductors in India
summary: Analog Devices and India's Tata Group are exploring a partnership to manufacture semiconductor products in India. Tata Electronics is investing $14 billion in semiconductor fabrication and assembly facilities in Gujarat and Assam, where ADI's products could be manufactured. This collaboration aligns with Prime Minister Narendra Modi's goal to establish India as a global chipmaking hub.

title: Micron hires multinational firm to design chipmaking complex in Central NY
summary: Micron Technology has selected Jacobs Engineering, a global firm, to design its planned $100 billion chipmaking complex in Clay, Central New York. This complex will include four fabrication plants and is expected to create 9,000 jobs over 20 years, plus many more support positions. The project is a significant economic development for the region, though some aspects, like its tax deal, have drawn commentary.

title: Analog Devices, Tata in talks over partnering in Indian fab
summary: Analog Devices Inc. and Tata Group are exploring a strategic alliance for chip manufacturing cooperation in India. The collaboration involves Tata Electronics, Tata Motors, and Tejas Networks, focusing on semiconductor manufacturing in Gujarat and Assam, and the use of ADI products in Tata's electric vehicles and networking infrastructure. ADI aims to explore manufacturing its products in Tata's upcoming fab in Gujarat and packaging facility in Assam.

title: 4 Stocks to Buy as the Fed Cuts Interest Rates
summary: Morningstar strategists discuss the Federal Reserve's recent interest rate cut and their projections for future cuts, noting a divergence in opinion between the Fed's dot plot and market expectations. The article then highlights four undervalued stocks—Healthpeak Properties, Crown Castle, U.S. Bancorp, and Evergy—that Morningstar analysts recommend as buys in a declining interest-rate environment. These picks are based on their valuation, dividend yields, and long-term competitive advantages.

title: EXCLUSIVE: Invesco Smiles On South Korea, Indonesia
summary: Fiona Yang, co-manager of the Invesco Asia Trust, is optimistic about South Korea and Indonesia, viewing them as undervalued markets with strong potential. She highlights South Korea's undervaluation despite corporate governance reforms and Indonesia's good fundamentals, particularly in the banking sector. While the trust maintains China as its top market, Yang has reduced exposure to India due to expensive valuations.

title: Micron (MU) Q4 earnings beat Street view; revenue nearly doubles
summary: Micron Technology Inc. reported strong fourth-quarter 2024 results, returning to profit after a loss the previous year. The company's revenues nearly doubled, soaring 93% to $7.75 billion, significantly exceeding consensus estimates. Adjusted earnings of $1.18 per share also beat analysts' forecasts.

title: Semiconductor company eyes Mundy Twp. megasite
summary: Mundy Township is reportedly being considered by Western Digital Technologies, a California-based semiconductor company, for a potential megasite development. This project, which could involve an investment of $35 billion to $55 billion, would rely on federal incentives through the CHIPS and Science Act and possibly additional state incentives. The development could create up to 10,000 permanent jobs.

title: Micron rises 13% as strong forecast impresses AI-hungry investors
summary: Micron Technology's shares jumped over 13% after its strong first-quarter revenue forecast indicated high demand and favorable pricing for HBM chips used in AI. The company's forecast and impressive quarterly revenue growth, exceeding Wall Street estimates, suggest a robust market for memory chips, despite earlier concerns about HBM pricing. This positive outlook also influenced other chip firms, though some gains were pared due to news of a U.S. Department of Justice probe into Super Micro Computer.

title: AI Mania in Fine Fettle: ETFs to Invest
summary: The article discusses the resurgence of AI investment fervor on Wall Street, driven by strong performances from companies like Micron and significant investments from tech giants such as Microsoft and Amazon. It highlights how these developments are boosting the AI sector and suggests several AI-focused ETFs, including BOTZ, ROBT, and WISE, for investors looking to capitalize on this trend. The piece also touches upon NVIDIA's fluctuating valuation and its central role in the AI infrastructure.

title: Coherent Corp. (COHR) Unveils 1.6T SiPho Product with NVIDIA Chip, Set to Challenge Marvell and Broadcom by 2025
summary: Coherent Corp. (COHR) has unveiled a new 1.6T SiPho product, enhanced with a 5nm NVIDIA chip, which is expected to go into production by late 2025. This development positions Coherent to potentially challenge competitors like Marvell and Broadcom in the long term by offering advanced optoelectronic components crucial for AI infrastructure. The article highlights the significant investments companies are making in AI infrastructure, including specialized hardware and data centers, to meet the increasing demands of AI-driven applications.

title: SK Group may beef up reform drive after CEO seminar
summary: SK Group is set to hold its annual strategic meeting, presided over by Chairman Chey Tae-won, from October 31 to November 2. This comes as the conglomerate faces challenges like a slowdown in EV adoption and is already implementing significant restructuring efforts, including job cuts at SK On and mergers among subsidiaries. The seminar will review these changes and discuss future strategies amidst AI market expansion and global uncertainties.

title: New law that exempts chip plants from U.S. environmental review won’t affect Micron in Clay
summary: A new federal law exempts chipmaking plants from rigorous U.S. environmental review, but it will not impact Micron Technology's planned facility in Clay, New York. This is because Micron's project still requires a federal permit for wetlands and is subject to New York State's stringent environmental quality review act, which is comparable to federal standards. Micron's $100 billion plant will undergo both state and specific federal reviews regardless of the new legislation.

title: Sify Technologies adopts a 'Grow Wide, Grow Deep' channel strategy
summary: Sify Technologies is implementing a "Grow Wide, Grow Deep" channel strategy, aiming to strengthen engagement with existing partners and expand its reach. The company views partners as a crucial extension of its sales teams, providing them with comprehensive training to drive digital transformation conversations and leverage Sify's innovative solutions. This partner-first approach is central to Sify's growth strategy for 2024-2025 and beyond.

title: Micron's stock builds on gains as AMD event highlights growing importance of memory
summary: Micron Technology Inc. shares rose during AMD's Advancing AI keynote presentation, contrasting with AMD's stock decline. The memory-chip maker, an AMD partner, saw its stock increase by 3.6% as AMD showcased new chips emphasizing high-bandwidth memory for artificial intelligence workloads. This highlights the increasing importance of memory in powering AI.

title: 5 ETFs to Use as Semiconductor Sales Continue to Grow
summary: The semiconductor industry has seen significant growth in 2024, with the S&P 500 Semiconductors Index up over 80%. This surge is largely driven by demand from the artificial intelligence sector, leading to a 20.6% increase in global semiconductor sales year-over-year in August. Investors can leverage this bullish trend through various Direxion ETFs, including the broad-based Direxion Daily Semiconductor Bull and Bear 3X Shares (SOXL) and single-stock ETFs focusing on companies like Nvidia, Taiwan Semiconductor, Micron Technology, and Broadcom.

title: Micron Fuels New Wave of AI PCs With Launch of Ultra-Fast Clock Driver DDR5 Memory Portfolio
summary: Micron Technology has launched a new category of clock driver DDR5 memory, including CUDIMM and CSODIMM modules, which are now shipping in volume. These modules offer speeds up to 6,400 MT/s, more than twice as fast as DDR4, and are designed to enhance stability and performance for AI PCs and high-end workstations. Intel has validated these solutions for its Core Ultra processors, enabling high memory densities of up to 256GB for AI PCs.

title: Patented: Dallas-Based Worlds' Object Tracking in a Virtual 3D Space and More North Texas Inventive Activity
summary: Dallas-based Worlds Inc. has secured a U.S. patent for a system that tracks 2D objects in a virtual 3D space, enhancing operational capabilities across various industries including logistics and manufacturing by creating a virtual 3D environment mirroring real-world movements. This article also highlights other newly granted patents with connections to the Dallas-Fort Worth area for the week of October 1, reflecting the region's strong inventive activity, ranking number 11 nationally with 133 patents granted. The patents cover a diverse range of fields from healthcare and automotive technology to telecommunications and advanced computing.

title: Intel Vs. Micron Technology: Which Chipmaker Holds The Edge?
summary: This article compares Intel and Micron Technology, assessing which chipmaker currently holds an advantage. Intel faces challenges with stock performance, geopolitical scrutiny from China, and the complexity of its partnerships, while analysts have a cautious outlook. Micron, conversely, is experiencing strong growth driven by its focus on next-gen memory technologies crucial for AI and its global expansion efforts, particularly in India, with analysts predicting significant upside.

title: Invesco’s Semiconductor ETF Rallies on Strong TSMC Earnings
summary: Invesco's PHLX Semiconductor ETF (SOXQ) rallied by 2.5% following strong third-quarter results from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC), a top holding in the ETF. TSMC's positive sales outlook and raised annual growth expectations boosted semiconductor stocks. SOXQ has shown significant year-to-date and one-year gains, with substantial asset growth.

title: Peoria unlocks State Trust land to meet growth in semiconductor industry
summary: Peoria leaders have taken steps to unlock about 8,300 acres of State Trust land, annexing and zoning 1,600 acres (North Peoria Gateway) to support the growing semiconductor industry. This move aims to facilitate commercial and residential development along Loop 303, capitalizing on investments by companies like TSMC and Amkor Technology Inc. Additionally, a refined development agreement with Amkor and Vistancia outlines infrastructure improvements to support Amkor's $2 billion semiconductor testing and packaging plant.

title: Should you buy Nvidia stock now? It may be a once-in-a-generation opportunity
summary: Bank of America analysts have reiterated a "buy" rating on Nvidia stock, raising their price target to $190 from $165, suggesting a 38% upside. They view Nvidia as a "generational opportunity" due to the expanding AI market, robust demand for its next-generation AI chips like Blackwell, and strong enterprise partnerships. Nvidia's software ecosystem and potential for over $200 billion in free cash flow over the next two years further support BofA's bullish outlook.

title: Earnings Preview: What To Expect From Micron Technology’s Report
summary: Micron Technology is expected to report significant EPS growth for the current year, driven by increasing demand for its high-performance chips, especially those used in data centers and AI systems. Analyst consensus remains bullish, contributing to a climb in MU stock. Investors are optimistic about Micron's AI-linked future, with the stock maintaining a "Strong Buy" recommendation.

title: Cramer's Lightning Round: Vertex Pharmaceuticals is a buy
summary: Jim Cramer conducted his "Lightning Round" where he offered quick takes on various stocks. He recommended Vertex Pharmaceuticals as a buy, praised CME Group as a strong buy, and suggested buying T. Rowe Price Group. Cramer also advised against selling Micron.

title: US fines GlobalFoundries for shipping chips to sanctioned Chinese firm
summary: The U.S. has fined GlobalFoundries $500,000 for shipping chips worth $17.1 million to SJ Semiconductor, an affiliate of the blacklisted Chinese chipmaker SMIC, without authorization. GlobalFoundries attributed the lapse to a data-entry error and stated its commitment to trade compliance. This action comes amidst heightened U.S. concerns about China's access to sensitive American technology and follows criticism regarding the monitoring of other chipmakers.

title: GMIF2025 Awards Unveiled: Samsung Semiconductor, Sandisk, Silicon Motion, BIWIN, and More Honored!
summary: The Fourth GMIF2025 Innovation Summit concluded, celebrating excellence in storage technologies, solutions, and AI applications with its Annual Awards. Companies like Samsung Semiconductor, Sandisk, and BIWIN were honored across categories including International Leadership, China’s Strength, and Ecosystem & Industrial Innovation, highlighting advancements in AI storage, computing architecture, and independent innovation. The awards recognized significant contributions to digital transformation and China's growing influence in the memory sector.

title: The Zacks Analyst Blog Oracle, Micron Technology, Medtronic and The Cato
summary: This Zacks Analyst Blog highlights top analyst reports for Oracle, Micron Technology, Medtronic, and The Cato Corp. It discusses each company's recent financial performance, market positioning, growth drivers, challenges, and future outlook, based on Zacks Equity Research. The report emphasizes Oracle's cloud growth, Micron's semiconductor demand, Medtronic's medical device expansion, and Cato's financial strength despite sales decline.

title: Micron Introduces World’s Fastest, Most Energy Efficient 60TB SSD
summary: Micron Technology announced qualification for its 6550 ION NVMe SSD, which is the world’s fastest 60TB data center SSD and the industry’s first E3.S and PCIe Gen5 60TB SSD. This new SSD sets a new standard for performance, energy efficiency, endurance, security, and rack density for exascale data center deployments, excelling in high-capacity NVMe workloads. It delivers up to 179% faster sequential reads and 213% higher write bandwidth per watt compared to competing drives, while reducing rack storage needs by up to 67%.

title: GraniteShares new Short and Leveraged Single Stock ETF launches Highlights: 2X Micron, 2X Taiwan Semiconductor and 2X CrowdStrike
summary: GraniteShares, an ETF issuer specializing in high conviction ETFs, has launched three new short and leveraged single-stock ETFs. These ETFs aim to replicate +2X the daily variations of Micron Technology (MU), Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM), and CrowdStrike (CRWD) stocks. This expansion brings GraniteShares' total offering of such ETFs to 19, building on its over $9 billion in AUM and its role as a pioneer in leveraged single-stock ETFs.

title: MULL Stock Fund Price and Chart — NASDAQ:MULL
summary: The MULL Stock Fund, or GraniteShares 2x Long MU Daily ETF, aims to deliver twice the daily price return of MU stock, less fees and expenses. It is a leveraged ETF designed for short-term tactical use, offering amplified exposure but also increased volatility due to its lack of diversification. The fund was launched on November 11, 2024, has assets under management of $239.11 million, and an expense ratio of 1.50%.

title: Applied Materials Announces New Collaboration Model for Advanced Packaging at Summit on Energy-Efficient Computing
summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) has announced an expansion of its global EPIC innovation platform to accelerate the commercialization of advanced chip packaging technologies, specifically targeting energy-efficient computing for AI. The company convened over two dozen semiconductor R&D leaders in Singapore to foster collaboration across the industry. This initiative, EPIC Advanced Packaging, aims to address challenges in developing next-generation packaging technologies by facilitating co-innovation and providing early access to new equipment and expertise.

title: PR News | Avoq Works DC for Micron Technology - Tue., Nov. 19, 2024
summary: Avoq has been hired by Micron Technology to lobby on issues related to investment tax credits for semiconductors and US manufacturing competitiveness, especially in light of the Biden administration's CHIPS and Science Act funding. Micron plans to invest $50 billion to boost US output through 2030, with expansion in Boise and a new manufacturing complex in Clay, NY. The lobbying effort comes as the political landscape shifts regarding tech sector support and international investments.

title: Axos Clearing announces partnership with Envestnet
summary: Axos Clearing, a subsidiary of Axos Financial Inc, has announced a strategic partnership with Envestnet to integrate Envestnet’s managed account solutions into its Axos Complete portal. This collaboration aims to provide hybrid broker-dealers and registered investment advisers with enhanced customer service by streamlining portfolio management, automating tasks, and offering a wider range of investment options. The partnership is a key step in developing Axos Complete as a comprehensive platform for advisory and operational solutions.

title: US Preps China Chip Curbs That Stop Short of Early Proposals
summary: The Biden administration is preparing to introduce new curbs on sales of semiconductor equipment and AI memory chips to China, a move that would escalate US efforts to limit Beijing's technological ambitions. These forthcoming restrictions are less stringent than some earlier proposals and follow extensive deliberations and lobbying by US chip equipment manufacturers. The rules could impact companies like Huawei, SMIC, Micron, Samsung, and SK Hynix.

title: ASML jumps as US government eyes tweaks to China chip measures
summary: ASML Holding NV shares rose significantly after reports that the US government is planning to adjust restrictions on semiconductor equipment and AI memory chip sales to China. These tweaks, expected next week, will specifically target Huawei suppliers and chipmaking factories. The changes aim to curb China's tech ambitions while avoiding overly harsh measures on US and other foreign companies supplying to the People's Republic, following lobbying from US chip equipment makers concerned about competitiveness.

title: Micron Technology Inc. Stock Climbs 2.7%, Outperforms Market
summary: Micron Technology Inc. (MU) saw its stock climb 2.7%, outperforming the overall market. The article also briefly mentions Unity Software (U.US) reporting fourth-quarter revenue of $609 million, exceeding estimates, despite a quarterly loss of 66 cents per share.

title: Intel Announces Retirement of CEO Pat Gelsinger
summary: Intel Corporation announced the retirement of CEO Pat Gelsinger after a 40-plus-year career, effective December 1, 2024. David Zinsner and Michelle Johnston Holthaus have been named interim Co-CEOs, with Holthaus also appointed CEO of Intel Products. Frank Yeary will serve as interim executive chair while the board searches for a permanent successor.

title: US again targets China with more curbs on chip-related exports
summary: The outgoing Biden administration has imposed new restrictions on China's technological advancement by extending semiconductor-related export curbs to nearly 150 international companies, marking the third such action in three years. These measures, impacting both American and international firms, include new controls on software and high-bandwidth memory crucial for AI chips, drawing strong opposition from China, which considers them economic coercion detrimental to the global economy. The new Entity List additions include numerous semiconductor-related companies and expand US powers to curb chipmaking equipment exports to China.

title: NXP, TSMC Affiliate Aim to Expand $8 Billion Singapore Plant
summary: NXP Semiconductors NV and Vanguard International Semiconductor Corp., an affiliate of Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co., are planning an expansion of their $7.8 billion chip plant in Singapore. This move is driven by the need to diversify chip production amidst rising US-Chinese technology tensions. The joint venture aims to produce mature 130-nanometer to 40-nanometer chips for various industries and is also developing a localized supply chain for Chinese customers.

title: REX Crypto Equity Premium Income ETF Holdings
summary: This article details the holdings of the REX Crypto Equity Premium Income ETF (CEPI), which was created on December 4, 2024, by REX Shares. The ETF has $78.72 million in assets under management with 28 holdings, focusing on crypto-related and digital payment solutions stocks, and aims to generate income by writing covered call options. The list includes top holdings such as Micron Technology, Applied Digital Corp, and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co Ltd ADR, along with various options contracts.

title: Entegris and U.S. Department of Commerce Announce Entry Into Award Agreement Under CHIPS Act
summary: Entegris and the U.S. Department of Commerce have finalized an agreement for up to $77 million in funding under the CHIPS and Science Act, making Entegris the first semiconductor supplier to secure such an award. This funding will support Entegris' new manufacturing center of excellence in Colorado Springs, which is expected to create approximately 600 jobs and focus on producing critical materials like liquid filtration products and semiconductor wafer carriers. The center, targeted to begin operations in 2025, also aims to recruit 50% of its workforce from veterans and military families.

title: REX Crypto Equity Premium Income ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:CEPI)
summary: The REX Crypto Equity Premium Income ETF (CEPI) is an actively managed ETF that invests in crypto-related and digital payment solution stocks, aiming to generate income through covered call options. Launched on December 4, 2024, the fund seeks both capital growth and current income, with a dividend yield of 52.31% and a net expense ratio of 0.85%. Its top holdings include companies like Micron Technology, Applied Digital, and NVIDIA, reflecting its focus on the crypto and tech sectors.

title: U.S. Finalizes $6.1 Billion Investment in Micron Technology for Semiconductor Manufacturing
summary: The U.S. Department of Commerce has finalized a $6.1 billion investment in Micron Technology under the CHIPS and Science Act. This funding will support the construction of new memory chip manufacturing facilities in New York and Idaho, and expand an existing plant in Virginia. The initiative aims to significantly increase the U.S. share of advanced memory manufacturing, create over 20,000 jobs, and reduce reliance on foreign semiconductor vendors.

title: News | Micron edges closer to getting billions from government to build, expand US chip plants
summary: Micron Technology is moving closer to receiving billions in federal funding to construct new chip facilities in New York and Idaho, and expand an existing plant in Virginia. The U.S. Department of Commerce has finalized a $6.1 billion award and preliminarily agreed to expand the Manassas, Virginia, facility with up to $275 million, with the Biden administration aiming to secure these funds before the incoming Trump administration takes office. These investments are part of the CHIPS and Science Act, aiming to increase the U.S. share of advanced memory manufacturing from nearly 0% to 10% over the next decade.

title: Western Digital faces headwinds, shares drop
summary: Western Digital's shares dropped significantly after executives forecasted a challenging period due to excess PC and smartphone inventory and unexpected pricing headwinds in flash storage. Despite short-term difficulties, management is optimistic about a market recovery in 2025, particularly for enterprise SSDs and HDDs. Other industry players like Seagate Technology and Micron Technology also saw their shares fall.

title: Lam Research’s Dextro cobot boosts semiconductor production efficiency
summary: Lam Research has introduced Dextro, a collaborative robot arm designed to enhance the efficiency of critical maintenance tasks on semiconductor wafer fabrication equipment. Deployed in advanced wafer fabs globally, Dextro aims to optimize human engineers' effectiveness by increasing automation, particularly for tasks requiring sub-micron precision. The cobot, which uses a Universal Robots UR5e arm, reduces tool downtime and improves production yield by performing precise and repeatable maintenance, such as installing components and tightening bolts, often at a faster rate and with higher accuracy than manual methods.

title: Suppliers to Samsung and other chip firms play waiting game amid slow grants and industry slump
summary: Suppliers to Samsung and other major chip manufacturers like Intel and TSMC are experiencing delays due to the slow distribution of grants and an industry slump. These smaller companies, which had invested millions to increase capacity following expansion announcements from the chip giants, are now facing the challenge of timing their operations correctly to avoid idle equipment and personnel. The project delays are creating ripple effects throughout the entire supply chain.

title: Sandisk Previews Its New Corporate Branding Defined by a 'Mindset of Motion'
summary: SanDisk Corporation has unveiled its new corporate branding and creative direction, ahead of its planned re-launch as a standalone Flash and memory technology innovator in early 2025. The new branding, defined by a "Mindset of Motion," aims to symbolize progress and the enabling of aspirations through resilient data expression and storage. The logo, featuring clean lines and a pixel-driven "S" uniting with an open "D," is designed to embody innovation and collaboration in technology.

title: Micron shares plunge on weak second-quarter guidance
summary: Micron shares plunged 13% in extended trading after issuing weaker-than-expected second-quarter guidance, despite beating earnings estimates for the latest period. The company expects lower revenue and adjusted EPS for the second quarter compared to analysts' predictions, though first-quarter revenue was in line with expectations. Micron highlighted growth in data centers and AI ventures with Nvidia's processors as key areas for future growth.

title: Micron Set for Biggest Drop Since 2020 on Sluggish Sales Outlook
summary: Micron Technology Inc. experienced its largest stock drop in over four years after its revenue forecast for the fiscal second quarter missed analyst projections significantly. The company attributes this downturn to sluggish demand in the smartphone and personal computer markets, despite growth in AI. Micron's sales are estimated at $7.9 billion, well below the $8.99 billion analyst estimate, with profit also falling short of expectations.

title: Micron’s stock may see its worst day since 2020. Why the bulls are still upbeat.
summary: Micron Technology Inc. (MU) is experiencing a significant stock drop after providing a downbeat forecast for the current quarter. Despite this, analysts largely remain optimistic, citing the "well-understood weakness in near-term memory pricing" and an upbeat narrative surrounding artificial intelligence as key factors. TD Cowen analyst Krish Sankar views the decline as a buying opportunity, although the forecast fell short of even his below-consensus estimate.

title: SK Telecom announced on the 23rd that it will operate a joint exhibition hall under the theme of "Cr..
summary: SK Telecom, along with SK Hynix, SKC, and SK NMove, will operate a joint exhibition hall at CES 2025 focusing on "Creating a Sustainable Future with Innovative Artificial Intelligence (AI) Technology." The exhibition will showcase SK Group's AIDC (Artificial Intelligence Data Center) technology, various AI services, and collaborations with five global partners. Key highlights include SK's AI data center solutions, energy solutions for AIDC, AI accelerator, and innovative AI semiconductors like HBM3E 16th Stage and glass substrate technology.

title: Global semiconductor maker buys NY-taxpayer built factory that, so far, churned out losers
summary: Onsemi, a global semiconductor maker, has purchased a chip fabrication plant in DeWitt, New York, from the state for $20 million, significantly less than the $100 million taxpayers invested. Despite the financial loss for the state, officials view the sale as a win because Onsemi plans to create 80-100 jobs and requires no additional tax breaks. This acquisition marks a new chapter for the factory, which previously saw two tenants fail, and is bolstered by the anticipated arrival of Micron Technology nearby, creating a semiconductor ecosystem.

title: Jim Cramer on Micron Technology Inc. (MU) CEO Sanjay Mehrotr: ‘He Forecast So Badly’
summary: Jim Cramer criticizes Micron Technology Inc. CEO Sanjay Mehrotra for issuing an inaccurate forecast regarding a turnaround in various businesses, leading to a stock punishment for MU despite strong performance in high-bandwidth memory. Cramer also comments on the Federal Reserve's recent rate cut, suggesting they misjudged the economic data and got "trapped" by their own predictions. He further touches on US spending bills and Elon Musk's potential influence.

title: Electronic Manufacturing Services Market is projected to reach USD 1 trillion by 2032
summary: The global electronic manufacturing services (EMS) market, valued at USD 580 billion in 2023, is predicted to hit USD 1 trillion by 2032, growing at a 5% CAGR. This growth is driven by the proliferation of IoT devices and the increasing complexity of electronic products that necessitate specialized manufacturing capabilities. Key segments contributing to this expansion include engineering services for product development and innovation, consumer electronics due to high demand for advanced devices, and North America, which is fueled by its robust technological infrastructure and investments.

title: [News] Silicon Motion Joins Micron’s Cautious Outlook, Predicts Weak DRAM and NAND Demand until 1H25
summary: Silicon Motion echoes Micron's pessimistic forecast, anticipating soft demand for DRAM and NAND until the first half of 2025. The company also highlights the aggressive expansion of China's CXMT in the memory market, particularly in DDR5 and upcoming LPDDR5, which is predicted to intensify competition and pose challenges for Taiwanese and South Korean producers. Despite US chip export curbs, Chinese memory makers might still thrive by focusing on domestic sales, while overseas competitors could face price competition with significantly lower offerings from Chinese firms.

title: Jim Cramer on Micron Technology Inc. (MU): ‘I Think It Could Drop To Around $98 or $99. At That Point, I Would Consider Buying More’
summary: Jim Cramer discussed Micron Technology Inc. (MU), suggesting that a drop to around $98 or $99 would be a buying opportunity. He also commented on the broader market, criticizing the Federal Reserve's recent interest rate cut and expressing skepticism about speculative areas like quantum computing and Bitcoin. Cramer advises investors to focus on "cult stocks" that defy conventional valuation in 2025.

title: Micron Technology To Invest $3B For Virginia Expansion, Announces $3M Workforce Funding in New York
summary: Micron Technology is making a significant investment of $2.17 billion to expand its operations in Manassas, Virginia, creating 340 new jobs and modernizing its facility for semiconductor manufacturing. Concurrently, the company is committing $3 million to workforce development in New York, funding a specialized Semiconductor Hub at Syracuse University's D'Aniello Institute for Veterans and Military Families. These initiatives aim to strengthen domestic semiconductor production and provide career opportunities for veterans in the growing industry.

title: Samsung, Texas Instruments, and Amkor finalize CHIPS Act funding agreements
summary: Samsung, Texas Instruments, and Amkor have finalized their CHIPS and Science Act funding agreements with the US Department of Commerce. Samsung will receive $4.745 billion for its $37 billion investment in Texas, Texas Instruments will get $1.61 billion for its $18 billion project across Texas and Utah, and Amkor will receive $407 million for its $2 billion investment in an Arizona OSAT plant. These agreements are part of a broader effort by the US to secure a domestic supply of advanced semiconductors and are finalized before a new presidential term which may see changes to CHIPS Act support.

title: SK Group to Showcase 'Innovative AI, Sustainable Tomorrow' at CES 2025
summary: SK Group announced its participation in CES 2025 with an exhibition themed "Innovative AI, Sustainable Tomorrow," aiming to showcase its advanced AI technologies and services. Chairman Chey Tae-won will attend for the third consecutive year to strengthen global AI partnerships. The exhibition will feature AI data centers, AI services, and an AI ecosystem, jointly operated by four SK affiliates, to demonstrate their vision for AI business.

title: Markets News, January 7, 2025: Stocks Slide as Treasury Yields Rise After Economic Data; Nvidia Leads Tech Selloff; Bitcoin Plunges
summary: Stocks closed sharply lower on January 7, 2025, with major indexes like the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite falling, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average also slipped. This downturn was driven by rising Treasury yields following stronger-than-expected economic data, particularly in the labor market, which suggested the Federal Reserve might not cut interest rates as quickly as anticipated. Nvidia led a broader tech selloff despite initial gains from its CEO's CES keynote, and Bitcoin plunged below $100,000, reflecting decreased appetite for risk-on assets in a higher interest rate environment.

title: Micron Technology, Inc. (MU) Expands Crucial Lineup with High-Speed SSDs and DRAM Upgrades for Gaming, AI, and Professional Use
summary: Micron Technology (MU) has expanded its Crucial consumer memory and storage lineup with new high-speed SSDs and DRAM upgrades. These new products, including the Crucial P510 SSD and enhanced DDR5 Pro Gaming Memory, are designed for gamers, AI applications, and creative professionals. The article also touches upon the broader impact of AI, with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman discussing the pursuit of superintelligence and JC2 Ventures CEO John Chambers predicting significant productivity boosts from AI by 2025.

title: SK Telecom, SK hynix and Penguin Solutions Sign Collaboration Agreement for AI Data Center Solutions
summary: SK Telecom, SK hynix, and Penguin Solutions have signed a collaboration agreement to jointly research, develop, and promote AI data center (AIDC) solutions. The agreement, announced at CES 2025, builds on a prior strategic investment by SK Telecom in Penguin Solutions and aims to leverage the companies' complementary capabilities for global expansion, particularly in the Asia-Pacific and Middle East markets. They also plan to develop full-stack software solutions for AIDCs and cooperate on next-generation data center memory technology.

title: Micron Technology issues $1 billion in senior notes
summary: Micron Technology announced the completion of a $1 billion senior note offering with a 5.80% interest rate and a due date in 2035. The company plans to use the net proceeds to redeem its outstanding 4.975% Senior Notes due 2026 and for general corporate purposes. This information was disclosed in a recent 8-K filing with the SEC.

title: Tech Investing in 2025: Emerging Trends and Market Opportunities
summary: In 2025, technology continues to fuel innovation and economic expansion, with AI, semiconductors, cloud computing, cybersecurity, and renewable energy offering significant investment opportunities. A diversified investment strategy is crucial to navigate this dynamic environment, as the global economy and supportive policies are expected to provide strong tailwinds for the tech sector. The article highlights key areas like semiconductors, nuclear energy, and cloud computing, suggesting thematic ETFs (like SMH and NUCL) as practical solutions for broad exposure while mitigating risk.

title: SMART Modular Add-In Cards Now Listed on CXL Consortium Integrators' List
summary: SMART Modular Technologies announced its 4-DIMM and 8-DIMM CXL memory Add-in Cards (AICs) have successfully passed CXL 2.0 compliance testing and are now listed on the CXL Consortium's Integrators' List. This achievement highlights SMART Modular's dedication to industry standards and its role in advancing integrated memory technology to meet the growing demand for high-speed data processing. The company aims to provide cutting-edge solutions with enhanced performance and reliability.

title: Is Lam Research (LRCX) High Growth Semiconductor Stock That Is Profitable?
summary: The article analyzes Lam Research Corporation (LRCX) as a high-growth and profitable semiconductor stock. It details the company's strong position with its "4 Horsemen" technologies, key market leadership in wafer fabrication equipment, and positive analyst and hedge fund sentiments. Despite its strong standing, the article suggests that some undervalued AI stocks might offer even greater short-term returns.

title: NYSEARCA:NVDY: High-Yield Income ETF or Missed NVIDIA Gains?
summary: The YieldMax NVDA Option Income Strategy ETF (NYSEARCA:NVDY) offers an attractive 84% dividend yield through a covered call strategy on NVIDIA stock. While it provides high monthly income, this approach caps upside potential, leading to significant underperformance compared to NVIDIA's direct stock appreciation. Investors must weigh NVDY's income generation against its limited total return, dividend volatility, and specific risks like single-stock dependence and tax inefficiency.

title: One reason Nvidia looks like a bargain stock following the DeepSeek news
summary: Following a 17% decline in Nvidia's stock and investor concern over DeepSeek's low-cost generative AI, the article suggests it might be a good time for investors to consider buying. The DeepSeek news, indicating AI development for less money than OpenAI spent on ChatGPT, has caused apprehension that the hardware build-out driving Nvidia's growth could slow. However, the author notes that it is still a very early stage for analysis of the DeepSeek news.

title: One reason Nvidia looks like a bargain stock following the DeepSeek news
summary: Following a 17% decline in Nvidia's stock and investor concerns about DeepSeek's low-cost generative AI development, the article suggests that Nvidia might be considered a bargain stock. Despite fears that the hardware build-out driving Nvidia's growth may slow, the author emphasizes that it is still very early in the analysis of the DeepSeek news. Other technology stocks like Micron, Super Micro, and Workday are also mentioned as trading at relatively low valuations.

title: Ichor Holdings, Ltd. Announces Fourth Quarter and Fiscal Year 2024 Financial Results
summary: Ichor Holdings, Ltd. announced its financial results for the fourth quarter and fiscal year 2024, reporting revenues of $233 million for Q4 and $849 million for the full year. The company saw sequential revenue growth and expects continued strengthening customer demand, particularly in leading-edge foundry and logic investments, and a recovery in memory investments for 2025. Ichor also provided a financial outlook for the first quarter of 2025, projecting revenues between $235 million and $255 million.

title: DIH Holding US, Inc. completes public offering
summary: DIH Holding US, Inc. (NASDAQ: DHAI) has completed a public offering of 5,937,100 common units at $0.7832 each, comprised of Class A common stock and Class A warrants. The offering raised approximately $3.9 million net of fees, which will be used for capital expenditures, working capital, and general corporate purposes. This capital raise, conducted under a previously filed S-1 Registration Statement, comes as the company continues to expand its strategic partnerships and despite potential short-term liquidity challenges indicated by its current ratio.

title: Lam Research to invest Rs 10,000 crore in India, says Ashwini Vaishnaw
summary: Lam Research, a US-based semiconductor services company, plans to invest over Rs 10,000 crore in India. The company has signed an MoU with the Karnataka government to lease and eventually purchase land in Bengaluru, aiming to expand its supply chain operations in the country. This investment is part of India's broader Semiconductor Mission, which has already approved several projects with a total outlay of Rs 76,000 crore, including facilities by Micron Technology and Tata Electronics.

title: Client SSD Market to Grow by USD 38.14 Billion from 2025-2029, Driven by Focus on Enhanced Performance and Durability, Report on AI Impact on Market Trends - Technavio
summary: The client solid-state drive (SSD) market is projected to grow by USD 38.14 billion from 2025 to 2029, with a CAGR exceeding 35.7%, driven by increasing demand for enhanced performance and durability. Key drivers include the need for faster game loading, improved responsiveness in devices, and high-performance storage for notebooks, desktop PCs, and enterprise applications. The market is fragmented, with major players including Intel Corp., Samsung Electronics Co. Ltd., and Western Digital Corp., and growth is expected to be significant across APAC, North America, and Europe.

title: CEOs of these Idaho companies make millions. See what they and their median workers get
summary: A review of nine of Idaho's largest publicly traded companies reveals that many CEOs earn significantly more than their median workers, with some compensation packages in the multimillions of dollars. The article details the total compensation, median employee pay, and pay ratio for each CEO, highlighting how stock awards and other non-salary components contribute to their overall earnings. It also notes how some companies, classified as "emerging growth companies," are temporarily exempt from certain disclosure requirements regarding pay ratios.

title: Penguin Solutions’ Global Marketing VP Mark Seamans, “AI Currently Entering 2nd Wave with Business-specific Customization.”
summary: Mark Seamans, Global Marketing VP of Penguin Solutions, discusses the evolution of AI into its second wave, characterized by enterprise-level deployments and business-specific customization. He emphasizes the critical role of specialized AI infrastructure and Penguin Solutions' "OriginAI" platform in solving AI complexity for customers. Seamans also highlights the strategic significance of Penguin Solutions' cooperation with SKT in advancing AI solutions across various areas.

title: Micron Redefines Performance for AI PCs, Gamers and Professionals
summary: Micron Technology announced the new Micron 4600 PCIe Gen5 NVMe SSD, designed to significantly boost performance for AI PCs, gamers, creators, and professionals. Leveraging Micron G9 TLC NAND, this SSD delivers sequential read speeds of 14.5 GB/s and write speeds of 12.0 GB/s, effectively halving AI model loading times compared to Gen4 SSDs and offering improved energy efficiency. The 4600 SSD is compatible with leading processors from AMD and Intel and integrates advanced security features.

title: Semiconductor Chip Handler Market Size | CAGR of 9%
summary: The global semiconductor chip handler market is projected to reach USD 2.6 billion by 2034, growing at a CAGR of 9.10% from USD 1.1 billion in 2024. This growth is driven by increasing demand for semiconductors in consumer electronics and automotive industries, technological advancements, and the rise of automation in manufacturing. Asia-Pacific holds a dominant market position, largely due to its robust manufacturing base and significant R&D investments.

title: Micron Stock Soars 26% YTD: Should You Buy More or Book Profits?
summary: Micron Technology's stock has surged 26.5% year-to-date in 2025, driven by strong AI demand for high-bandwidth memory (HBM) and strategic partnerships, including with NVIDIA. However, the article suggests short-term risks such as margin contraction, weak consumer demand in PC and smartphone markets, and geopolitical tensions with China, making it potentially prudent to book profits. Despite long-term AI growth potential, near-term challenges and profitability concerns are highlighted.

title: 3D chipmaking gets a boost via plasma etch tool
summary: Lam Research has unveiled Akara, an advanced plasma conductor etch tool designed for 3D chipmaking. This new device is crucial for scaling gate-all-around (GAA) transistors, DRAM, and 3D NAND devices, enabling the formation of complex 3D structures through precise etch steps and EUV lithography. Akara features a solid-state plasma source, plasma species control, and an ion energy control system, boasting faster responsiveness and improved wafer output compared to previous technologies.

title: Complex AI chips will drive demand for Lam Research chipmaking tools, CEO says
summary: Lam Research CEO Tim Archer predicts that growing demand for complex AI silicon will significantly increase orders from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) for their chipmaking tools over the next three years. The company projects revenue between US$25 billion and US$28 billion in 2028, up from US$16.2 billion in 2024, and announced two new groundbreaking tools: one that uses molybdenum instead of tungsten for material deposition and another for more precise etching. These developments underscore the semiconductor industry's reliance on companies like Lam Research for advanced manufacturing capabilities.

title: Micron starts shipping 1γ DRAMs ...
summary: Micron Technology has announced it has begun shipping 1γ (1-gamma) 16Gbit DDR5 DRAMs to partners and customers, claiming to be the first to reach this sixth generation of DRAM process technology. This new memory offers up to a 15 percent speed increase and more than 20 percent power reduction compared to its predecessor, with a 30 percent increase in bits-per-wafer, though it requires EUV lithography. Micron plans to integrate 1γ across its DRAM range for various applications, including data centers, AI-PCs, mobile products, and automotive.

title: Italian government wants to replace ST's CEO, says report ...
summary: The Italian government, a joint shareholder in STMicroelectronics, is reportedly pushing for CEO Jean-Marc Chery to be replaced, according to Les Echos. Concerns include poor financial results in 2024, a perceived shift of power towards France within the company's executive committee, and potential disproportionate job cuts impacting Italian employees. The move may also be an attempt to ensure the next CEO is Italian.
