title: ON Semiconductor Implements Layoffs To Offset Costs, Save Up To $115M – Retail Sentiment Remains Bullish
summary: ON Semiconductor announced a restructuring plan, including 2,400 job cuts, to reduce costs and align with business trends, expecting annual savings of $105 million to $115 million. Despite the announcement and recent analyst downgrades following weaker-than-expected Q4 results, retail sentiment on Stocktwits remained extremely bullish. The stock has seen a 19% year-to-date decline, though the average analyst price target suggests a potential 19% upside.

title: How SkyWater's Billion-Chip Factory Acquisition Reshapes U.S. Semiconductor Manufacturing
summary: SkyWater Technology is acquiring Infineon's 200 mm fab in Austin, Texas, to significantly expand U.S. foundry capacity for essential chips. This strategic move aims to strengthen the domestic semiconductor supply chain for industrial, automotive, and defense applications, converting the facility from an IDM model to a foundry. The acquisition includes a long-term supply agreement with Infineon and will retain nearly 1,000 manufacturing jobs.

title: Sanan-STMicro JV Finishes Building USD3 Billion SiC Wafer Factory in Chongqing
summary: A joint venture between Chinese semiconductor firm Sanan Optoelectronics and Swiss company STMicroelectronics has completed construction of a CNY23 billion (USD3.2 billion) silicon carbide (SiC) wafer factory in Chongqing. The factory, China's first of its kind, is set to begin mass production of 8-inch automotive-grade SiC electronic control chips in Q4, with an expected annual output of 480,000 units. This initiative aims to localize SiC chip production for new energy vehicles in China, anticipating a significant reduction in costs and market growth to USD6.1 billion by 2030.

title: Taiwan semiconductor market to reach $66 billion by 2031
summary: Taiwan's semiconductor market is projected to reach USD 66.06 billion by 2031, growing at a CAGR of 5.8%. This growth is driven by increasing demand from emerging technologies like AI, autonomous driving, IoT, and 5G, as well as continuous R&D investment and expanding applications across various industries. Key players like TSMC, ASE Technology, and MediaTek are contributing to Taiwan's strong position in both advanced and mature process technologies.

title: Trump, Taiwanese chipmaker announce new $100 billion plan to build five new US factories
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) announced a new $100 billion investment to build five additional chip factories in the United States, bringing their total U.S. investment to $165 billion. This move, announced by CEO C.C. Wei alongside President Donald Trump, aims to bolster domestic chip production and reduce U.S. reliance on Asian-made semiconductors, aligning with Trump's strategy to create jobs and strengthen national security. The plan includes the production of advanced 2-nanometer and A16 technology chips in Arizona, building on previous agreements and government subsidies.

title: Germanium Market Size, Growth & Trends Forecast by 2033
summary: The global germanium market was valued at USD 263.80 million in 2024 and is projected to reach USD 375.12 million by 2033, growing at a CAGR of 4.07%. This growth is primarily driven by increasing demand in fiber optics, infrared optics, and semiconductor applications, alongside rising adoption in solar panels, especially for space applications. However, supply constraints and geographical concentration of production, notably China's dominance and export restrictions, pose significant challenges to market dynamics.

title: Trump calls Chips Act that funds Ohio Intel plant 'a horrible, horrible' thing
summary: Donald Trump has called for the repeal of the $52 billion Chips and Science Act, despite it funding projects like the Intel plant in Ohio and being critical for U.S. national and economic security. Trump argues that tariffs would be a better approach and has indicated that import levies on chips could be implemented soon, leading to uncertainty for companies that have committed to multibillion-dollar projects based on the Act's incentives. While many lawmakers and companies view the agreements as ironclad, a review by the Commerce Secretary indicates potential modifications or challenges to existing terms.

title: Semiconductor company seeks $3M tax deal for DeWitt chip fab site purchased from the state
summary: Onsemi, a semiconductor company, is seeking $3.4 million in tax exemptions from Onondaga County for a chip fabrication plant in DeWitt it purchased from New York state for $20 million. The company plans to invest $118.9 million to equip the facility, produce wide bandgap semiconductors, and create up to 80 jobs, with the expectation of also seeking federal tax credits under the CHIPS and Science Act. This marks the third attempt to bring the plant, which was built with $100 million in taxpayer money, to full operation.

title: embedded world 2025 Best-in-Show: Winners
summary: This article announces the winners of the embedded world 2025 Best-in-Show awards, recognizing excellence in design, performance, and market impact across various technology categories. Awarded products span AI & Machine Learning, Analog, Power & Related Components, Computer Boards, Systems, Components & Peripherals, Dev Tools, Software & OS, IoT & Connectivity, Memory & Storage, MEMS & Sensors, Microcontrollers, Microprocessors & IP, Security, and Wireless. The judging process involved a 15-point rubric assessed by the ECD Content Team.

title: SanDisk to Hike NAND Prices by Over 10% as US Memory Giant Expects Supply to Tighten
summary: SanDisk, a leading memory chip maker, plans to increase its NAND flash memory prices by over 10% from April 1st in anticipation of a supply shortage. This decision follows production cuts by major OEMs due to a previous surplus, and while it signals a potential market recovery, its acceptance by clients and the impact of weak demand in consumer electronics remain uncertain. The long-term outlook depends on the recovery of PC and smartphone demand and the adoption of AI features in gadgets.

title: [News] NAND Prices on the Rise: Micron and SanDisk Lead in April, with Samsung, SK hynix Likely to Follow
summary: Micron and SanDisk are initiating NAND price hikes, with SanDisk planning over 10% increase from April 1, signaling a market turnaround. Samsung and SK hynix are also expected to follow suit with their own price increases. This shift is attributed to anticipated demand surpassing supply, manufacturers' production cuts, reduced smartphone inventory, and growing demand from AI and DeepSeek applications.

title: HEICO shareholders approve board, executive pay, and auditor
summary: HEICO Corporation held its Annual Meeting of Shareholders, where all ten director nominees were re-elected, executive compensation received advisory approval, and Deloitte & Touche LLP was ratified as the independent auditor. The company reported strong financial health with over $36 billion market cap and impressive revenue growth. These decisions are set to guide HEICO's strategic direction, building on its recent strong Q1 2025 results and analyst upgrades.

title: Lumentum Selected As Nvidia Silicon Photonics Partner To Drive Scalable AI Networking Solutions
summary: Lumentum Holdings Inc. has partnered with NVIDIA to advance scalable AI networking solutions, leveraging Lumentum’s high-power, high-efficiency indium phosphide lasers. This collaboration aims to develop new NVIDIA Spectrum-X Photonics networking switches, significantly reducing power consumption and enhancing resilience in large-scale AI infrastructure. The partnership highlights Lumentum’s expertise in photonic innovations and NVIDIA’s vision for next-generation million-scale AI deployments, emphasizing the transition from copper to photonic interconnects.

title: Micron forecasts upbeat quarterly revenue on strong AI memory chip demand
summary: Micron Technology projected third-quarter revenue above Wall Street estimates, driven by robust demand for its high-bandwidth memory (HBM) chips crucial for AI models. The company reported that all its HBM chips are sold out for calendar year 2025, indicating strong market confidence. Micron also expects improved profitability in fiscal 2025 across both data center and consumer markets, despite potential uncertainties from new tariffs.

title: Micron’s stock falls despite solid jump in revenue from AI memory chips
summary: Micron Technology Inc. reported strong Q2 earnings and revenue, driven primarily by a significant increase in sales of high-bandwidth memory (HBM) chips vital for AI servers. Despite beating Wall Street's forecasts and strong growth projections for HBM and DRAM sales, the company's stock fell more than 7% on Friday in a down market. Analysts and the CEO highlighted Micron's strong competitive position and sustained demand for its products, particularly in the AI and next-generation PC markets.

title: Micron Technology opens manufacturing facility in China
summary: Micron Technology has officially opened its new manufacturing facility in Xi'an, China, dedicated to assembly and test operations for its semiconductor products. This facility marks Micron's first manufacturing presence in China, with an expected investment of $250 million and up to 2,000 employees. The company's expansion highlights its commitment to the Chinese market, having previously established offices and a design center in the country.

title: Chipmaker Micron's shares slump as tepid margin forecast eclipses AI prospects
summary: Micron's shares dropped 8% after the company issued a conservative gross margin forecast, overshadowing a strong revenue outlook driven by demand for AI-related semiconductors. The lower profitability is largely attributed to oversupply and weak pricing in consumer memory chips (NAND Flash), despite high demand for its high-bandwidth memory (HBM) chips used in AI applications. The company anticipates third-quarter revenue above estimates due to AI, but margins are pressured by NAND oversupply and the ramp-up of HBM production.

title: ASP Isotopes Begins Commercial Production of Enriched Silicon-28 for Quantum and Semiconductor Applications
summary: ASP Isotopes Inc. has started commercial production of enriched Silicon-28 at its facility in Pretoria, South Africa, with initial shipments expected in Q2 2025 to U.S.-based customers. Silicon-28 is crucial for quantum computing due to its spin-free nature which aids in protecting quantum information, and for advanced semiconductors due to its superior thermal conductivity for AI applications. The company utilizes a proprietary Aerodynamic Separation Process (ASP) technology to directly enrich silane, promising higher purity material compared to competitors.

title: US firm Vishay Intertechnology confirms £250m investment at UK's biggest semiconductor facility
summary: US tech company Vishay Intertechnology has announced a £250m investment at its Newport foundry in South Wales, which will focus on producing advanced silicon carbide semiconductors for electric vehicles. This investment is part of Vishay's larger plan to inject £1bn into the facility by 2030, creating hundreds of new high-skilled jobs and potentially expanding into defense, aerospace, and renewable energy sectors. The UK Government is supporting this through its Automotive Transformation Fund, aiming to boost the economy and position the UK as a leader in advanced semiconductors.

title: Utahns lose jobs at Texas Instruments after it snagged up to $1.6B in federal CHIPS Act funding
summary: Texas Instruments laid off workers at its Lehi, Utah facility, three months after receiving up to $1.61 billion in federal CHIPS Act funding to expand its semiconductor manufacturing. The company stated the layoffs are part of changes to "efficiently support long-term operational plans" and did not meet the WARN Act threshold for mass layoffs. Despite declining sales, Texas Instruments reiterated its commitment to its Utah operations and plans to build a second fabrication plant in Lehi, which Governor Spencer Cox described as the "greatest single economic investment in Utah history."

title: SOXL ETF Holdings List — AMEX:SOXL
summary: This article provides a list of the top holdings for the Direxion Daily Semiconductor Bull 3X Shares (SOXL) ETF as of February 26, 2026. The list includes semiconductor companies such as Micron Technology, NVIDIA Corporation, Advanced Micro Devices, and Broadcom, along with their respective weight percentages, share counts, and market values. It highlights the fund's composition and offers detailed ETF holdings data.

title: GlobalFoundries, Taiwanese chipmaker UMC consider merger, sources say
summary: Taiwan's United Microelectronics (UMC) and U.S.-based GlobalFoundries (GFS.O) are reportedly considering a merger, according to sources familiar with the matter. This potential deal aims to create a larger U.S.-based chip firm with a global manufacturing presence, strengthening the U.S. semiconductor supply chain amidst geopolitical tensions and competition from China. The combined entity could also become a significant alternative to industry giant Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC).

title: XAIX ETF Holdings List — FWB:XAIX
summary: The XAIX ETF, Xtrackers Artificial Intelligence & Big Data UCITS ETF, holds a diversified portfolio of companies involved in artificial intelligence and big data. As of February 27, 2026, its top holdings include major tech companies like Samsung Electronics, Micron Technology, SK hynix, Alphabet, and Alibaba. The ETF aims to provide exposure to the performance of some of the largest and most liquid companies globally that are engaged in the AI and big data sectors.

title: ARTY ETF Holdings List — AMEX:ARTY
summary: The ARTY ETF (iShares Future AI & Tech ETF) holdings list, as of February 26, 2026, details its top investments. Key holdings include Micron Technology, Taiwan Semiconductor, SK Hynix, NAVER Corp., NVIDIA, and Advanced Micro Devices. The ETF focuses on companies involved in artificial intelligence and technology.

title: International Business
summary: Virginia offers international businesses a competitive edge in the North American market through its top-rated infrastructure, robust workforce development, and strategic East Coast location. The state boasts a favorable business climate, attracting over 1000 foreign companies with its low corporate tax rate, extensive site availability, and leading educational system. Key advantages include seamless connectivity to global markets via its deep-water port and major airport, a highly educated workforce, and diverse industry support, making it an attractive destination for global expansion.

title: In the wake of Micron Technology, Inc.'s (NASDAQ:MU) latest US$27b market cap drop, institutional owners may be forced to take severe actions
summary: Micron Technology, Inc. recently experienced a US$27 billion market cap drop, leading to significant losses for its institutional owners who hold 80% of the company. These institutional investors, who have already seen a 47% loss over the past year, may be pressured to sell, potentially impacting individual investors. Despite insider selling, analysts maintain coverage, and institutional ownership suggests confidence, though the concentration of ownership among the top 18 shareholders means no single entity holds a majority.

title: Micron Technology (NasdaqGS:MU) Sees 27% Weekly Price Drop As AI Memory Solutions Announced
summary: Micron Technology experienced a 27% weekly price drop despite announcing AI memory solutions and restructuring its credit agreement. This decline is attributed to broader market turmoil, including the Nasdaq entering a bear market and global trade tensions affecting other chipmakers. While its AI memory solutions and partnerships have long-term potential, short-term pressures and tariffs have negatively impacted investor sentiment and stock performance.

title: Micron Technology: Nothing Is Over (NASDAQ:MU)
summary: Despite a recent market correction, the author maintains a "Buy" rating on Micron Technology (MU) due to strong fundamentals, increasing memory prices, and rising AI demand. Micron's Q4 revenue surpassed expectations, driven by record data center DRAM sales and significant HBM growth, and its $14 billion CapEx plan for FY2025 aims to meet future demand and achieve market share parity in HBM. The current valuation is considered very cheap, historically marking local bottoms for the stock before periods of expansion.

title: Stance Sustainable Beta ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:CHGX)
summary: This article provides detailed information on the Stance Sustainable Beta ETF (CHGX), including its current price, dividend information, key statistics, and top holdings. It highlights CHGX as an exchange-traded fund investing in large-cap US stocks meeting ESG standards, and answers frequently asked questions about its performance and shareholder base. The ETF was trading at $27.20 as of March 18, 2026, and focuses on Environmental, Social, and Governance criteria.

title: Stealthy SunRise is developing 3D ferroelectric RAM ...
summary: SunRise Memory Corp., founded by SanDisk co-founder Eli Harari, is developing 3D ferroelectric RAM for AI applications to address the "Memory Wall" limitations of conventional DRAM. The company aims for a tenfold improvement in density and high bandwidth by designing horizontally stacked FeFET memory cells, optimized for parallel processing. SunRise, backed by investors like Seagate and Lam Research, plans for manufacturability in existing 3D NAND fabs and has over 80 patents.

title: What’s Wrong With First Solar Stock?
summary: First Solar's stock appears significantly undervalued despite strong fundamentals like 26%+ growth, 30%+ operating margins, and low leverage. Investors are concerned about potential U.S. government policy shifts away from climate regulations and high capital expenditures leading to negative free cash flow. However, First Solar benefits from favorable domestic production positioning against tariff wars and has better fundamentals than competitors like Enphase Energy, suggesting it could be a long-term value buy.

title: ASML: Dip Presents a Compelling Opportunity Amid Macro Concerns
summary: Despite lower-than-expected first-quarter orders of EUR 3.9 billion, Morningstar maintains its EUR 850 fair value estimate for ASML, seeing a compelling opportunity due to significant macro uncertainty already being discounted in the stock price. The firm expects ASML to achieve its 2025 and 2026 revenue guidance, driven by AI growth and achievable order targets. ASML's current valuation, trading at 24x and 19x its 2025 and 2026 EPS estimates respectively, suggests an attractive entry point for investors.

title: Cadence Advances AI in the Cloud with Industry-First DDR5 12.8Gbps MRDIMM Gen2 Memory IP System Solution
summary: Cadence announced the industry's first DDR5 12.8Gbps MRDIMM Gen2 memory IP system solution on the TSMC N3 process, designed to meet the increasing memory bandwidth demands of AI processing in data centers and enterprise applications. This new solution features a high-performance PHY and controller, validated in hardware to achieve 12.8Gbps data rates, effectively doubling bandwidth with current DDR5 6400Mbps DRAM parts. The IP system aims to provide a significant performance advantage for advanced SoCs and chiplets, with multiple customer engagements already underway.

title: A Sharp Slowdown in Asia
summary: Morgan Stanley Research has revised down its economic growth forecast for Asia in 2025 due to mounting uncertainties surrounding tariffs and their impact on corporate confidence and trade. The report indicates a likely sharp and synchronized slowdown, with GDP growth in the region estimated at 4% for 2025, down from 4.4%. The severity of the slowdown will largely depend on the progress and outcomes of tariff negotiations, particularly with the U.S., with some countries like China facing more complex discussions than others.

title: Shanghai chip firm doubles quarterly profit as China expands supply chain role
summary: Chinese semiconductor company Montage Technology reported a significant surge in revenue and profit for the March quarter, driven by strong demand from the AI industry for its DDR5 DRAM connector chips. This performance highlights China's growing influence in the global memory chip supply chain, despite existing US tech restrictions. Montage Technology is one of the world's leading suppliers of these critical connector chips.

title: Avnet AI Tech Days Empower Singapore's AI Ambitions
summary: Avnet Asia is hosting "Avnet AI Tech Days – Shaping the Future of AI" in Singapore on May 14, 2025, to support the nation's AI development efforts. The event will showcase cutting-edge AI solutions from Avnet and its partners, feature insights from leading AI experts, and address challenges such as security and data quality based on an Avnet Insights survey. This initiative aligns with Singapore's National AI Strategy, aiming to empower local businesses and accelerate AI integration across various sectors.

title: Heavily Shorted MicroAlgo Grabs Retail Attention Amid After-Hours Rebound: Retail Stays Cautious
summary: MicroAlgo (MLGO) shares saw an after-hours rebound following an earnings-induced slump, trending on Stocktwits. The company reported a profit of 53.4 million yuan for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2024, attributed to a strategic shift towards central processing algorithm services. Despite the rebound and high short interest, retail sentiment remained cautious, with some users expressing skepticism about the stock.

title: Adani pauses talks with Israel's Tower for $10 billion India chip foray, sources say
summary: The Adani Group has reportedly paused discussions with Israel's Tower Semiconductor for a $10 billion chip manufacturing project in India. Sources indicate that Adani found the venture strategically and commercially unviable due to uncertain demand in India and Tower Semiconductor's insufficient financial commitment to the partnership. This decision marks a potential setback for India's aspirations to become a global chipmaking hub, following a previous collapse of a similar venture between Vedanta and Foxconn.

title: Adani Group pauses talks with Israel’s tower semiconductor on $10 billion chip project: Report
summary: The Adani Group has reportedly paused discussions with Israel’s Tower Semiconductor for a proposed $10 billion chip manufacturing venture in India. This decision stems from concerns over the project's strategic and commercial viability, including uncertain domestic demand and Tower's investment commitment. The setback challenges India's aspirations to become a global chipmaking hub, following similar issues with the Vedanta-Foxconn deal.

title: Wolfspeed is hoping a new CEO will be able to right its financial woes
summary: Wolfspeed's new CEO, Robert Feurle, faces the critical task of addressing the semiconductor company's financial challenges, including $6.4 billion in long-term debt and the need to refinance $575 million coming due next May. He also aims to secure $750 million in CHIPS Act funding. Feurle is optimistic about silicon carbide technology's future, despite a slowdown in the EV market, and plans to diversify Wolfspeed's business beyond EVs to data centers and solar farms.

title: Qualcomm Beats Analyst Forecasts
summary: Qualcomm (Nasdaq: QCOM) reported better-than-estimated revenue for its second quarter and provided guidance for the third quarter, projecting $9.9 billion to $10.7 billion in revenue. Despite beating analyst consensus for diluted earnings per share and revenue, shares saw a dip due to Wall Street's cautious outlook regarding economic uncertainty and challenges in global trade. The company highlighted strong performance in automotive chips and IoT, along with a significant increase in smartphone handset revenue.

title: Flat Panel Display Market Size, Share & Growth, 2033
summary: The global flat panel display market was valued at USD 131.48 billion in 2024 and is projected to reach USD 224.03 billion by 2033, growing at a CAGR of 6.10%. Key drivers include increased demand from consumer electronics and automotive sectors, technological advancements like OLEDs, and rising applications in various industries. However, challenges such as high R&D costs and price volatility are also present.

title: Astera Labs Ramps Production of PCIe 6 Connectivity Portfolio Supercharging Advanced AI and Cloud Infrastructure Deployments
summary: Astera Labs announced that its PCIe 6 connectivity portfolio is ramping up production to accelerate the deployment of modern AI platforms. This expanded portfolio now includes gearbox connectivity solutions, alongside existing fabric switches, retimers, and active cable modules, providing a comprehensive platform for high performance and scalability in AI and general-compute systems. The company's solutions, including the new Arias 6 PCIe Smart Gearbox, Scorpio P-Series Smart Fabric Switches, and Aries 6 PCIe/CXL Smart DSP Retimers, address the demand for higher bandwidth and lower latency interconnects driven by advanced AI workloads.

title: Sandisk slides into loss after split from Western Digital
summary: Sandisk reported revenue and profit declines in its first quarter as a standalone company after splitting from Western Digital. The company recorded a GAAP loss of $103 million due to a $1.83 billion goodwill impairment charge, despite revenues of $1.7 billion. CEO David Goeckeler highlighted innovation with BiCS 8 technology and initiatives to reduce supply and increase prices to maximize returns amid an oversupplied market.

title: What Happens to the CHIPS Act Now?
summary: The article discusses the uncertain future of the CHIPS Act under a potential Trump presidency, highlighting concerns about renegotiation, tariffs on foreign-made semiconductors, and the potential impact on global manufacturing and the tech sector. It details how the Biden administration leveraged the Act to boost domestic chip production and attract major investments, contrasting this with Trump's view of the Act as wasteful and his preference for avoiding tariffs over subsidies. The piece also touches on efforts to counter China's influence and the potential economic consequences of new tariffs on consumers and the semiconductor industry.

title: Rambus Launches Client Chipsets for Advanced AI PC Memory Modules
summary: Rambus Inc. has released new client chipsets for next-generation AI PC memory modules, including two new Power Management ICs (PMICs), the PMIC5200 and PMIC5120. These chipsets, which also include a Client Clock Driver and SPD Hub, offer complete solutions for LPDDR5 CAMM2, DDR5 CSODIMMs, and CUDIMMs, addressing the performance and power efficiency demands of AI PCs. Rambus now provides complete memory interface chipsets for all JEDEC standard DDR5 and LPDDR5 memory modules for both server and client applications.

title: 5 Technology Stocks Powering S&P 500 ETF
summary: The S&P 500 has rebounded in 2025, driven significantly by technology stocks, wiping out earlier losses attributed to tariff plans. This article highlights five key technology stocks, Microchip Technology Inc. (MCHP), Seagate Technology (STX), Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR), Micron Technology (MU), and Arista Networks Inc. (ANET), that are leading the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) rally. These companies boast strong performance, positive earnings revisions, and favorable Zacks Ranks, contributing to renewed market optimism amid easing economic slowdown concerns and positive inflation and jobs data.

title: Applied Materials’ chips are down following revenue miss and light guidance
summary: Applied Materials Inc. reported mixed second-quarter results, with earnings beating expectations but revenue slightly missing estimates at $7.1 billion. The company's guidance for the current quarter also fell short, leading to a more than 5% drop in stock price after hours. Despite the cautious outlook, CEO Gary Dickerson emphasized that AI computing remains a major growth driver and that demand hasn't changed, though investor concerns persist regarding ongoing U.S.-China trade tensions.

title: Foxconn Builds AI Factory in Partnership With Taiwan and NVIDIA
summary: NVIDIA and Foxconn are partnering with the Taiwan government to build an AI factory supercomputer, featuring 10,000 NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs, to boost AI computing for researchers, startups, and industries in Taiwan. This initiative, led by Foxconn's subsidiary Big Innovation Company, will also support TSMC's R&D and the Taiwan National Science and Technology Council's efforts to accelerate AI development across various sectors. The AI factory aims to enhance smart cities, electric vehicles, and manufacturing through advanced AI infrastructure and cloud services.

title: Qorvo endorses activist investor from Starboard Value for seat on company's board
summary: Qorvo has endorsed Peter Feld, an activist investor from Starboard Value, for a seat on the company's board of directors. This expansion of the board and the addition of an activist investor representative suggest potential future changes for the semiconductor firm. The move indicates a proactive step by Qorvo in response to, or in anticipation of, activist involvement.

title: Chipmaker TSMC is booming, but its factory near Portland is at a low point
summary: While TSMC's overall sales and profits surged in 2024, driven by AI-related demand, its Camas, Washington factory (TSMC Washington) experienced a significant downturn. This factory, which uses decades-old technology, saw a 30% sales drop and a $32 million loss, struggling with a cyclical demand dip for older chips, trade headwinds, and its isolation from TSMC's core operations. Despite its current struggles and historical challenges adapting to the U.S. market, TSMC maintains it is still investing in the site.

title: How Will Analog Devices Stock React To Q2 Earnings?
summary: Analog Devices (ADI) is projected to report strong Q2 FY'25 earnings with anticipated revenue of $2.51 billion and EPS of $1.70, driven by improving demand in industrial, automotive, and data center sectors. Historically, ADI has shown a slightly higher probability of positive one-day post-earnings returns, particularly over the last three years. The article also discusses strategies for understanding post-earnings stock reactions, including correlations between short-term and medium-term returns and peer performance.

title: Mizuho Raises Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Target to $135, Reiterates Outperform
summary: Mizuho has increased its price target for Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) to $135 from $117, maintaining an "Outperform" rating, following an artificial intelligence server supply chain call. The article also discusses the broader context of AI's energy demands, mentioning former US President Donald Trump's push for coal-fired plants to meet this need, despite the tech industry's focus on renewable energy and natural gas. AMD is highlighted as a significant AI stock, ranking 9th on Wall Street's radar, with a strong presence in semiconductors, processors, and GPUs for AI.

title: NVIDIA Selects Navitas to Collaborate on Next Generation 800 V HVDC Architecture
summary: Navitas Semiconductor is collaborating with NVIDIA on its next-generation 800 V HVDC architecture to support 'Kyber' rack-scale systems for GPUs like Rubin Ultra, utilizing Navitas' GaNFast and GeneSiC power technologies. This initiative aims to establish high-efficiency, scalable power delivery for AI workloads, reducing infrastructure complexity and improving reliability. The 800V HVDC architecture is projected to enhance end-to-end power efficiency by up to 5% and significantly lower maintenance and cooling costs in AI data centers.

title: Intel, Micron, Qualcomm, TI join call for chip tariff exemptions ...
summary: US chip giants Intel, Micron, Qualcomm, and Texas Instruments have asked the US Department of Commerce for semiconductor import tariff exemptions or lighter burdens. Their submissions are a response to a "Section 232" investigation into semiconductor imports, coming after TSMC also appealed to the Trump administration. The companies, while supporting reshoring, warn that poorly designed tariffs could harm US interests and the complex semiconductor supply chain.

title: KEPCO to invest $53.1 bil. by 2038 to expand power supply infrastructure for advanced industries
summary: Korea Electric Power Corp. (KEPCO) announced a plan to invest $53.1 billion by 2038 to enhance power supply infrastructure for key industries like semiconductors and improve its electricity transmission system. This investment includes building a substation in a Yongin industrial cluster to power AI memory chip production and establishing advanced high-voltage direct current (HVDC) systems. KEPCO projects this initiative will generate significant economic impact, valued at approximately 134 trillion won, and create 480,000 jobs.

title: Engineer: No need to drag out Micron decision after environmental review comes out (Your Letters)
summary: A retired engineer argues that the environmental review process for the Micron project in Clay, NY, should be swift. He asserts that the project is a "yes/no" decision crucial for the region's economic future, contrasting it with the I-81 decision process. The author emphasizes that a 45-day public comment period is sufficient and that any delay or rejection needs a compelling reason beyond mere preference for a different outcome.

title: New $56M SPAC Targets Asian Tech Giants: Wintergreen IPO Closes with China Focus
summary: Wintergreen Acquisition Corp. (NASDAQ: WTGUU) successfully closed its initial public offering, raising $55.95 million. The SPAC aims to acquire a technology, media, or telecommunications business with operations in the Asia Pacific region, specifically Greater China. A total of $56.09 million has been placed in a trust account for future business combinations.

title: Malaysia's delicate balancing act
summary: Malaysia is facing increasing pressure to abandon its neutral stance amidst the escalating US-China tech war, particularly concerning semiconductor supply chains. The US's efforts to reshore chip production are reconfiguring the global industry, impacting Malaysia, which is a significant semiconductor exporter. A recent incident involving Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching and Huawei’s Ascend chips highlighted the difficulties Malaysia faces in maintaining its neutrality given geopolitical tensions.

title: Synopsys suspends China sales; Ansys merger gets FTC nod
summary: Synopsys has suspended all sales to China due to new trade restrictions from the Trump administration, impacting a market that accounts for about 10% of its revenues. Concurrently, the Federal Trade Commission has conditionally approved Synopsys' $35 billion acquisition of Ansys, requiring divestment of certain assets—specifically optical software tools, photonic software tools, and power consumption analysis tools—to Keysight Technologies due to anticompetitive concerns. This follows similar regulatory hurdles in the European Union, while the China sales suspension may complicate the Ansys acquisition's approval in China.

title: Nvidia tops Microsoft, regains most valuable company title for first time since January
summary: Nvidia has once again surpassed Microsoft to become the most valuable publicly traded company in the world, marking the first time since January. The artificial intelligence chipmaker's stock rose about 3% on Tuesday to $141.40, bringing its market capitalization to $3.45 trillion compared to Microsoft's $3.44 trillion. Nvidia's growth is largely fueled by the high demand for its AI chips, essential for developing advanced AI technologies like ChatGPT.

title: aethlon medical announces reverse stock split effective june 6
summary: Aethlon Medical Inc. (NASDAQ: AEMD) will implement a 1-for-8 reverse stock split of its common stock, effective June 6, 2025, with trading on an adjusted basis commencing June 9, 2025. This decision, approved by stockholders, aims to consolidate shares without altering par value or percentage ownership, except for fractional share rounding. The company also recently completed a warrant inducement agreement, raising approximately $2.316 million for working capital.

title: India’s first Aatmanirbhar semiconductor chip is finally here!
summary: India is set to roll out its first indigenously produced semiconductor chips by mid-2025, marking a significant step towards self-reliance in technology. Union IT Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw confirmed that 28nm and 90nm nodes will begin production, a crucial development for India despite not being cutting-edge global technology. This initiative aims to establish India's credibility in chip manufacturing, foster a domestic supply chain for essential electronics, and reduce dependence on foreign imports and geopolitical risks.

title: Micron Technology, Inc. Ships HBM4 36GB Samples, Enhancing Memory Performance for Next-Gen AI Applications
summary: Micron Technology announced the shipment of HBM4 36GB 12-high samples to key customers, marking a significant advancement in memory performance for AI applications. The new HBM4 memory boasts speeds over 2.0 TB/s and over 20% better power efficiency compared to its predecessor, HBM3E, which is crucial for managing generative AI workloads. Micron plans to increase HBM4 production in 2026 to align with the development of next-generation AI platforms, reinforcing its leadership in the AI memory market.

title: CleanCore Solutions Settles Lawsuits, Issues Shares
summary: CleanCore Solutions (NYSE American:ZONE) settled two disputes, one with its former CEO Matthew Atkinson and another with Boustead Securities, LLC, involving the issuance of shares and cash payments. These settlements aim to resolve outstanding legal matters, allowing the company to focus on its core business operations. Additionally, CleanCore's stockholders elected directors, ratified an accounting firm, and approved an increase in its equity incentive plan's share reserve.

title: GGLL Bulls Watch Willow—GGLS Bears Watch DC
summary: This article discusses the current performance of Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) stock, which has seen a significant drop after reaching an all-time high in February. It explores two potential catalysts for the stock's future: Alphabet's new Willow quantum computing chip, which could drive growth and innovation, and looming antitrust risks and U.S. export controls, which could negatively impact the company. The article also highlights Direxion's leveraged ETFs, GGLL and GGLS, for traders looking to capitalize on bullish or bearish movements in GOOGL.

title: 10 Hot Large Cap Stocks to Buy Now
summary: This article highlights Seagate Technology Holdings plc (NASDAQ: STX) as one of the top 10 hot large-cap stocks to buy now. It details the company's strong fiscal Q3 2025 financial performance, including revenue growth, margin expansion, and debt reduction. Ananda Baruah from Loop Capital Markets maintained a Buy rating on STX with a price target of $200.00.

title: Wells Fargo Remains Bullish on Micron Technology (MU) With a $130 PT
summary: Wells Fargo analyst Aaron Rakers maintained a Buy rating on Micron Technology (MU) with a $130 price target, citing the company's strong market positioning and technological advancements. The analyst highlighted Micron's recent shipment of 36GB HBM4 12-High to customers, its advanced 1-beta DRAM process, and its strategic focus on customizing logic base die with HBM4E+ as key growth drivers. Micron's HBM segment also saw significant revenue growth in fiscal Q2 2025, exceeding $1 billion.

title: Texas Instruments plans $60 billion U.S. investment amid Trump's onshoring push
summary: Texas Instruments announced plans for a $60 billion U.S. investment to expand its manufacturing footprint, responding to government pressure to onshore semiconductor production. This investment, aimed at building or expanding facilities in Texas and Utah and creating 60,000 jobs, follows similar commitments from other chipmakers. The move is seen as an overture to the Trump administration, which has advocated for domestic semiconductor manufacturing and threatened tariffs.

title: Texas Instruments plans $60 billion US investment under Trump push
summary: Texas Instruments announced plans to invest over $60 billion to expand its U.S. manufacturing footprint, constructing seven chip plants across Texas and Utah. This significant investment is driven by a push from the Trump administration to boost domestic semiconductor production, following a $1.61 billion subsidy from the Biden administration. The company aims to create 60,000 jobs and reinforce the supply chain for foundational chips used in everyday devices.

title: Jabil’s US$500m Manufacturing Expansion for AI Data Centres
summary: Jabil is investing US$500m to expand its US manufacturing capabilities, focusing on cloud and AI data center infrastructure with a goal to launch an AI infrastructure facility in the southeastern US by mid-2026. This expansion coincides with Jabil’s acquisition of Mikros Technologies, specializing in liquid cooling systems, to address the thermal management needs of high-performance computing in data centers. The investment is part of a broader trend of significant US manufacturing investments in technology hardware, driven partly by the CHIPS and Science Act, aiming to establish domestic supply chains for critical components.

title: Texas Instruments to invest $60 billion in US under Trump
summary: Texas Instruments (TI) plans to invest over $60 billion in the U.S. to expand chip manufacturing facilities, aligning with former President Donald Trump's push for increased domestic production. This investment will involve building or expanding seven facilities in Texas and Utah, aiming to create 60,000 jobs and reduce reliance on Asian supply chains. The move also serves to bolster U.S. semiconductor self-sufficiency and respond to geopolitical pressures for reshoring manufacturing.

title: Economic Development Organization Presents Annual Highlights & Achievement Awards
summary: Operation Oswego County (OOC) recently held its 73rd annual meeting, highlighting its 2024 achievements including the creation or retention of 605 jobs and over $165 million in capital investment across Oswego County. The event recognized several businesses and individuals for their significant contributions to economic development, including ConnextCare, NET & Die, Sorbello and Sons Farms, Oswego Industries, and Pathfinder Bank's Ron Tascarella. Keynote speaker Dr. Peter Nwosu of SUNY Oswego discussed the college's role in workforce development and supporting Micron's investment.

title: Sanjay Mehrotra
summary: Sanjay Mehrotra, Chairman, President, and CEO of Micron Technology, has over 40 years of experience in the tech industry, co-founding SanDisk and now leading Micron's innovation in AI and data. He advocates for STEM education and inclusion, earning significant industry recognition for his contributions. Despite his financial and business achievements, his tenure at Micron has faced controversies including layoffs, strategic missteps, and allegations of insider trading.

title: Chewy stock falls after $1 billion share offering announcement
summary: Chewy Inc. (NYSE: CHWY) stock dropped 4% after its largest shareholder, Buddy Chester Sub LLC (affiliated with BC Partners PE, LP), announced a public offering of $1 billion worth of Class A common stock. Chewy itself will not receive any proceeds from this sale but has agreed to repurchase $100 million of Class A common stock from the selling shareholder, which will be canceled upon completion. This repurchase is separate from the company's existing $500 million share repurchase program and is contingent on the completion of the public offering.

title: Stocks making the biggest moves after hours: Micron, H.B. Fuller, Kratos Defense & Security Solutions and more
summary: Micron's stock rose nearly 4% after exceeding fiscal third-quarter earnings and revenue expectations, with strong Q4 guidance. H.B. Fuller saw its stock jump over 6% due to better-than-expected quarterly earnings and a strong full-year forecast. Conversely, Kratos Defense & Security Solutions' shares fell almost 7% following the announcement of a $500 million public stock offering, while MillerKnoll surged 9% on increased net sales despite a drop in adjusted EPS.

title: The Zacks Analyst Blog Highlights Micron Technology, NVIDIA, Broadcom and Marvell Technology
summary: Zacks Analyst Blog highlights Micron Technology (MU), NVIDIA (NVDA), Broadcom (AVGO), and Marvell Technology (MRVL). The article focuses on Micron's upcoming fiscal third-quarter earnings report, discussing strong demand for its HBM products driven by AI, increased pricing, and its collaboration with NVIDIA. Despite potential margin compression, the outlook for Micron's stock remains positive for long-term investors due to high demand and strategic positioning.

title: Stock Market Today: S&P 500 flattens as Nvidia breaks records
summary: U.S. stocks saw mixed results, with the S&P 500 flattening and the Nasdaq Composite rising, largely due to Nvidia's record-breaking rally to $154.31, reaching its highest ever valuation. Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell expressed caution over potential inflation from tariffs, dampening hopes for early interest rate cuts, while investors now await Friday's PCE inflation report for further market direction. Despite the S&P 500 being near all-time highs, analysts urge caution due to ongoing risks from inflation, earnings, and geopolitics.

title: Micron shares rise on bets of strong demand for AI-related memory chips
summary: Micron Technology shares rose 2% in premarket trading due to strong demand for its memory chips used in AI data centers, particularly its high-bandwidth memory (HBM) chips. The company reported a significant jump in HBM sales and plans to continue investing in their production, driven by AI chip leaders like Nvidia and AMD. Analysts expect Micron's HBM sales to accelerate, leading to multiple brokerage price target increases for the stock.

title: SkyWater has big plans following $93M purchase of Infineon's Austin chipmaking facility
summary: SkyWater Technology has acquired Infineon's former semiconductor fabrication plant in Austin for $93 million. The Minnesota-based chipmaker plans to expand the facility's capacity to meet the rising demand for U.S.-made semiconductors. This acquisition will allow SkyWater to ramp up factory production and potentially manufacture millions of chips for various applications.

title: Upexi Stock Soars Over 600% On $100M Solana Treasury Bet
summary: Upexi (UPXI) shares surged over 600% after the company announced a $100 million private placement, primarily to acquire Solana (SOL) and shift towards a cryptocurrency-based treasury strategy. The initiative, if completed, would make Upexi one of the first public companies to adopt Solana as a core treasury holding. This move follows Upexi's recent pivot towards digital assets, including plans to expand its cryptocurrency mining operations.

title: Report: UMC eyes shift to cutting-edge chips with Intel deal
summary: United Microelectronics Corp. (UMC) is reportedly considering a return to advanced process technology nodes by expanding its partnership with Intel, potentially including 6 nm semiconductors. This strategic shift would move UMC away from its recent focus on mature nodes and allow it to share significant capital expenditure costs with a partner. The collaboration with Intel could also extend to advanced packaging alongside their current 12 nm FinFET process technology agreement.

title: IDVO ETF: 5.53% Yield, Global Covered Call Income with 10.9% Growth
summary: The IDVO ETF offers a 5.53% yield through a high-yield covered call strategy with a global edge, combining international stock selection with discretionary covered call overlays for monthly income. It features active management, strong dividend growth, and diversification across developed and emerging economies. The article identifies IDVO as a "Strong Buy" for income-focused investors, especially for tax-advantaged accounts, due to its impressive performance, distribution growth, and tactical adaptability.

title: Goldman Sachs resumes Geron stock coverage with Sell rating on weak Rytelo sales
summary: Goldman Sachs has resumed coverage of Geron Corporation (NASDAQ: GERN) with a Sell rating and a $1.00 price target due to disappointing sales of its drug Rytelo. The firm's analysis indicates weak gross profit margins and physician surveys suggest limited near-term sales growth for Rytelo. Goldman Sachs' estimates for Geron are significantly lower than consensus, predicting sustained pressure on stock performance.

title: Deutsche Bank Initiates Coverage of Micron Technology (MU) stock with Buy Rating
summary: Deutsche Bank has initiated coverage of Micron Technology (MU) with a "Buy" rating and a $150 price objective, citing optimism regarding the cyclical and secular backdrop driven by increased demand for High Bandwidth Memory (HBM). The bank believes HBM is an underrated driver of AI processor performance, expecting its growth to boost DRAM revenue and profitability beyond historical levels. Micron delivered record revenue in Q3 2025, with significantly increased HBM and data center revenues, and projects strong Q4 2025 results.

title: Mizuho Securities Maintains 'Outperform' Rating on Micron Technology, Inc. (MU); Raises PT
summary: Mizuho Securities has reiterated its 'Outperform' rating on Micron Technology, Inc. (MU) and increased its price target from $130 to $150 following strong Q3 fiscal 2025 earnings. This move reflects Micron's growing influence in the AI market, particularly with its high-bandwidth memory (HBM) chips, and projected segment revenue of $17 billion in 2026. The company's stock is up 46.76% year-to-date, driven by high demand for its products and anticipated strong performance in the latter half of 2025.

title: Direxion's Micron-Focused MUU, MUD ETFs Garner Attention Amid The AI Boom
summary: Direxion's Micron-focused ETFs, MUU and MUD, are attracting attention as Micron Technology Inc. (NASDAQ: MU) plays a critical role in the AI boom through its specialized memory chips. While MU has shown strong financial results and benefits from AI spending and political dynamics, it faces risks like commodity pricing and geopolitical tensions with China. These ETFs offer leveraged and inverse exposure to MU stock for traders looking to capitalize on its volatility.

title: NUWE Stock Soars: Will It Last?
summary: Nuwellis Inc. (NASDAQ: NUWE) shares soared by 143.8% following positive FDA designations and promising results, boosting investor confidence. Despite the stock surge and a new patent for an 'Extracorporeal Blood Filtering Machine and Methods' which could revolutionize fluid management, the company faces significant financial challenges, including negative profitability margins, slightly below-expected revenue of $8.74M, high debt-to-equity ratios, and an operating cash flow deficit of $2.54M. While innovation sparks optimism, long-term sustainability hinges on addressing these core financial issues and effective execution.

title: KLA Corporation, Marvell Technology, Micron, Nova, and FormFactor Shares Are Falling, What You Need To Know
summary: Shares of KLA Corporation, Marvell Technology, Micron, Nova, and FormFactor fell after semiconductor equipment giant ASML issued a cautious outlook, triggering a broad sell-off in the sector. ASML cited "increasing uncertainty driven by macro-economic and geopolitical developments," including potential new U.S. tariffs, casting a chill over future capital spending in the semiconductor industry. Marvell Technology, in particular, has seen significant volatility and is down nearly 40% year-to-date, with recent drops attributed to escalating concerns around new US tariffs.

title: Next Generation Non Volatile Memory Market | Global Market Analysis Report - 2035
summary: The next generation non-volatile memory market is predicted to expand from USD 1,097.9 million in 2025 to USD 2,348.5 million by 2035, exhibiting a CAGR of 7.9%. Non-Volatile Memory and consumer electronics are expected to dominate segments due to increasing demand for faster, more reliable data storage solutions in various devices. Growth is driven by advancements in semiconductor technology and the proliferation of smart devices requiring efficient memory.

title: DRAM Demand Powers Micron Technology's Growth: Will the Momentum Last?
summary: Micron Technology's DRAM segment experienced significant growth in the third quarter of fiscal 2025, with revenues rising 51% year-over-year to $7.1 billion, largely driven by demand from data centers, automotive, PC, and mobile markets, especially for AI-related applications. The company's High Bandwidth Memory (HBM) chips saw a 50% sequential surge in revenues, contributing to record DRAM figures. Despite competition from Western Digital and Seagate Technology, Micron maintains a solid financial outlook, with its stock outperforming its industry and favorable valuation, leading to strong earnings growth estimates for fiscal years 2025 and 2026.

title: MEMs Market Size, Industry Share and Forecast [2026-2034]
summary: The global MEMS market is projected for significant growth between 2026 and 2034, driven by increasing demand for micro sensors and actuators across industries like consumer electronics, automotive, and healthcare. Key drivers include the boom in IoT applications, substantial innovation evidenced by patent filings, and robust production facilities. However, high developmental and manufacturing costs, technical challenges, lack of universal standards, and a shortage of skilled talent are identified as restraints.

title: The future of RF technology for wireless communications and expendables
summary: This article explores the evolution of RF technology, its diverse applications in wireless communications and electronic warfare, and the challenges posed by extreme Size, Weight, Power, and Cost (SWaP-C) mandates. It details design strategies, key technologies like MMICs and MCMs, and crucial components such as tuners and filters, concluding with future trends like silicon photonics and quantum-assisted signal processing. Darren Hintze of Mercury Systems emphasizes the need for adaptable, efficient, and cost-effective RF front-end solutions.

title: Should iShares MSCI USA Value Factor ETF (VLUE) Be on Your Investing Radar?
summary: This article analyzes the iShares MSCI USA Value Factor ETF (VLUE), highlighting its structure as a passively managed large-cap value fund with assets over $6.79 billion. It details the ETF's low expense ratio, sector allocations, top holdings (Cisco Systems, Intel, Micron Technology), and performance metrics, including a 10.10% year-to-date return. The article concludes by recommending VLUE as a "Strong Buy" due to its low cost, diversification, and strong Zacks ETF Rank, while also suggesting alternative ETFs like SCHD and VTV.

title: Texas Instruments Is Better Positioned Than Micron In This Chip Cycle (NASDAQ:TXN)
summary: The article argues that Texas Instruments (TXN) is better positioned than Micron Technology (MU) in the current chip cycle. While Micron, a highly cyclical stock with a focus on memory semiconductors, faces potential downsides, Texas Instruments benefits from its diversified analog and mixed-signal portfolio, which offers better margins and reduced cyclicality.

title: 100+ CNY leaders: Micron chose the best place in the world to build semiconductors (Guest Opinion)
summary: Over 100 Central New York leaders assert that Micron Technology's decision to invest $100 billion in a semiconductor complex in Clay validates the region's unique infrastructure, skilled workforce potential, and commitment to environmental sustainability. The project is expected to bring substantial economic growth, job creation, and infrastructure improvements, setting new standards for megaprojects in New York. The leaders believe the Draft Environmental Impact Study confirms Central New York as an optimal location for leading-edge semiconductor manufacturing.

title: Spintronics Market Size to Hit USD 49.33 Billion by 2035
summary: The spintronics market is projected to reach USD 49.33 billion by 2035, growing at a CAGR of 36.48% from 2026, driven by demand for energy-efficient, high-speed memory and data processing. Asia Pacific currently dominates the market, with North America expected to be the fastest-growing region. Key drivers include energy efficiency, demand for data storage, and automotive applications, while challenges include magnetization control at nanoscale and limited industry expertise.

title: Here's How Nvidia and AMD Could Help This Super Semiconductor ETF Turn $500 Per Month Into $1 Million
summary: This article highlights how the iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX), with major holdings in Nvidia and AMD, is well-positioned to capitalize on the growing demand for AI-related computing power. Nvidia's CEO Jensen Huang predicts significant spending on data center infrastructure by 2028, benefitting chip suppliers. The ETF has shown strong historical performance and could potentially turn a $500 monthly investment into $1 million over the long term, even with more conservative returns.

title: MICRON INVESTIGATION ALERT: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. is Investigating Micron Technology, Inc. on Behalf of Long-Term Stockholders and Encourages Investors to Contact the Firm
summary: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. is investigating potential claims against Micron Technology, Inc. (NASDAQ:MU) on behalf of long-term stockholders. This follows a class action complaint filed on January 9, 2025, alleging that Micron made materially false and misleading statements about the demand for its products, particularly NAND products, between September 28, 2023, and December 18, 2024. Investors who purchased Micron stock during this period and suffered losses are encouraged to contact the firm.

title: Skyworks Solutions, Micron, Vishay Intertechnology, Photronics, and Kulicke and Soffa Shares Plummet, What You Need To Know
summary: Shares of Skyworks Solutions, Micron, Vishay Intertechnology, Photronics, and Kulicke and Soffa plummeted after divergent earnings reports highlighted a split in the semiconductor industry. Investors are favoring companies exposed to the AI boom, while those tied to slowing consumer electronics and manufacturing equipment markets are suffering. This trend was reinforced by a cautious outlook from ASML, a key industry bellwether, which further triggered a broad sell-off across the sector.

title: SPHB ETF Holdings List — BOATS:SPHB

Class A, Dell Technologies, Inc. Class C, Micron Technology, Inc., and Palantir Technologies Inc. The list details the weight percentage, number of shares, and market value for each holding.

title: Argus Raises PT on NXP Semiconductors N.V. (NXPI) Stock, Maintains Buy
summary: Argus analyst Jim Kelleher increased the price target on NXP Semiconductors N.V. (NXPI) to $270 from $235, maintaining a "Buy" rating after the Q2 2025 report. Despite strong guidance, the stock traded lower due to tariff and macroeconomic concerns. The company's future growth is linked to cyclical improvements in its core markets, company-specific growth drivers, and strategic acquisitions like TTTech Auto.

title: China fears Nvidia chips could track, trace and shut down its AIs
summary: China has raised concerns that Nvidia's H20 AI chips could contain backdoors allowing the US to track, locate, and remotely shut down its artificial intelligence systems. The Cyberspace Administration of China (CAC) summoned Nvidia for an explanation and proof, citing allegations that US lawmakers have called for "tracking and positioning" functions in advanced chips exported abroad. While Nvidia denies these claims, the incident reflects China's growing apprehensions about potential vulnerabilities in foreign-made technology, reminiscent of previous US suspicions regarding Huawei.

title: Teradyne Launches Advanced Tester for HBM2E, HBM3, and HBM4 Devices
summary: Teradyne, Inc. has announced the Magnum 7H, an advanced memory tester designed for high-bandwidth memory (HBM) devices used in generative AI servers. This tester supports various HBM versions including HBM2E, HBM3, and HBM4, offering high-parallelism, speed, and accuracy for testing HBM stacked die at scale. It provides complete test coverage from wafer test to burn-in, ensuring device quality and reliability for both current and future HBM technologies.

title: Applied Materials Partners with Apple and TI to Enhance U.S. Semiconductor Manufacturing with $200 Million Arizona Investment
summary: Applied Materials is investing over $200 million to establish a new advanced manufacturing facility in Chandler, Arizona, to boost U.S. semiconductor production. This initiative involves partnerships with Apple and Texas Instruments, aiming to supply American-made chipmaking equipment for Apple products and create up to 200 jobs in the next five years. This investment underscores Applied Materials' commitment to strengthening the U.S. semiconductor supply chain and leadership, particularly in the context of emerging AI technologies.

title: MKS Instruments Q2 2025 slides: revenue tops guidance as semiconductor demand surges
summary: MKS Instruments (NASDAQ: MKSI) reported strong Q2 2025 financial results, exceeding revenue guidance with $973 million, driven by surging demand in semiconductor and electronics markets, particularly from AI applications. The company achieved a non-GAAP gross margin of 46.6% and adjusted EBITDA of $240 million, reporting non-GAAP net earnings per diluted share of $1.77. MKS also continued its focus on debt reduction, making a $100 million principal prepayment and lowering its net leverage ratio to 4.0x.

title: Corsair Reports Strong Q2 2025 with Double-Digit Year-Over-Year Growth in Revenue and Gross Profit
summary: Corsair Gaming, Inc. announced strong financial results for Q2 2025, exceeding expectations with a 23% increase in revenue to $320 million and a 36% rise in gross profit. This growth was driven by new product launches, NVIDIA and AMD's next-gen GPUs, and strong demand in both gaming components and gamer peripherals segments. The company reaffirmed its full-year 2025 revenue outlook of $1.4 billion to $1.6 billion.

title: Micron Technology Lifts Q4 Outlook To Reflect Improved Pricing; Stock Up Over 5% In Pre-Market
summary: Micron Technology (MU) has raised its fourth-quarter outlook due to improved pricing, especially in DRAM, and strong execution. The company now projects higher net profit per share and revenue for the quarter than previously anticipated. Following this updated guidance, Micron's stock was up over 5% in pre-market trading.

title: Micron Stock (MU) Rises After Chipmaker Raises Earnings, Revenue Guidance
summary: Micron Technology's stock rose after the chipmaker increased its earnings and revenue guidance, indicating strong business performance. This positive outlook led to significant gains for Micron, outperforming the iShares semiconductor ETF. The article highlights the company's robust financial health.

title: Applied Materials seeks trade zone designation for Central Texas operations
summary: Applied Materials is seeking a foreign trade zone designation for its Central Texas operations to avoid tariffs and mitigate the impact of higher import taxes. Company officials deem this designation "a crucial element" for its U.S. operations, especially amidst President Donald Trump's plans to expand tariffs. This move could inspire other companies to take similar actions to protect their operations.

title: Applied Materials Q3 2025 Earnings Preview: AI & Memory Tailwinds Meet Geopolitical Headwinds
summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) is set to report its Q3 2025 earnings, with Wall Street expecting EPS of $2.34 on $7.21 billion in revenue, driven by strong AI and high-bandwidth memory demand. The company's valuation appears attractive with a forward P/E of 21, but near-term stock movement will depend on guidance and investor reaction, especially given geopolitical headwinds from U.S. export restrictions affecting its China revenue. The earnings report will serve as a bellwether for the broader semiconductor sector and the strength of the AI boom.

title: Applied Materials Earnings: Disappointing Guidance Doesn’t Dent Our Long-Term Conviction

The firm maintains long-term conviction due to expected AI investment driving chip equipment growth.

title: India Strategy In Spotlight As MacDermid Alpha Names New VP
summary: MacDermid Alpha Electronics Solutions has appointed Dr. Ravi Bhatkal as Vice President, Strategy, India, a newly created role. This appointment aims to bolster the company's growth and transformation initiatives in the Indian market, aligning with India's focus on strengthening its electronics manufacturing ecosystem through schemes like the PLI and India Semiconductor Mission. Dr. Bhatkal brings over two decades of leadership experience and will focus on accelerating business growth.

title: Entegris Announces Plans for $700 Million Investment in the United States, Technology Center in Illinois
summary: Entegris, Inc. is investing $700 million in domestic R&D over several years to accelerate semiconductor innovation, in collaboration with the Trump Administration. A significant portion of this investment will establish a new U.S. Technology Center in Aurora, Illinois. This investment complements a previous $700 million commitment for a manufacturing center in Colorado, bringing Entegris' total planned U.S. investment to $1.4 billion to strengthen the domestic semiconductor ecosystem.

title: SkyWater pitches itself as all-American firm as Trump mulls more equity deals following Intel deal
summary: President Trump is pursuing a new economic strategy where the U.S. government takes direct equity stakes in major American companies, exemplified by the recent 10% ownership in Intel. This approach aims to strengthen industries and national security, contrasting with traditional government support via loans or tax breaks. Semiconductor manufacturers like SkyWater Technology, GlobalFoundries, and Micron Technology could be future candidates for similar equity-linked deals, though the strategy has drawn criticism regarding potential favoritism and its "socialistic" implications.

title: Micron Technology (MU) Up By 36% After Jim Cramer Said It Was “Insanely Cheap”
summary: The article discusses how Micron Technology (MU) saw a significant 36% increase in its stock price after Jim Cramer commented on its valuation, calling it "insanely cheap." This surge highlights the potential impact of high-profile financial commentators on market movements and investor sentiment towards specific stocks.

title: Nvidia Isn't the Only Way to Profit From the AI Boom

The insatiable demand for advanced semiconductors suggests the entire sector is a promising investment avenue, with many companies demonstrating strong performance and more attractive valuations than Nvidia.

title: UBS Reiterates Buy Rating on Micron Technology, Keeps PT at $155

Analyst Timothy Arcuri remains optimistic, citing improved pricing, particularly in DRAM, and strong execution. The updated guidance projects higher revenue, gross margins, and EPS than previously expected, leading to an implied upside of almost 32% from current levels based on analyst estimates.

title: 7 Best AI Infrastructure Stocks to Buy Now
summary: This article identifies seven top AI infrastructure stocks, including Equinix, American Tower, and Micron Technology, that are essential for the ongoing AI revolution. It highlights how these companies, spanning data centers, semiconductors, and network operators, provide the foundational hardware and services needed to power AI advancements. The piece emphasizes that investing in these "picks and shovels" companies offers a stable way to capitalize on the AI megatrend, given their recurring revenues, high switching costs, and strong long-term growth potential.

title: SEMICON 2025: Building the Next Semiconductor Powerhouse India’s Biggest Semiconductor & Electronics Show
summary: India is rapidly advancing its semiconductor industry, moving from vision to reality in just four years since the 2021 launch of the India Semiconductor Mission (ISM). With a ₹76,000 crore Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme and significant investments, the country aims to become a global hub for semiconductor innovation and manufacturing. The SEMICON India 2025 event, inaugurated by Prime Minister Narendra Modi, will showcase India's progress, attracting global leaders and fostering collaborations to strengthen its position in the global semiconductor ecosystem.

title: Amkor to quadruple footprint at new Peoria chip packaging factory
summary: Amkor is set to significantly expand its chip packaging factory in Peoria, Arizona, with plans to immediately begin groundwork and quadruple its footprint. This expansion follows a strategic land swap in Peoria and builds on previous funding ahead of construction. The article notes that previous plans for a $2 billion factory at a different Peoria site were superseded, with HonorHealth now constructing a medical campus there instead.

title: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) Q3 Earnings and Revenues Surpass Estimates
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) reported Q3 earnings of $0.44 per share, surpassing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $0.43, and revenues of $9.14 billion, exceeding estimates by 4.07%. The company has consistently beaten revenue estimates over the last four quarters. Despite underperforming the S&P 500 year-to-date, HPE holds a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy), indicating potential for market outperformance.

title: Amkor relocates $2B semiconductor facility to larger Arizona site

This relocation follows earlier pushback from residents and intends to meet market demands and increased project scope, with production expected by early 2028. The facility, which received $407 million in CHIPS and Science Act funding, will largely support Apple, packaging and testing chips produced by TSMC.

Despite the high returns, the article also notes the challenge of sustained rapid growth for large companies like Nvidia, while maintaining optimism for the overall semiconductor industry's prospects.

Meanwhile, HMM Co. shares rallied amid reports of a potential acquisition by POSCO Group, though POSCO Holdings' shares saw a slight decline due to analyst concerns over financial risks and limited synergies.

Investments are also increasing in AI chip testing and panel-level packaging.

The combined entity, AMC Robotics Corporation, will trade on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the ticker "AMCI" and plans to use transaction proceeds to accelerate development of its AI-powered patrol and incident response robot. The stock has shown significant momentum, surging 53.84% in the past week and seeing a 61.67% year-to-date return.

title: Nvidia, Broadcom, TSMC, other AI names rally on Oracle's massive growth projections
summary: Oracle's projection of its cloud infrastructure business reaching $114 billion in sales by fiscal year 2029 has led to a rally in AI-related stocks. Companies like Nvidia, Broadcom, AMD, Micron, and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. saw significant gains as investors expect high demand for chips and systems required for Oracle's data center expansion. UBS analysts noted that Oracle's capital expenditure plans for GPU cloud demand are bullish for AI hardware suppliers and their partners.

title: Wolfspeed Announces the Commercial Launch of 200mm Silicon Carbide Materials Portfolio, Unlocking the Industry's Ability to Manufacture at Scale
summary: Wolfspeed, Inc. announced the commercial launch of its 200mm Silicon Carbide (SiC) materials products, a move intended to accelerate the industry's transition from silicon to SiC. This commercial release, following a positive response from select customers, also includes 200mm SiC epitaxy for immediate qualification. The company states that these advancements will enable device makers to improve MOSFET yields, accelerate time-to-market, and deliver more competitive solutions across various high-growth applications like automotive and renewable energy.

summary: Jim Cramer discussed Western Digital Corporation (WDC), highlighting its 106% year-to-date gain in 2025, driven by catalysts like a Morgan Stanley upgrade and a price target hike to $99. Cramer noted the company's valuation, mentioning that storage companies historically trade at a discount to the S&P, and he has seen WDC trade at "8 times earnings" compared to its current 15 times earnings. He also reiterated his prior stance comparing Micron and Western Digital, suggesting Micron's stock is more likely to be bought back by the company when it declines.

title: Micron Technology and Wendy's have been highlighted as Zacks Bull and Bear of the Day

summary: ZyVersa Therapeutics (OTCQB:ZVSA) announced that Nasdaq will formally delist its common stock due to non-compliance with minimum bid price requirements. While delisted from Nasdaq, the company's shares will continue to trade on the OTCQB Venture Market under the symbol ZVSA. summary: This article reviews the Q2 earnings for several semiconductor stocks, including Micron, IPG Photonics, Himax, and NXP Semiconductors. summary: Mizuho has increased its price targets for SanDisk, Western Digital, and Seagate Technology, citing a positive outlook due to rising storage prices. Analyst Vijay Rakesh's decision follows recent supply chain checks, indicating potential upside for these companies in the storage sector.

summary: A new "deep tech" startup hub in Phoenix, Tesoro Venture Capital, will focus on fostering early-stage companies in AI and semiconductor manufacturing. The initiative is a collaboration between Tesoro Venture Capital, TSMC, Amkor Technology, Cadence Design Systems, the Greater Phoenix Economic Council, and city officials.

title: Seagate Stock Surges 153.4% in 6 Months: Will the Uptrend Continue?
summary: Seagate Technology Holdings plc (STX) has seen its stock surge 153.4% in the past six months, outperforming its industry and the S&P 500, driven by strong demand for mass capacity storage solutions and expansion into SSDs. The company is poised for continued growth due to HAMR technology and increasing cloud/AI infrastructure investments but faces challenges from high debt, stiff competition, and macroeconomic uncertainties. Investors are advised to consider its Zacks Rank #3 (Hold) rating, with existing shareholders seeing a compelling story while new entrants might await a better entry point.

summary: Lam Research's stock is experiencing a historic rally, driven by enthusiasm for the booming memory-chip market. The company's stock has achieved its longest winning streak on record and is among the top gainers in the S&P 500 this year. However, a crucial test awaits with Micron's upcoming spending forecast, which could impact the rally.

title: SanDisk stock target more than doubled at BofA on AI demand surge
summary: Bank of America has significantly raised its price target for SanDisk Corporation (NASDAQ: SNDK) to $125 from $59, citing a surge in AI-driven demand and tightening conditions in the NAND market. Analysts project improved margins for SanDisk due to undersupply, increased enterprise solid-state drive (eSSD) adoption, and limited supplier capacity expansion. The firm also raised its earnings per share forecasts for 2026 and 2027, maintaining a "Buy" rating on the stock.

This shift is reshaping AI and semiconductor ETF strategies, with leveraged and diversified options gaining traction.

summary: Micron Technology Inc. reported robust fiscal fourth-quarter results, surpassing Wall Street expectations with a net income of $3.2 billion, or $2.83 per share, adjusted to $3.03 per share. The chipmaker also exceeded revenue forecasts, posting $11.32 billion for the quarter. Looking ahead, Micron projects strong fiscal first-quarter earnings and revenue, indicating continued growth.

title: These chip stocks are rising off Trump’s latest proposal to boost U.S. manufacturing
summary: Shares of U.S. chip manufacturers rose on Friday following reports that the Trump administration is proposing a new plan to boost domestic chip production. The plan would require U.S. companies to manufacture the same amount of chips domestically as they import from abroad, with tariffs imposed for non-compliance. This initiative aims to further bolster the U.S. chip manufacturing industry.

title: CAMTEK Metrology Innovations Target Advanced Packaging in Chip Manufacturing
summary: Camtek is a leading manufacturer of metrology and inspection equipment for the semiconductor industry, focusing on advanced packaging, memory, CMOS image sensors, MEMS, RF, and other segments. In an exclusive interview, Rafi Amit, CEO, and Omri Katz, Senior Vice President for Manufacturing and Sales Support at Camtek, discuss their company's role in semiconductor manufacturing, the unique aspects of their technologies, and their strategies for addressing growing demands from chip manufacturers. They emphasize the increasing complexity in advanced packaging driven by AI and HPC applications, which necessitates reliable metrology solutions to ensure device performance.

title: Kioxia and Sandisk Announce Beginning of Operation of Fab2 at Kitakami Plant, Japan to Meet the Market Demand Driven by AI
summary: Kioxia Corporation and Sandisk Corporation have announced the start of operations at Fab2 (K2), a new semiconductor fabrication facility at the Kitakami Plant in Iwate Prefecture, Japan. This state-of-the-art facility will produce eighth-generation, 218-layer 3D flash memory using revolutionary CBA technology to meet the rising demand for storage driven by artificial intelligence. Production is expected to ramp up in stages, with meaningful output anticipated in the first half of 2026.

summary: Micron Technology Inc. is seeking an incentive package from the Longmont City Council for a proposed $212 million expansion. summary: Morgan Stanley has upgraded Micron (MU) to Overweight and significantly increased its price target to $220. title: David Tepper's Appaloosa Drops Nvidia from Top 5 Holdings, Boosts Micron Stake
summary: David Tepper's Appaloosa Management significantly reduced its stake in Nvidia, dropping it from its top five holdings, while tripling its position in Micron Technology. summary: New York is proactively addressing the manufacturing skills gap, particularly in response to Micron Technology Inc.'s $100 billion investment in a semiconductor-manufacturing campus.
